title: vuln-scan: touchesGoMod compares basename to full path, never fires
description: VulnScanCheck.Run gates on touchesGoMod(in.FilePaths), which does slices.Contains(paths, "go.mod"). But FilePaths is populated from PromotionBatch.Files[].Path, which carries the full filesystem path that the cold scan / fsnotify watcher passes through Ingester.Save (see coldscan.go walkAndSave -> save(path,...) at internal/application/coldscan.go:272). Result: touchesGoMod returns false on every promotion that includes go.mod, so the vuln-scan check is a silent no-op even when [vuln_source] provider="osv" is configured. Repro: enable osv, register a repo with known-vulnerable deps (e.g. dgrijalva/jwt-go v3.2.0+incompatible / gin v1.6.0 / yaml.v2 v2.2.2), reindex — findings list is empty even after the OSV cache hydrates. Fix: either store repo-relative paths in staging/PromotionBatch (matches the doc comment 'FilePaths entries are repo-root-relative') or change touchesGoMod to match by filepath.Base==go.mod / strings.HasSuffix("/go.mod"). Same suspicion applies to any future check that gates on a manifest filename.
close_reason: Fixed: touchesGoMod now matches by filepath.Base so it catches both repo-relative and absolute paths. Added regression test TestVulnScanCheck_GoModTouched_AbsolutePath covering the cold-scan/fsnotify path.

title: eng_get_call_chain returns empty nodes/edges for a function with obvious callers
description:
eng_get_call_chain on node_id for Execute() in /home/jrose/src/reglet/cmd/reglet/root.go returns {nodes:[], edges:[], included_staging:false}. The same node returns proper callers via eng_get_blast_radius (which includes 'main' package as an entry). So CALLS edges exist in some form but call_chain's traversal returns nothing.

Reproduce:
1. veska repo add /path/to/cobra-cli-repo
2. wait for cold scan complete
3. find Execute via eng_find_symbol
4. call eng_get_call_chain with that node_id

Investigate application/blastradius vs the call-chain service in application; likely a missing edge-type filter or wrong direction enum.
acceptance_criteria: 1) eng_get_call_chain returns the same callers eng_get_blast_radius surfaces. 2) Unit test in application covers an Execute()-style cobra root.
notes: Investigation 2026-05-23: with new daemon binary, the bug is more nuanced. (1) Auto-link queue had 394 pending so CALLS edges for reglet may not be built. (2) The agent snippet docs eng_get_call_chain as 'incoming/outgoing CALLS edges' but tool_get_call_chain.go BFSes only OutgoingEdges. So even with edges fully built, asking 'what calls Execute' (incoming) returns nothing. Two possible fixes: (a) add an incoming-direction traversal (or accept a 'direction' param), (b) update snippet to say 'outgoing only; use eng_get_blast_radius for inbound'.
close_reason: Closed

title: veska repo add returns SQLITE_BUSY error but actually succeeds
description:
When registering a second repo concurrently (or while another scan is active), 'veska repo add' returns 'insert repo: database is locked (5) (SQLITE_BUSY)' as a failure exit, but 'veska repo list' shows the repo was actually inserted. The cold scan even kicks off. A user retrying the command will hit duplicate-row errors or assume the repo isn't registered.

Reproduce: while a cold scan is in flight on repo A, run 'veska repo add /path/B'. CLI prints SQLITE_BUSY, exit 1; repo list shows B as unindexed/added.

Fix direction: either retry under the busy lock with a short backoff (sqlite busy_timeout / explicit retry loop in internal/repo's insert path), or roll the partial insert back so the error message is truthful. Surfaced through cmd/veska/repo.go -> daemon eng_add_repo path.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Reproduce case (concurrent add during scan) no longer returns SQLITE_BUSY to the user. 2) Either succeeds cleanly or fails with no row inserted. 3) Test in internal/repo or cmd/veska/repo_test.go covers the contention path.
close_reason: Closed

title: doctor status 'healthy' rollup misses post_promotion_queue failures
description:
'veska doctor status' reports 'status: healthy' while 'veska doctor post_promotion_queue' shows degraded with failed/auto_link: 3 and 394 pending. The top-level rollup omits queue health from the aggregated subsystems list (currently embedder, egress, config, ingestion).

A new user trusts doctor status as the single signal; today it gives false reassurance while a real subsystem is broken.

Fix: add queue_state to the rollup in cmd/veska/doctor.go's status command; treat 'failed > 0' or 'pending > some-high-water-mark' as degraded; expose the same field from eng_get_status.
acceptance_criteria: 1) doctor status reflects post_promotion_queue health. 2) eng_get_status includes a queue_failed/queue_pending counter (or a degraded_reasons entry). 3) Test covers the failed>0 case.
close_reason: Closed

title: Agent init snippet teaches contract daemon does not honor (auto-resolve repo_id/branch/cwd)
description:
'veska init --agent claude' writes CLAUDE.md saying: 'repo_id and branch are usually optional. When exactly one repo is registered the daemon auto-resolves repo_id; when branch is omitted it defaults to the repo active branch.' In reality every MCP call returns 'missing required params: repo_id, branch' or 'branch is required'.

Additionally eng_get_current_repo requires explicit cwd param even when veska-mcp is invoked from inside the registered repo — the stdio shim does not forward cwd.

AI agents read the snippet, paste calls verbatim, and bounce on their first attempt. This is the contract failure most likely to make the product look broken to LLMs.

Fix direction: either (a) implement the auto-resolution in the daemon (single-repo => infer repo_id; missing branch => active_branch; eng_get_current_repo => fall back to os.Getwd of the *daemon*? no — have veska-mcp inject cwd from its own process env into params), or (b) rewrite cmd/veska/init_agent.go snippet to match real required-param behavior.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Either the snippet matches behavior, or behavior matches the snippet. 2) An MCP call with no repo_id/branch from a registered cwd works end-to-end through veska-mcp. 3) tests/mcp covers the auto-resolve case.
close_reason: Verified against freshly-built daemon: snippet IS accurate. resolveRepoIDOrSingleton auto-resolves repo_id when exactly one repo registered; resolveBranchOrActive auto-resolves branch from active_branch. My original test had 5 repos (so singleton path didn't trigger, correctly) AND was hitting a stale daemon binary that pre-dated resolveBranchOrActive. After rebuild + daemon restart, MCP calls without branch work end-to-end. Cwd-from-shim is a separate, smaller enhancement — filed as follow-up.

title: MCP tools/list returns empty inputSchema (no properties, no required) for every tool
description: Sample query of tools/list shows all 31 tools have inputSchema = {required:[], properties:[]}. MCP clients (Claude Code, Cursor, etc) rely on inputSchema to know argument names, types, and what is required. Currently they must call blind and parse the human error message ('symbol is required', 'node_id is required'). Fix: populate inputSchema with JSON-schema descriptions of every parameter the tool accepts. Without this, the MCP surface is effectively undiscoverable to anyone but the local CLI, which knows the args by convention.

title: vuln scan does not run on first config-reload after enabling vuln_source; OSV cache cold
description: Repro: (1) fresh ~/.veska, init, repo add solov2 + tack; (2) uncomment [vuln_source] in config.toml; (3) veska config reload. Outcome: tack re-promoted, vulnrefresh+vuln-scan ran, but 'findings: 0' because OSV cache was being fetched concurrently. solov2 promoted before vulnrefresh started and was NEVER vuln-scanned. Second config reload (17 min later) hit SQLITE_BUSY on solov2 from a sink-before-delete, succeeded on tack (31 findings appeared). Asks: (a) gate the first vuln-scan on vulnrefresh completion so the first promotion after enable doesn't silently score 0; (b) ensure every registered repo gets a vuln-scan when [vuln_source] is enabled, not just whichever ones get re-promoted in the right window; (c) consider a 'veska vuln scan-all' command so users have a deterministic way to retry without restarting the daemon.

title: junior-journey round 3: fresh install + vuln scanner end-to-end (solov2 + tack)
description: Wipe ~/.veska, rebuild from source, follow README as a junior would. Index this repo and ../../all-reglet/tack. Exercise CLI surface (init, repo add, service, doctor, backup, wiki, context, findings, reindex, config reload). Exercise every MCP tool. Specifically validate the OSV vuln scanner end-to-end (config enable -> reindex -> findings). File a child bead for every friction/gap.

title: veska reindex runs cold scan in-process, racing the running daemon → SQLITE_BUSY
description:
Repro:
  veska service start
  veska repo add /path/to/tack    # works; daemon does the scan
  veska reindex /path/to/tack     # FAILS: 'promoter: sink before-delete for "...": database is locked (5) (SQLITE_BUSY)'

Daemon log shows the reindex never appears (no 'cold scan: starting' lines from daemon). The veska CLI is invoking the cold-scan in-process and competing with the running daemon for the sqlite write lock — specifically, the daemon's embedder worker is flushing vectors when reindex tries to do its before-delete in the sink. Repeatedly reproducible, even after waiting for the daemon to idle.

Reindex should dispatch through the daemon (same as  does — note the help text on  literally says 'via daemon' but  does not). Either route it over the MCP socket as a 'reindex' RPC, or refuse to run if the daemon is up.

User impact: high — the only way to retry a failed scan or pick up a config change (e.g. enabling vuln_source then wanting to scan retroactively, exactly what the README tells you to do) is broken on a default install.
close_reason: reindex now probes the MCP socket via the existing daemonRunning() helper from restore.go; if up, refuses with an actionable two-line hint (stop-reindex-start). Verified: refusal triggers when daemon up, full reindex still works when daemon down. Follow-up (real fix): route reindex through an eng_reindex MCP RPC so the daemon owns the write — filed as solov2-mdn3-followup if needed.

title: Secret scanner FP: Go Benchmark/Test function names match 'high-entropy' rule
description: Repro: scanning tack registered repo, the secret_leak rule fires on `func BenchmarkLoaderDiscovery(b *testing.B)` at internal/plugin/loader_bench_test.go:14 with rule "high-entropy". The detector inspects the entire line including the Go identifier; long CamelCase identifiers (Benchmark*, Test*) reliably trip entropy thresholds. Impact: every Go repo with non-trivial benchmark/test names will surface high-severity secret findings on first scan — a poor first-impression for new users and noise that drowns real leaks. Fix candidates: (1) skip lines matching `^func (Test|Benchmark|Example|Fuzz)`, (2) ignore identifiers entirely when computing entropy, (3) require additional signal (e.g., string-literal context, key=value shape) before flagging. Already saw a user suppression in the DB labeled "false positive: benchmark name" — confirming this hits real users.
close_reason: already fixed in e247108 (solov2-3455) — looksLikeIdentifier skip; stale finding in DB closed retroactively

title: eng_list_findings: 'rule' param silently ignored (filters not applied)
description: Repro: `eng_list_findings` with `params.rule="vuln"` (or any other rule) returns the FULL unfiltered list (all rules mixed in). `severity` filter works correctly. Verified on tack repo (9a4bd5499f1e): 237 findings returned regardless of rule. Impact: agents/users can't isolate vuln/secret/dead-code findings from MCP — must post-filter client-side, defeating the param's purpose. Likely missed switch arm in the SQL where-clause builder for list-findings. Add MCP/CLI test that asserts rule-filter narrowing.
close_reason: fixed in e4b8dd5

title: epic: resolve chained-selector CALLS edges (s.field.method)
description:
Followup from solov2-o2y5 (junior-journey sweep). On a hexagonal/DDD codebase, calls through interface-typed struct fields are the dominant call-chain edge type, but the treesitter Go adapter currently drops them — call_chain/blast_radius miss 75%+ of the real outgoing edges from any method that uses dependency injection.

See solov2-o2y5 closure note for detailed root-cause analysis and proposed fix outline. Touches:
- internal/infrastructure/treesitter/go.go: parseTypeDecl, collectCallNames, extractCallEdges, extractTopLevelVarInitCalls
- internal/core/domain: maybe richer UnresolvedCall (carry field-qualifier chain)
- internal/infrastructure/sqlite/promotion_store.go: cross-file resolver may need to honor field-qualifier hints
- tests: new fixtures for struct fields, interface fields, embedded structs

Acceptance: eng_get_call_chain direction=out on Promoter.Promote returns ≥ 7 outgoing edges (currently 2).

title: first repo add after fresh service install times out on MCP socket
description:
After 'veska service install && veska service start' and 'veska doctor status' reporting healthy, the first 'veska repo add <path>' times out reading from the MCP socket. CLI falls back to direct DB write but no cold scan fires; user must 'veska service restart' to trigger the startup-resync code path. Reproduced on the first TWO adds; a third add minutes later went through the daemon cleanly. Looks like a socket-ready race or per-process dispatcher init delay.

Error message: 'added repo <id> (direct write; daemon dial failed: read response: read unix @->/home/jrose/.veska/mcp.sock: i/o timeout — restart daemon to cold-scan/live-watch)'
close_reason: Closed

title: eng_suppress_finding records suppression but does not filter list_findings
description:
eng_suppress_finding returns suppression_id and writes a row visible via eng_list_suppressions, but the suppressed finding still appears in eng_list_findings with state='open'. Suppression has no observable effect on the open-findings stream.

Repro:
  FID=65b6937d5b2d64eacc3a08686aa49c38   # secret_leak fp on tack
  printf '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"eng_suppress_finding","params":{"finding_id":"'$FID'","reason":"fp"}}' | bin/veska-mcp
  # Then list with state=open — finding is still there.
close_reason: Closed

title: vuln scanner skips // indirect deps
description:
OSV vuln-scan only flags entries from the direct require block; // indirect entries are silently skipped. In modern Go modules almost all transitive risk lives in indirect deps, so this neuters the check on real projects.

Repro: registered tack at /home/jrose/src/all-reglet/tack which has 'golang.org/x/crypto v0.48.0 // indirect'. OSV cache contains many <0.52.0 advisories for that exact module. eng_list_findings returns 0 vuln findings. Moving the same dep to a direct require in /tmp/veska-vuln-test produces dozens of findings.
close_reason: verified-not-a-bug after closer inspection. ReadGoMod (internal/application/manifest/manifest.go) iterates f.Require and includes ALL entries — direct and // indirect alike (no Indirect filter). Live verification: forced a fresh vuln-scan on tack by touching go.mod; daemon logged 'vuln-scan: scanned repo_id=9a4bd5499f1e deps=145 findings=31' and eng_list_findings now returns 31 vuln findings on tack's indirect deps. Original observation of 0 findings was likely a sequencing issue (vuln_source not yet wired into the daemon's running state when the first promotion ran), made invisible by the lack of a per-scan log line — fixed by solov2-fw6z's new INFO line.

title: eng_find_changed_symbols ignores N in HEAD~N / HEAD^ refs
description:
All HEAD~1, HEAD~2, HEAD~3, HEAD^, main~2 return identical results (the HEAD~1..HEAD diff). Only literal SHAs traverse multi-commit windows. Likely a ref-parser that recognizes only HEAD and HEAD~1 then falls through.

Repro:
  for r in HEAD~1 HEAD~3 HEAD^ main~2; do
    printf '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"eng_find_changed_symbols","params":{"repo_id":"0b3b70ab9f89","branch":"main","ref_a":"'$r'","ref_b":"HEAD"}}' | bin/veska-mcp | jq '.result.added | length'
  done
  # all → 9; explicit SHA 5 commits back → 44
close_reason: could-not-reproduce in current state: HEAD~1, HEAD~3, HEAD~5 against HEAD (and HEAD~6/7/8 against the historical test SHA 3023804) all return appropriately-scaling result counts: a:27/59/68 and a:9/20/44 respectively. Symbol-count differences track git diff --name-only file counts. Verified ref_a='71dc978' (SHA) vs ref_a='HEAD~?' resolving to the same commit both produce identical results. The original observation of 'all HEAD~N return identical results' may have been a coincidence: HEAD~1..HEAD touched only 3 files (2 Go), and HEAD~3..HEAD added .beads/issues.jsonl + 2 more Go files (providers) — added-symbol counts could have plateaued by chance. Reopen if observed again with reliable repro.

title: call_chain/blast_radius miss interface-mediated calls
description:
On internal/application/promoter.go::Promoter.Promote, eng_get_call_chain direction=out depth=3 returns only 2 of ~8 outgoing edges. Calls through interface-typed fields are dropped: p.store.Promote(), p.audit.Write(), p.staging.Snapshot(), p.staging.DeleteStagedFile(). Same root cause makes blast_radius and call_chain direction=in return empty/near-empty for heavily-used methods in DDD/hexagonal codebases. Investigate treesitter Go adapter's call edge resolution for selector expressions whose receiver type is a named interface.

Repro:
  PID=292275788eb3bf30fa1b8246af499b7386d7373b784a75a790ddf825c629c4f8
  printf '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"eng_get_call_chain","params":{"repo_id":"0b3b70ab9f89","branch":"main","node_id":"'$PID'","depth":3,"direction":"out"}}' | bin/veska-mcp | jq '.result.edges | length'
  # → 2, expected ~8
close_reason:
known-limitation; needs scoped epic. Root cause analysis: internal/infrastructure/treesitter/go.go collectCallNames at line 631+ handles only single-level selector_expression where operand.Type()=='identifier'. Multi-level selectors like 'p.store.Promote()' parse as nested selector_expression: outer { operand: selector_expression{p, store}, field: Promote }. The outer-operand check at line 634 (operand.Type()=="identifier") is FALSE for chained selectors and the call site is silently dropped. The collectCallNames comment at line 611 explicitly notes 'Chained selectors (s.field.X()) whose operand is not a plain identifier are still skipped.'

A correct fix needs (a) per-struct field-name→declared-type map built during struct_type parsing (currently parseTypeDecl at line 270 captures only the struct's name, not its fields), (b) wiring that map through extractCallEdges, (c) in collectCallNames, for outer selector with operand p.field where p==recvName, resolve field→type via the struct-field map, then look up 'type.method' in the symbol map. For interface-typed fields, also need a same-package implementation lookup or emit a polymorphic UnresolvedCall.

Estimated 1-2 days with tests covering: struct fields with simple types, interface-typed fields, pointer-typed fields, embedded fields, fields-of-fields chains. Skipping for this junior-journey sweep; file as scoped epic.

title: One-line install + auto-config for popular agent harnesses
description: No equivalent of 'claude mcp add veska …' one-liner. No first-run auto-pick-a-backend. No AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md snippet for users to drop into projects. Ship: (a) install one-liner, (b) MCP-registration helper per harness (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, OpenCode at minimum), (c) ready-to-paste AGENTS.md snippet. From Semble competitive analysis 2026-05-24.

title: Default to model2vec backend; demote Ollama to opt-in
description: README and defaults currently push Ollama as primary. Model2vec is a first-class CPU backend (internal/infrastructure/embedding/model2vec/) that needs no external service. Switch default-on-first-run to model2vec, update README, position Ollama as the higher-quality opt-in path. Pairs with the fat-binary packaging issue. From Semble competitive analysis 2026-05-24.

title: Packaging: single fat binary with embedded model2vec weights
description: No fat binary, no brew/curl|sh installer, no published releases. Capability is at parity with Semble (model2vec backend exists, embedded_on.go build tag bundles weights) but distribution isn't. First impression for a new user is 'build three Go binaries from source.' Goal: one-line install that drops a binary which works out of the box with no Ollama, no env vars. Surfaced in Semble competitive analysis 2026-05-24.

title: Tree-sitter: add language parsers beyond Go
description: Currently internal/infrastructure/treesitter/ ships only go.go. Competitor Semble covers 19+ languages in its benchmarks. Until Veska parses TS/JS/Python/Rust/Java/Ruby/C/C++/etc., it cannot be evaluated on the polyglot repos most agents target. Priority gap surfaced during Semble competitive analysis (2026-05-24).

title: Short-id prefix matching: docs say prefix; impl requires exact 12 chars
description:
README: 'you may pass the full id or that short prefix.' Reality: prefix 'f43242f9' (8 chars) returns 'unknown repo_id'; only the full 12-char short_id is accepted.

Either implement true prefix-matching (min length 4, ambiguity error if 2+ match), or update docs + add a hint in the NotFound message ('short_id is exactly 12 chars; see eng_list_repos').
acceptance_criteria: 1) Either prefix-matching works for >=4 chars, or NotFound mentions the 12-char requirement. 2) tests/mcp covers the chosen behavior.
close_reason: Already fixed in HEAD (internal/infrastructure/mcp/tools_helpers.go resolveRepoID step 3 supports >=4 char prefixes with explicit ambiguity error; NotFound message mentions the prefix minimum). The user's earlier failure was against a stale daemon binary. Verified after rebuilding: 8-char prefix 'f43242f9' now resolves cleanly to the full id.

title: 'veska search' with zero results prints nothing and exits 0
description:
'veska search <query>' produces no output and exits 0 when results are empty. A new user can't distinguish 'ran successfully, no hits' from 'broken / misconfigured / wrong repo'. JSON mode does emit {results:[]} but the default text mode is silent.

Fix in cmd/veska/search.go: when results are empty, print a one-liner to stderr — e.g. 'no results (NN embeds pending — try again in a moment)' when get_status shows pending_embeds > 0, or 'no results' otherwise. Keep exit 0 (no-results is not an error).
acceptance_criteria: 1) Empty text-mode result prints a clear human-readable line. 2) When pending_embeds>0 for that repo, message hints at it. 3) cmd/veska/search_test.go covers both branches.
close_reason: Closed

title: eng_search_semantic should signal 'embeddings_pending' in degraded_reasons
description:
When pending_embeds > 0 (esp. for the queried repo), eng_search_semantic returns {results: []} with degraded_reasons: []. The README promises in-band quality signals (e.g. low_quality_static_embedder) but the embeddings-still-warming case is silent.

Add a degraded_reasons entry like 'embeddings_pending' (with the pending count, or per-repo if cheap) whenever the queried repo has un-embedded nodes. application/search and the MCP encoder.
acceptance_criteria: 1) eng_search_semantic adds 'embeddings_pending' to degraded_reasons when applicable. 2) tests/mcp covers the warming case. 3) veska search CLI surfaces it.
close_reason: Closed

title: eng_get_status missing per-repo scan progress (file count / pct)
description:
scans_in_flight currently exposes only {repo_id, phase, started_at}. For a large repo (e.g. engram, 6+ min) a user can't tell if it's progressing or hung. Add files_seen / files_total (or a percentage / elapsed_ms / last_event_at) so the CLI and doctor can show progress.

Tie this to ingestion/cold-scan in application/ingester and surface through the MCP encoder.
acceptance_criteria: 1) scans_in_flight entries include files_seen + files_total (or equivalent). 2) veska repo list shows progress on (unindexed). 3) ingester unit test verifies progress updates.
close_reason: Closed

title: repo_id short prefix not honored: only exact 12-char short_id works
description: README contract: 'anywhere a repo_id is required you may pass the full id or that short prefix.' In practice, only the exact 12-char short_id from eng_list_repos is accepted; an 8-char prefix returns 'unknown repo_id: 6754f6c9 (run eng_list_repos)'. Either honor any unambiguous prefix (preferred) or document the 12-char minimum and update the error to say so. Affects every MCP tool that takes repo_id.
close_reason: Closed

title: Cobra/struct var-decl blind spot: top-level *cobra.Command vars not extracted as nodes
description: For repos where the CLI surface lives in top-level `var rootCmd = &cobra.Command{...}` declarations (and `rootCmd.AddCommand(...)` in init()), eng_find_symbol/eng_get_file_nodes/eng_get_call_chain return only func + package nodes. The entire command tree is invisible to structural tools. Repro: register a cobra repo, eng_find_symbol "rootCmd" -> nodes:[]. Tree-sitter extraction needs to capture struct-typed var decls (at minimum), and ideally treat *cobra.Command var-decls as first-class command nodes named by their Use: string. Discovered during junior-engineer journey walkthrough on 2026-05-23.
close_reason: Closed

title: repo add accepts non-source paths like /tmp; permanently degrades doctor status
description: `veska repo add /tmp` silently succeeds (no validation that the path is a git repo or contains source). After adding, the repo sits in `(unindexed)` state and `veska doctor status` returns exit code 1 with 'status: degraded — 1 unindexed repo(s)' forever — until the user notices and runs `veska repo remove <id>`. \n\nProposed: `repo add` should refuse paths that (a) are not inside a git work-tree, (b) contain no recognized source files, or at minimum warn and require --force. Additionally, the daemon should auto-prune repos that fail cold-scan for N retries or whose path no longer exists.
close_reason: Closed

title: wiki auto-writes docs/veska/ into user repo; contradicts README
description: README states 'Hot-zones and entry-points surfaced through MCP tools (no files written to disk)'. In practice, after `veska repo add`, the post-promotion wiki render writes `docs/veska/entry_points.md` and `docs/veska/hot_zones.md` into the user's working tree. They show up as untracked files, with no .gitignore guidance, and risk being committed by the user. Either: (a) make this opt-in, (b) write under VESKA_HOME instead, or (c) document it loudly + provide a .gitignore stanza in `veska init --agent`. Repro: `veska repo add /tmp/empty-go-repo`; `ls docs/veska/` shows two .md files; `git status` shows them untracked.
close_reason: Closed

title: eng_get_diff_blast_radius rejects short repo_id
description: README documents that 'repo_id accepts a short alias' universally, but eng_get_diff_blast_radius returns 'repo not found: 1cb168672af0' for a short id that every other tool (find_symbol, search_semantic, blast_radius, context_pack, find_changed_symbols) accepts. Likely a missing call to the short-id resolution helper in the diff_blast handler.
close_reason: Closed

title: secret-scanner: false positives on go.sum and go.mod
description: On a vanilla Go project, the first commit produces 12 severity=high secret_leak findings from the 'high-entropy' rule against go.sum hashes, go.mod module path, and indirect-dep lines (cobra/pflag/mousetrap/etc). Every new Go user trips this. The rule should exclude go.sum/go.mod by default, or recognize go-module-hash format and skip it. Repro: fresh repo, go mod init+tidy with cobra, veska repo add, commit; check eng_list_findings.
close_reason: Closed

title: xc51.3 Promotion: cross-repo CALLS via cross_repo_edge_stubs (closes 1gj write side)
description: For package-qualified calls whose import path is NOT under this repo's module, emit a cross_repo_edge_stub(src_node, kind=CALLS, module_path=importPath, symbol_path=CalleeName, language=go). The existing query-time resolver (internal/infrastructure/sqlite/resolver) already turns stubs into edges when a repo with that module_path is registered. Wire stub insertion into the promotion transaction.
acceptance_criteria: Importing a symbol from another registered repo (module_path match) yields a resolvable cross-repo CALLS edge surfaced by call_chain's cross-repo expansion; stub rows created in promotion; tests. Coordinate with solov2-1gj.
close_reason: Package-qualified calls into external modules now emit cross_repo_edge_stubs (module_path=import path, symbol_path=callee); stdlib filtered out. The existing query-time resolver binds them to the owning registered repo. Verified live (appx Execute->pflag::Parse) + unit test incl. resolver round-trip.

title: xc51.2 Promotion: intra-repo cross-package CALLS resolution -> concrete edges
description: At promotion, resolve package-qualified UnresolvedCalls whose import path is under THIS repo's module_path (from repos table). importPath - modulePath => package dir; find exported symbol CalleeName among nodes in that dir (batch first, then promoted graph) and emit concrete EdgeCalls. Must be robust to the abs(cold-scan)/rel(commit) file_path inconsistency. No false positives (skip on ambiguity/miss).
acceptance_criteria: eng_get_call_chain(main) on a cobra repo returns main->Execute after cold scan AND after an incremental commit; no spurious edges to stdlib/external; unit + live tests.
close_reason: Cross-package qualified calls resolve to concrete CALLS edges via import-map -> module-relative package dir, batch-first with promoted-graph fallback for incremental commits. module-relative path normalization handles abs/rel inconsistency. Verified live (call_chain main->Execute->helper) + unit tests for batch and incremental.

title: xc51.1 Parser: extract per-file imports + capture package-qualified calls
description: Go parser (treesitter/go.go) currently drops package-qualified selector calls (cmd.Execute()) in collectCallNames. Extend: (a) extract import specs per file into a localName->importPath map; (b) capture qualified calls as UnresolvedCall carrying a PkgQualifier alongside CalleeName. Thread Imports + qualifier through ParseResult -> StagedFile -> PromotionFile so promotion can resolve them.
acceptance_criteria: ParseResult exposes per-file imports (alias->path) and qualified UnresolvedCalls (PkgQualifier set); existing intra-file/intra-package behavior unchanged; unit tests for import extraction + qualified-call capture (incl. aliased imports).
close_reason: Parser extracts per-file import map (alias->path, blank/dot excluded) and captures package-qualified calls as UnresolvedCall.PkgQualifier; threaded ParseResult->StagedFile->PromotionFile. Existing same-package pass guarded to skip qualified calls. Tests added; TS behavior preserved.

title: Go parser discards all symbols on any syntax error (no error recovery)
description:
go.go:62-63: when hasErrorNode(root) is true, ParseFile returns ONLY a ParseFailure and zero Nodes/Edges. A single broken function erases every symbol, edge, and embedding for the whole file from the graph until the syntax is fixed. The fsnotify watcher re-parses on save, which is exactly when files are transiently broken mid-edit, so the graph degrades precisely when it's being used.

ts.go:60-62 already does the right thing: it records firstErrorFailure AND continues extracting whatever tree-sitter recovered. tree-sitter's core feature is localized error recovery, so recoverable top-level declarations are usually still valid.
design: Make go.go match ts.go: keep appending firstErrorFailure(root) to result.Failures but do NOT early-return — fall through to symbol/edge/chunk extraction. Risk to manage: partial trees can yield malformed symbol names; the original commit message for the bail-out cited 'noisy/incorrect symbols.' Mitigate by skipping individual decls whose name node is missing/inside an ERROR subtree (parse* helpers already nil-check name nodes). Acceptance: a Go file with one function containing a syntax error still indexes the file's other valid top-level functions/types; add a go_test.go case asserting the valid symbols survive alongside a recorded ParseFailure.
close_reason: Go parser no longer discards the whole file on a syntax error: it records the ParseFailure but still extracts every top-level declaration whose own subtree is error-free (per-child hasErrorNode skip). Broken decls are skipped; siblings survive the mid-edit re-parse. Test added.

title: eng_find_todos returns PascalCase Go struct fields, violating snake_case response contract
description: README: 'Responses are snake_case.' eng_find_todos leaks raw Go struct serialization: keys are FindingID, RepoID, Branch, FilePath, Message, State, CreatedAt. Every other tool returns node_id/file_path/etc. Likely a missing DTO/json tags on the todos response type.
acceptance_criteria: eng_find_todos emits snake_case keys (finding_id, repo_id, branch, file_path, message, state, created_at) consistent with the rest of the surface.
close_reason: find_todos now emits snake_case via TodoDTO; PascalCase leak gone. Also added short_id resolution. Tests added.

title: eng_get_context_pack and eng_get_hot_zone reject the short_id alias (repo not found)
description: README contract: 'anywhere a repo_id is required you may pass the full id or that short prefix.' eng_find_symbol/eng_get_blast_radius/eng_get_node honor the 12-char short_id, but eng_get_context_pack and eng_get_hot_zone return error -32002 'repo not found: <short_id>'. Both work with the full 64-char id. A user copies short_id from eng_list_repos and half the tools reject it.
acceptance_criteria: context_pack and hot_zone resolve the short_id prefix identically to the other tools; add a regression test exercising short_id across the full tool surface.
close_reason: context_pack, hot_zone, and entry_points now resolve short_id via resolveRepoID; unknown ids error loudly. Regression tests added.

title: eng_get_call_chain returns empty on multi-package repos (cross-package CALLS edges never resolved)
description: First-run journey on a standard cobra CLI (main → cmd.Execute, multi-package). eng_get_call_chain(main) and (Execute) return {nodes:[],edges:[]}. Root cause: treesitter parser emits EdgeCalls only when the callee is in the SAME file (internal/infrastructure/treesitter/go.go:260 'when the callee is known in the file'); cross-package/cross-file calls become UnresolvedCalls, never CALLS edges. DB confirms: the whole graph has only CONTAINS + SIMILAR_TO edge kinds, zero CALLS. eng_get_blast_radius appears to 'work' only because it traverses CONTAINS/SIMILAR_TO, so it returns a similarity neighborhood mislabeled as structural blast radius. Auto-link findings exist but anchor unresolved edges as findings, not CALLS edges, so call_chain still sees nothing.
acceptance_criteria: Cross-package/cross-file calls resolve into CALLS edges (or call_chain consumes accepted auto-link edges); eng_get_call_chain(main) on a cobra repo returns the main→Execute chain; document the resolution path for a new user.
notes:
INVESTIGATION (deferred — this is a feature, not a quick fix):

The parser DOES emit CALLS edges, and promotion already resolves same-package
cross-file calls — buildPackageSymbolMap in internal/infrastructure/sqlite/promotion_store.go:338
resolves UnresolvedCalls against same-directory (= same Go package) symbols and
writes EdgeCalls with Probable confidence. So intra-package call graphs work.

The gap is strictly CROSS-PACKAGE calls (e.g. main -> cmd.Execute):
1. internal/infrastructure/treesitter/go.go:401 collectCallNames only records a
   selector_expression call when the operand is the method receiver. A
   package-qualified call like cmd.Execute() is DROPPED entirely — not even
   recorded as an UnresolvedCall — so nothing downstream can resolve it.
2. Code comments (go.go:306, promotion_store.go:344) explicitly defer
   cross-package resolution to "a cross-repo IMPORTS graph (solov2-1gj)", which
   is itself deferred (P3).

WHY NOT A NAIVE FIX: resolving pkg.Symbol requires import-path -> package
mapping (the import alias `cmd` is not necessarily the package name). A naive
global leaf-name match would create FALSE-POSITIVE CALLS edges, which would
actively worsen blast_radius and the dead-code check (solov2-jdok).

VIABLE PATHS (decided to defer per owner):
- Conservative: capture package-qualified calls in collectCallNames; in the
  promotion cross-file pass, emit CALLS only when the leaf name resolves to
  exactly ONE symbol across the batch (skip ambiguous). Fixes call_chain on cold
  scan; no false edges. Does not resolve incremental single-file commits (batch
  lacks the callee's file) without also querying the promoted graph.
- Full: conservative matching + promoted-graph lookup so incremental commits
  resolve too. Largest change; overlaps solov2-1gj.

User-visible symptom: eng_get_call_chain(main) returns empty on any multi-package
repo (every cobra app). eng_get_blast_radius still traverses CONTAINS/SIMILAR_TO,
so it returns a similarity neighborhood rather than a true call/dependency radius.

Blocks: solov2-jdok. Related: solov2-1gj (deferred IMPORTS graph).
close_reason: Cross-package CALLS resolution shipped across 3 phases: parser captures imports+qualified calls (xc51.1); promotion resolves intra-module calls to concrete CALLS edges with batch+promoted-graph lookup (xc51.2); cross-module calls emit cross_repo_edge_stubs the resolver binds (xc51.3). call_chain/blast_radius now traverse real call edges on cobra-style multi-package + multi-repo graphs. Unblocks jdok.

title: Invalid repo_id is silently swallowed by graph tools instead of erroring
description: eng_find_symbol with repo_id="nope" returns {"included_staging":false} with no error and no nodes key. A stale/wrong repo_id is indistinguishable from a genuine no-match, making agent misconfiguration undebuggable.
acceptance_criteria: MCP tools return a -32602/typed error for an unknown repo_id rather than an empty success payload.
close_reason: Graph/search/blast tools now validate repo_id via resolveRepoID and return a NotFound (-32002) error for unknown ids instead of a silently-empty result. Verified live.

title: veska search (daemon-free) writes to the same veska.db as a running daemon
description: The 'daemon-free' search CLI opens and writes the same VESKA_HOME/veska.db that a live daemon holds, with no guard against concurrent operation. Two SQLite writers on the single file that holds graph+queue+FTS+vectors is a contention/corruption risk.
acceptance_criteria: veska search either coordinates with a running daemon (dials the socket) or refuses/guards against concurrent write access to veska.db.
close_reason: Closed

title: veska search <path> silently duplicates an existing repo registration
description: After 'veska repo add <path>', running 'veska search "q" <same-path>' pushed repo count 1->2. findOrRegisterRepo/matchByPath fails to match the daemon's existing record and re-registers, defeating the index-reuse AC and fragmenting the repos registry. Likely the cause of the empty-results bug (search runs against the fresh, un-embedded duplicate).
acceptance_criteria: Running veska search against an already-registered path reuses the existing repo_id; repo count does not increase.
close_reason: Not reproducible — the repo-count increase observed during the journey was a cwd artifact: 'veska search' with no path arg resolves the current working directory and registers it. With an explicit path, repo.Add is idempotent (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING on the canonical-path sha) and matchByPath canonicalises identically, so no duplicate is created. Clean repro keeps count at 1. The underlying empty-results symptom is tracked in solov2-b1q.

title: veska search CLI returns empty results for an already-indexed repo
description: The daemon-free 'veska search' CLI exits 0 with no output when run from inside (or with the explicit path of) a repo that 'veska repo add' already indexed, while the daemon's eng_search_semantic returns hits for the same repo+query. Discovered during a first-time-user journey on a fresh cobra-CLI repo.
design: See cmd/veska/search.go:76 (DefaultVectorDir db open), :100 (svc.Semantic), :282 (buildSearchService vector storage). Suspect the CLI resolves a different/empty repo_id than the daemon-promoted one — see related dup-registration bug.
acceptance_criteria: veska search <query> [path] returns the same non-empty results as eng_search_semantic for an indexed repo.
close_reason: Closed

title: Embedder health checks probe Ollama instead of elected embedder
description: veska init and 'veska doctor embedder' report embedder health by probing Ollama/nomic-embed-text (e.g. 'embedder: healthy (nomic-embed-text @ http://localhost:11434)'), but the daemon actually elects and uses model2vec(potion-code-16M) (confirmed in embedder.locked + boot log). On a clean machine (the documented zero-external-services path) init/doctor would report the embedder UNHEALTHY even though search works via model2vec, scaring new users and contradicting the headline value prop. Also reports the wrong embedder identity, and VESKA_OLLAMA_URL override was ignored by 'doctor embedder'. Fix: health probe + init/doctor/get_config output should reflect the elected in-process embedder, only probing Ollama when VESKA_EMBEDDER=ollama. Found during first-run UX walkthrough.
close_reason: Fixed and tested in working tree (uncommitted).

title: repo.Add does not detect HEAD branch — cold-scanned nodes are unqueryable (branch='')
description:
Discovered during user-journey smoke test. End-to-end repro:

1. fresh `veska init`
2. `git init -b main` a repo, add a Go file, commit
3. eng_add_repo via MCP → returns repoID, scan_pending:true
4. wait → c47 confirms cold scan ran (last_promoted_sha advanced, node_embeddings populated)
5. but: `SELECT branch FROM nodes` → all rows are '' (empty)
   `SELECT active_branch FROM repos` → NULL
6. eng_find_symbol / eng_get_file_nodes / eng_search_semantic all error "branch is required" — the cold-scanned graph is COMPLETELY UNQUERYABLE via the MCP API for end users.

Root cause: internal/repo/registry.go's Add inserts a repos row without setting active_branch (verified — only repo_id, root_path, added_at, module_path are bound). resync.go's RepoLister flattens NULL→"". cold-scan reparser propagates ActiveBranch="" into Ingester.Save → StagingArea (keyed by branch) → Promoter.Promote → nodes.branch="".

Fix options:
- In repo.Add: detect HEAD's branch via 'git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD' (or use gitwatch.Querier; though Querier is in infra not repo — wire via a small port or add a helper to internal/repo) and store it.
- Or in cold-scan reparser: when RepoRecord.ActiveBranch is empty, detect HEAD's branch at scan time and use that.
- repo.Add is the right site — active_branch is a property of the registry record, not a scan-time concern.

Once stored, downstream cold-scan + queries work without API changes.

Severity: P1. Every user who 'eng_add_repo's a freshly-init'd repo via the documented path ends up with an unqueryable graph. The system reports 'status: ok' so this is silent failure — by design the worst kind.
acceptance_criteria: After eng_add_repo against a repo with HEAD on 'main', repos.active_branch is 'main'; cold-scanned nodes have branch='main'; eng_find_symbol / eng_get_file_nodes / eng_search_semantic with branch:'main' return the expected hits.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 59c0469: repo.Add detects HEAD branch via 'git symbolic-ref' and writes active_branch on insert; hookScript bakes os.Executable() absolute path; 'veska repo add' CLI now dials the daemon MCP socket first (parity with eng_add_repo) and falls back to direct sqlite write with migrations. Tests cover all three paths.

title: Add the review post-promotion-queue lane
description:
Why: review runs as a work_kind='review' lane drained by its own poller goroutine. WorkKindReview is already reserved in the ports.WorkKind enum. Scope boundary: the lane + dispatch wiring only; the failure contract is solov2-nz2.3, token quota is solov2-nz2.5. Design ref: docs/milestones/M5.md epic m5.01. DoD: WorkKindReview handler registered in the queue poller; queue-lane test (a review row drains to done); review.enabled config gate honored; go test -race + vet + layercheck green.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
AC1: with review enabled, a promotion enqueues a work_kind='review' row that a dedicated poller goroutine drains to state='done' on success. AC2: the lane dispatches each row through the LLMGenerator using the loaded review prompts. AC3: with review disabled (default), no review row is enqueued.

CLARIFICATION (2026-05-17, approved): Enqueue one review row PER CHANGED FILE (matching the per-file embed/auto_link/revalidate lanes and nz2.4's per-file prompt Input), but ONLY when config review.enabled is true. Thread review.enabled into the promotion store's enqueue path so 'review' is conditionally appended to the per-file work kinds. The handler is a thin orchestrator: load prompt for the kind, Render(Input), call LLMGenerator.Generate (pass Prompt.Version() as GenerateRequest.PromptTemplateVersion), Parse(resp.Text). Producing domain.Findings from the parsed ReviewFindings and the failure contract are NOT in scope (nz2.6 / nz2.3) — for this task the handler may parse and drain to done; emitting review findings is nz2.6.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: with review enabled, a promotion enqueues a work_kind='review' row that a dedicated poller goroutine drains to state='done' on success. AC2: the lane dispatches each row through the LLMGenerator using the loaded review prompts. AC3: with review disabled (default), no review row is enqueued.
close_reason: DoD verified: WorkKindReview lane — per-file rows gated on review.enabled, handler dispatches via LLMGenerator+prompts; AC1-3 tested, race/vet/layercheck green

title: Harden the Ollama LLMGenerator for review use
description:
Why: llm.OllamaGenerator (internal/infrastructure/llm/ollama.go) exists as an M2 stub — no provenance, retry, timeout, or provider gate. The review pipeline needs all four. Scope boundary: the Ollama adapter + its config/startup gate only; the review goroutine is solov2-nz2.2; no hosted providers. Design ref: docs/milestones/M5.md epic m5.00. DoD: ports LLMGenerator response type carries Provenance (model_id, prompt_template_version, input_hash) per SOLO-05 §2.5; configurable timeout honored; unit tests for retry-count, provenance population, startup refusal; go test -race + vet + layercheck green.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
AC1: a successful Generate call returns provenance carrying model id, prompt-template version, and an input hash. AC2: a transient Ollama failure (HTTP 5xx or network error) is retried up to 3x with backoff; a 4xx is not retried. AC3: starting the daemon with [llm_generator].provider != 'ollama' exits non-zero with a message naming the V2.0.1 deferral.

CLARIFICATION (2026-05-17, approved): Provenance threading — add a PromptTemplateVersion field to ports.GenerateRequest; the caller supplies it and the generator echoes it into Provenance alongside model_id (known by the generator) and input_hash (computed by the generator from the prompt). The config field config.LLMGenerator.Provider already exists (defaults 'ollama'); the AC3 fail-fast gate lives in cmd/veska-daemon/wire.go.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: a successful Generate call returns provenance carrying model id, prompt-template version, and an input hash. AC2: a transient Ollama failure (HTTP 5xx or network error) is retried up to 3x with backoff; a 4xx is not retried. AC3: starting the daemon with [llm_generator].provider != 'ollama' exits non-zero with a message naming the V2.0.1 deferral.
close_reason: DoD verified: ports.Provenance + retry/timeout + non-ollama provider gate; AC1-3 tested, build/race/vet/layercheck green

title: M5 — Optional Review Pipeline
description:
Full plan: docs/milestones/M5.md (status: active).

## Problem
After M4 the graph is populated, searchable, and has mechanical wiki surfaces — but nothing reviews changes for security issues or contract drift that need an LLM to spot.

## Approach
An optional LLM-driven review pipeline as a post-promotion goroutine (work_kind='review'), OFF by default, local Ollama only at V2.0. Findings surface via MCP under the human-action gate. Token-budget capped with an honest cost story. Config lives in the [llm_generator]/[review] sections of config.toml (loader shipped in solov2-s5c.6). WorkKindReview is already reserved in the ports.WorkKind enum.

## Goals
- Review pipeline disabled by default; one LLMGenerator impl (ollama), fail-fast on any other provider
- Per-promotion token-budget cap enforced; honest cost surface in veska doctor
- Review findings surface via eng_list_findings under the human-action gate
- MCP surface completed: 27 -> 32 tools (full SOLO-09 surface)

## Out of scope
- Hosted LLM providers (anthropic/openai/gemini) -> V2.0.1
- USD cost machinery -> V2.0.1
- New graph/vector substrate

## Dependency order
Roots: .1 (LLMGenerator), .4 (prompts), .7 (MCP tools). Then .2 (lane) after .1+.4; then .3/.5/.6 after .2.
close_reason: M5 milestone closed: all 5 exit gates met (review time budget measured 1h35m31s/100 files); M5.md moved to closed/

title: config.toml is not parsed; CONFIG-SURFACE documents an unwired config layer
description: Surfaced by: operations/CONFIG-SURFACE.md (solov2-4ty.25). internal/config ships only paths.go — there is no TOML loader, so every [section] key in CONFIG-SURFACE.md (daemon, backup, embedder, post_promotion_queue, mcp, etc.) is currently inert. Also a path divergence: CONFIG-SURFACE documents [daemon] cli_socket_path=~/.veska/cli.sock but internal/config/paths.go DaemonSockPath() returns /daemon.sock. And VESKA_VECTOR_BACKEND (in CLAUDE.md) is missing from the CONFIG-SURFACE env-var table. Decide: build config.toml parsing, or prune CONFIG-SURFACE to the env-var-only reality.
close_reason: DoD verified: minimal config.toml loader (env>toml>default precedence + Validate) added, threaded into wire.go for embedder rate + queue poll interval; build/test-race/vet/layercheck green

title: Verify design doc 12-wiki
description:
Doc in scope: docs/design/12-wiki/README.md. M4 just shipped the wiki surface.

What to check:
1. hot_zone + entry_points mechanical kinds described match internal/application/wiki (HotZoneService, EntryPointsService, renderers, page-path constants docs/veska/).
2. Ranking formula (recent_change_frequency x blast_radius) and entry-point criteria (low blast radius, adjacent test, no open findings) match the code.
3. WorkKindWiki regeneration lane + veska wiki command + doctor wiki_render check described.
4. Any LLM-driven wiki kinds clearly marked as M5/future, not shipped.
5. files: frontmatter paths exist; internal links + ADR cross-refs resolve.
6. Frontmatter present; add verified:true + verified_date.

title: Verify design doc 09-mcp-surface (tool registry)
description:
Doc in scope: docs/design/09-mcp-surface/README.md. HIGH DRIFT RISK — M4 added 3 tools.

What to check:
1. Every MCP tool documented exists in internal/infrastructure/mcp (registry + tools_*.go); every registered tool is documented.
2. Total tool count is correct: 24 as of M3, 27 after M4 (eng_get_context_pack, eng_get_hot_zone, eng_get_entry_points added).
3. Tool names obey eng_<verb>_<object> (ADR-S0008).
4. Per-tool params / response envelopes match the handler structs.
5. files: frontmatter paths exist; internal links + ADR cross-refs resolve.
6. Frontmatter present; add verified:true + verified_date.
Note: this doc is M4.md's 'SOLO-09' — context-pack bundle structure should be documented here or the reference reconciled.
close_reason: verified: reconciled 32->27 registered tool count, marked 5 unregistered tools planned, corrected context_pack scoping; finding solov2-s5c.2 filed

title: Verify + correct milestone doc M4, move to closed/
description:
Doc in scope: docs/milestones/M4.md. KNOWN STALE — epic solov2-erp enumerated the corrections.

What to check / fix:
1. Frontmatter status: planned -> closed (or done); update last_reviewed.
2. related: [SOLO-12, SOLO-14] — SOLO-12 does not exist in the repo; remove or correct.
3. Exit gate line says eng_context_pack — correct to eng_get_context_pack (MCP names require a verb).
4. m4.01 DoD says 'Bundle structure documented in SOLO-09' — SOLO-09 does not exist; correct to self-documenting / the bead.
5. Tool count: M4.md says 26 from M3 / 29 after M4. Actual: 24 registered as of M3, 27 after M4. Verify against the registry and correct.
6. m4.04 note about WorkKind enum: confirm WorkKindWiki now exists.
7. Tracker IDs: M4.md cites veska:m4.01-context-pack etc. and sub-issues m4.01.1-handler etc. — the real epic is solov2-erp with children solov2-erp.1..7. Correct the tracker references.
8. Exit gates / DoD checkboxes -> reflect actual shipped state (all delivered).
9. Internal links resolve.
10. Add verified:true + verified_date, then move the file to docs/milestones/closed/M4.md (git mv) and fix any links that pointed at the old path.

title: Docs audit — verify all docs/ against shipped M0-M4 code
description:
Why: M0-M4 are shipped; before starting M5 we need every doc under docs/ verified against the actual codebase so M5 builds on an accurate base. M4.md is known-stale (wrong tool counts, eng_context_pack naming, non-existent SOLO-09/12 refs, missing WorkKindWiki). The milestones/README.md links to M1-M3 at the wrong path (they are in closed/).

Approach: one child issue per doc (or small coherent group). Each is audited via the doc-sweep skill — file-existence audit, section audit vs code, cross-reference check, then marked verified:true + verified_date. Scope gaps found become new beads, not silent edits.

Out of scope: changing design intent; implementing code. This epic only verifies and corrects documentation.

Definition of done: every child closed; every doc carries verified:true frontmatter; M4.md corrected and moved to docs/milestones/closed/.
close_reason: Docs audit complete: all 26 docs verified against shipped M0-M4 code; M4.md corrected + moved to closed/; 10 code/design findings filed under M4.5 (solov2-s5c)

title: Add a git commit-history reader
description:
Why: hot_zone ranking (sibling task) and the context-pack tool need per-file commit history, but internal/infrastructure/git only exposes working-tree diff (ChangedFiles) — there is no windowed/historical reader. Without this, the hot_zone ranking formula has no recent_change_frequency input.

Scope boundary: read-only history queries only. Does NOT rank, render, or touch git hooks/promotion. The worker chooses the data source — git log over a window, or derive from post_promotion_queue done-rows (note: queue done-rows may be gc-pruned, so git-log is the more durable choice).

DoD: unit tests against a fixture repo; go test + go vet + make layercheck green. Design ref: docs/milestones/M4.md, epic solov2-erp.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: reader returns per-file change counts within a configurable window (default 30 days); out-of-window changes excluded. AC2: reader returns the list of recent commits touching a given file path. AC3: both queries deterministic for fixed repo state + window.
close_reason: DoD verified: windowed git commit-history reader (ChangeCounts/FileHistory) with deterministic output, fixture tests + layercheck green

title: M4 — Wiki Mechanical Kinds
description:
M4 milestone root. Canonical doc: docs/milestones/M4.md (this epic supersedes that doc's tool-count arithmetic and the eng_context_pack naming — see below).

## Problem
After M3 the graph is populated and searchable but there is no human- or agent-facing digest of it. Engineers and AI agents need to know where the codebase is volatile (hot zones) and where it is safe to start (entry points), with no LLM in the loop.

## Approach
Two mechanical wiki pages — hot_zone, entry_points — rendered as pure deterministic functions of promoted graph state to docs/veska/. Plus an eng_get_context_pack MCP tool bundling graph context for an agent. No LLM anywhere in M4 (that is M5).

## Goals
- hot_zone + entry_points pages render to docs/veska/, deterministic (same promoted state -> byte-identical output)
- both pages regenerate on promotion in <5s for a typical commit
- eng_get_context_pack returns <50ms p95 for typical inputs
- no M4 code path calls the LLM generator
- 3 new MCP tools (eng_get_context_pack, eng_get_hot_zone, eng_get_entry_points) -> registry total 24 -> 27

## Out of scope
- LLM-driven review/summarization (M5)
- docs/ cutover (M6)
- new graph/vector substrate

## Verified corrections to M4.md
- M4.md says 26 tools from M3 / 29 after M4. Actual registered count is 24; M4 adds 3 -> 27.
- M4.md goal line says eng_context_pack; the MCP registry rejects names without a verb (eng_<verb>_<object>). Correct name: eng_get_context_pack.
- M4.md m4.04 says work_kind='wiki'; the WorkKind enum has no 'wiki' value — a task adds WorkKindWiki.
- docs/veska/ does not exist; created by M4.
- recent_change_frequency has no data source — internal/infrastructure/git only does working-tree diff. A foundational task adds a commit-history reader.
- SOLO-09/12/14 referenced by M4.md are not in the repo; the context-pack task is self-documenting.

## Risks / open questions
- Change-frequency source: worker chooses git-log-over-window vs deriving from post_promotion_queue done-rows (the latter is gc-fragile; git-log is more durable).

## Constraints
- MCP tool names must match eng_<verb>_<object>.
- Deterministic output: sorted iteration, no map-order leakage.
close_reason: M4 Wiki Mechanical Kinds complete: all 7 child tasks shipped

title: Embedder must L2-normalize vectors before storage (autolink threshold broken)
description:
ROOT CAUSE found during d5z gate-3 work.

internal/application/autolink/autolink.go documents: 'score = 1/(1+dist) ... threshold is a simple lower bound on Hit.Score in [0,1]'. This holds ONLY for unit-normalized vectors, where L2-squared distance is bounded in [0,4].

nomic-embed-text (the default embedding model) returns vectors with L2-norm ~19, NOT 1.0. For two within-cluster vectors at cosine 0.74, L2-squared distance is ~188, so score = 1/(1+188) = 0.005. EVERY candidate score collapses toward zero; the autolink DefaultThreshold of 0.85 (and any threshold > ~0.01) emits zero candidates. Auto-link is effectively non-functional against nomic in production.

Verified empirically: gate-3 autolink FP harness produced tp=0 fp=0 total=0 across a full threshold sweep (0.85 -> 0.50) at 6k and 50k pop, despite recall@10 measuring 1.00 on the same vectors (ranking is magnitude-tolerant; threshold filtering is not).

Fix options:
(a) embedder.Worker L2-normalizes each vector before SaveEmbedding/MarkReady. Simplest, one place, makes score=1/(1+dist) land in [0,1] as documented.
(b) Both VectorStorage backends normalize on UpsertEmbeddings. Two places.
(c) autolink switches to cosine similarity explicitly. Changes the port contract.

Recommend (a). After the fix, re-tune autolink.DefaultThreshold against the gate-3 numbers from solov2-d5z (the harness now normalizes fixture vectors so its threshold is already meaningful).

Affects: internal/application/embedder/embedder.go, internal/application/autolink/autolink.go (DefaultThreshold + package doc), possibly the m3.03 search path.
close_reason: Fixed via option (a): embedder.Worker L2-normalizes every vector right after Embed succeeds (single point feeding node_embeddings + inflight cache + VectorStorage upsert). autolink package doc updated to state the unit-vector dependency; DefaultThreshold retuned 0.85 -> 0.60 against the d5z gate-3 numbers (within-topic ~0.66, cross-topic ~0.50; 0.60 gave 100% recall, 0.00% FP on the fixture). TestWorker_NormalizesVectorsBeforeStorage added. Full suite green.

title: Ollama EmbeddingProvider adapter (production impl)
description:
No production EmbeddingProvider adapter exists in internal/infrastructure/. Required for M3 gates 1 (embedder throughput), 2 (semantic recall), 3 (auto-link FP rate against real fixture). Spec:

- Package: internal/infrastructure/embedding/ollama/ollama.go.
- Implements ports.EmbeddingProvider: Embed(ctx, text) ([]float32, error) + ModelID() string.
- HTTP client against $VESKA_OLLAMA_URL (default http://localhost:11434); model name from $VESKA_EMBED_MODEL (default nomic-embed-text).
- Endpoint: POST /api/embeddings {model, prompt}; response {embedding: []float}.
- Error wrapping: any net.OpError / DNS failure / EOF / 5xx / connection refused MUST be wrapped with errors.Is-friendly fmt.Errorf containing ports.ErrEmbedderUnreachable. This is what triggers search.Service's lexical fallback (m3.03.2). 4xx body validation errors do NOT wrap the sentinel (those are caller faults; let them propagate).
- Timeout: configurable (Option), default 30s. Honour ctx cancellation.
- ModelID returns the configured model string verbatim. If empty, panic at construction.
- Functional options: WithBaseURL, WithModel, WithHTTPClient, WithTimeout.
- Tests: httptest.NewServer covering happy path, 5xx, connection refused (force-close listener), ctx cancel, wrong response shape.
- Dimension assertion: m3.02.1's worker passes dim to MarkReady; verify the adapter returns a stable dim for the configured model (don't enforce a hardcoded 768; the worker accepts whatever the adapter produces).

Blocked-by: nothing (independent piece). Blocks: solov2-3lv (daemon wiring needs to construct this), and via that, M3 gates 1/2/3.
close_reason: Closed

title: m3.05 perf: revalidation sweep exceeds 60s for 10k findings (fsync-per-UPDATE bottleneck)
description:
Bench tools/loadtest/revalidate (added in solov2-64k.5.4) measures 69.8s wall time for the 10k-node / 10k-edge / 3k-stale-finding M3 exit-gate fixture. Target is <60s.

Profile: per-file Handle calls a sequence of single-row UPDATE statements via RevalidateRepo.RefreshAnchorHash (and CloseAsRevalidatedObsolete). With 3000 stale findings and default SQLite WAL+sync settings each UPDATE auto-commits and fsyncs, giving the observed ~23ms per finding * 3000 = ~70s.

Likely fix candidates:
  1. Batch all per-file refresh+close UPDATEs into a single tx inside Handler.Handle (or at the repo layer via a new BatchApply port method). This drops the fsync count from O(findings) to O(files) = 100, well inside budget.
  2. PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL on the eval bench DB — production may already do this; verify with sqlite.OpenWithOptions defaults.
  3. Combined UPDATE...WHERE finding_id IN (...) batches.

Smaller isolation sub-tests (4 files / 80 findings) pass comfortably (~1.5s wall) so the bottleneck is fsync count, not query plan.

Bench reproduction:
  make eval-revalidate-bench
Current run: elapsed=69815ms, p95_handle=1306ms, refreshed=3000, closed=0, gate=FAIL.

Blocks: solov2-64k.5.4 (revalidation timing bench DoD).
close_reason: DoD verified: ApplyDecisions port + adapter batches per-file UPDATEs in one tx (sql.Tx + prepared stmts); Handler.Handle uses one tx per file; metrics increment only post-commit; empty batch is no-op; pre-existing 5.2/5.3 tests green. Bench Combined10k: 69.8s → 6.4s (10.9× headroom under 60s gate). Commit 15e5e4c.

title: Compose daemon main loop (M3 wiring)
description:
cmd/veska-daemon/main.go is a 75-line shell. None of the long-running pieces are wired up:

- MCP Unix socket server (cli.sock + mcp.sock) — registry.MustRegister + RegisterTool* calls
- StagingArea + IngestionGate + Ingester (with SetFindingStorage(sqlite.FindingRepo))
- Promoter (with SetCheckRunner(checks.Runner registered with DeadCodeCheck + ContractDriftCheck))
- embedder.Worker (Start/Stop; default rate 10/s; max attempts 3)
- queue.Poller registering ports.WorkHandler for ports.WorkKindEmbed (Embedder is currently invoked direct; wire via poller for symmetry), AutoLink (autolink.Handler), Revalidate (revalidate.Handler)
- fsnotify watcher → Ingester.Save
- procmgr for any external supervised processes (sqlite-vec native? Ollama is external, no procmgr needed)
- Ollama EmbeddingProvider adapter (does not yet exist; missing) — must wrap network/connection errors with ports.ErrEmbedderUnreachable so search.Service's lexical fallback actually triggers.
- DI validation: every NewX constructor that panics on nil collaborator should be replaced by typed errors during composition root — verify with one failing test per missing dep.
- Schedule.Embedder workers, revalidate workers, autolink workers — start each as part of daemon Start; Stop on signal.
- Wiring depends on: m3.01 check pipeline, m3.02 embedder worker, m3.04 autolink handler, m3.05 revalidate handler. All five features now closed; this is the integration step.
close_reason: DoD verified: daemon composes MCP+staging+gate+ingester+promoter+embedder+queue poller+watcher under one lifecycle; typed ErrMissingDep on bad config; dual-backend vector via env; Ollama wired; SIGINT/SIGTERM ordered shutdown. Follow-ups filed: 9un, rer, ktv, nlg, 30r.

Commit 4ceff42. Full -race suite clean.

title: m3.02.1 — Batch loop: pull pending, batch by 32, embed
description:
Goroutine that pulls node_embedding_refs where state='pending' and embeds in batches of 32. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.02.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):
1. Enqueue path: this task wires Promoter to INSERT a pending row into node_embedding_refs for every promoted node (one extra prepared stmt in the existing tx, mirroring queueStmt).
2. Each batch: read text for the node, call EmbeddingProvider.Embed, write bytes into node_embeddings (content-addressed PK content_hash), set node_embedding_refs.state='ready' + content_hash + embedded_at, AND upsert into VectorStorage so m3.03 search can hit them in the same tick.
3. Lifecycle: long-running goroutine with Start(ctx)/Stop(); default poll cadence 250ms (matches post-promotion-queue worker pattern). NOT routed through post_promotion_queue.
4. Out of scope: rate limit (2.2), retry policy (2.3), content_hash dedup before calling Ollama (2.4). m3.02.1 calls Embed unconditionally per row.
close_reason: DoD verified: Promoter enqueues pending refs atomically; embedder.Worker drains in batches of ≤32, embeds, writes content-addressed node_embeddings, upserts VectorStorage, marks ref ready; Start/Stop race-clean (sync.Once + mu-guarded cancel); veska_embed_queue_depth gauge; per-row errors isolated; idempotent via ON CONFLICT. Commit 7f374c8. Full suite -race clean.

title: m3.01.1 — Synchronous check pipeline runner
description:
Runner that executes registered structural checks after the promotion tx commits, before Promoter.Promote returns to the hook. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.01.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):
- Execution: post-tx (tx commits first, then checks run against the committed state). Findings are advisory and CANNOT abort a promotion. This task ships the dispatch shape; per-check rollback semantics are explicitly NOT supported.
- Findings persistence: bundle a FindingStorage port (internal/core/ports) + sqlite repo adapter (internal/infrastructure/sqlite) in this task so that m3.01.{2,3,4} only have to author check logic.
- Scope: interface (Check), registry, invocation site in Promoter, FindingStorage port + adapter, per-check timing emitted via the existing veska_seal_latency_seconds histogram (label per check name).
- Out of scope: actual check implementations (1.2/1.3/1.4).
close_reason: DoD verified: Check interface + Registry + Runner ship; Promoter.SetCheckRunner invokes post-commit; FindingStorage port + sqlite adapter round-trip source_layer='structural'; veska_check_latency_seconds histogram per-check; panic-guard ensures checks never abort promotion; no-checks regression test green. Commit 53ba029. 42+ pkgs -race clean.

title: M3 — Pipelines and Embedder
description:
M3 milestone root. Promotion pipeline runs structural checks synchronously; async embedder worker drains node_embedding_refs; semantic_search live on dual-backend VectorStorage (sqlite-vec default, usearch above threshold per ADR-S0015); auto-link surfaces unresolved edges as findings; revalidation invalidates stale findings on content drift; 6 new MCP tools registered (total 24 after M3, post solov2-4r6 stub removal). Canonical doc: docs/milestones/M3.md. Dependencies: M1, M2 (incl. m2.06 dual-backend ratification). Out of scope: new vector backend (done in M2), LLM review (M5), wiki refresh (M4).

## State at 2026-05-14 (sub-tasks complete; gates remain)

All 6 features (m3.01–m3.06) closed. 25 feature commits + 1 cleanup pass + 1 perf fix.

## M3 exit gates — close-out checklist

- [ ] Gate 1: Embedder throughput 5–20 emb/s on Ollama. Blocked by: Ollama adapter + gate-1 bench bead.
- [ ] Gate 2: Semantic recall @ 50k and 250k. Blocked by: Ollama adapter + fixture-seed bead.
- [ ] Gate 3: Auto-link FP rate on fixture. Blocked by: Ollama adapter + fixture-seed bead.
- [x] Gate 4: Revalidation <60s @ 10k edges. MET at 6.4s (commit 15e5e4c).
- [ ] Gate 5: All work_kind lanes drain under fuzz. Blocked by: daemon wiring + fuzz bead + gate-5 framing decision (3 vs 4 lanes).

## Critical-path beads for M3 close

1. (P1) solov2-3lv — daemon wiring (composes everything).
2. (P1) Ollama adapter bead (this batch).
3. (P2) gate-5 framing decision bead (this batch).
4. (P2) gate-5 fuzz test bead (this batch).
5. (P2) gate-1 throughput bench bead (this batch).
6. (P2) Ollama fixture-seed operational bead (this batch).
7. Update M3.md with measured numbers; close solov2-64k.

## Carry-forward beads (NOT blockers for M3 close)

- solov2-jml (parse-failure reopen-on-fix)
- solov2-311 (embed text enrichment)
- solov2-l8y (auto-link revalidation refinement)
- solov2-4j5 (eng_find_changed_symbols deferred per SOLO-09)
- solov2-mu6, bx6, v65, a8z, 2hk (cleanup/refactor follow-ups)
close_reason: All M3 exit gates resolved: 1 MET (8.43 emb/s), 2 MET (0.99@50k), 3 PARTIAL (harness end-to-end, corpus + backend follow-ups filed: wqi/d5z/0vy), 4 MET (6.4s), 5 MET (3-lane drain). Doc moved to closed/M3.md.

title: Update SOLO-08 §3.1 / §3.2 / §4 per branch-in-PK verdict
description:
**Why.** SOLO-08 §3 schema is the substrate M1's m1.03 builds on; a red verdict requires the schema to be rewritten before m1.03 begins.

**Scope boundary.** SOLO-08 sections only (and a delta-override ADR if red). ADR-S0001 is .10; SOLO-13 is .12.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Outcomes branch-in-PK table; §Epic m0.03.2.
acceptance_criteria:
green / yellow → SOLO-08 §3 / §4 unchanged; a note records the curve and the m1.08 re-verification expectation
red → an ADR for the delta-override schema ("Option C") is drafted and accepted; SOLO-08 §3.1 / §3.2 are rewritten against it before M1's m1.03 begins
SOLO-08 commit cites the branch-pk spike commit hash from .9
close_reason: DoD verified: OQ-S006 resolved (green), §3 note + §8 update, spike commit 72d6ca4 cited

title: Update ADR-S0001 status per vector-substrate verdict
description:
**Why.** ADR-S0001 names sqlite-vec as the entire vector substrate; its status must reflect measured numbers before M1's m1.03 begins.

**Scope boundary.** ADR-S0001 only (and a replacement/pivot ADR if required by verdict). SOLO-08 is .11; SOLO-13 is .12.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Outcomes vector table; §Epic m0.03.1.
acceptance_criteria:
green → status remains accepted; new Measured section added with numbers from .5
yellow → status remains accepted; OQ-S003 gains a numeric kill criterion derived from the curve
red-quality → ADR-S0001 amended or retracted; replacement ADR drafted and accepted citing measured failure data
red-ceiling → ADR-S0001 amended citing the ceiling number; OQ-S003 HNSW pivot ADR promoted from M3 to M1 work and accepted before m1.03 begins
ADR commit cites the vector spike commit hash from .5
close_reason: DoD verified: ADR-S0001 amended (ceiling=100k), OQ-S001 resolved, OQ-S003 promoted to M1, ADR-S0014 created

title: M0 — Substrate Spike (sqlite-vec + branch-in-PK)
description:
# M0 — Substrate Spike

Canonical doc: `docs/milestones/M0.md` (extract below).

## Problem
ADR-S0001 names sqlite-vec as the entire vector substrate, and SOLO-08 §4 puts `branch` in the PK of nodes/edges/findings. Both rest on measurements that have not been taken. Building M1 on a failing assumption forces a fallback design under schedule pressure with code already written on top.

## Approach
Two throwaway Go spike harnesses against the target schemas — one for sqlite-vec at 50k/1M vectors, one for branch-in-PK at 50 branches × 100k symbols. Numbers feed decision matrices in M0.md §Outcomes. Failures amend the relevant ADR/schema before M1 starts.

## Goals
- Vector-substrate verdict (green/yellow/red-quality/red-ceiling) with warm p95, recall@10, vec0 ceiling at 50k and 1M
- Branch-in-PK verdict with row growth, disk size, indexed-lookup p95, GC sweep cost
- ADR-S0001, SOLO-08, SOLO-13, OQ-S001/S003/S006 updated against measured numbers
- M1 plan confirmed or amended before m1.03 begins

## Out of scope
- Any production code in `internal/`
- Parser, promotion pipeline, application layer
- Real-repo data (m1.08 re-verifies later)
- All spike code is throwaway; deleted or moved to attic when M0 closes

## Constraints
- Reference hardware: 8-core, 16 GiB RAM, NVMe SSD (M1 MacBook Air or equivalent x86-64 laptop). Numbers measured on this tier are normative.
- Vec0 ceiling must be ≥ 250 000 embedded nodes to avoid red-ceiling verdict.

## Risks / open questions
- OQ-S001 (vec0 latency/recall envelope), OQ-S003 (HNSW pivot trigger), OQ-S006 (branch-in-PK scaling) all resolve out of this milestone.
- A red verdict on either spike blocks M1's m1.03; m0.03 owns the amendment work.

See `docs/milestones/M0.md` for the full outcome tables, sub-issue checklist, and rationale.
close_reason: M0 complete: vec0 RED-CEILING (100k nodes, spike 4d63d34), branch-in-PK GREEN (0.039ms/0.047ms, spike 72d6ca4); ADR-S0001 amended, ADR-S0014 accepted, OQ-S001/S003/S006 resolved, M1 amended with m1.hnsw-pivot gate

title: MCP arg-name conventions inconsistent; CLI uses --name/--file/--path, MCP wants symbol/node_id/file_path
description: Without inputSchema (solov2-jtl5.9), callers guess arg names. Observed mismatches across the surface: eng_find_symbol takes 'symbol' (CLI: --name), eng_get_blast_radius takes 'node_id' (CLI: --file → resolved to node), eng_find_owner takes 'file_path' (intuitive 'path' rejected), eng_get_diff_blast_radius silently returned 0 entries when given 'base':'HEAD~1' (likely wrong key, probably wants base_sha). Either populate inputSchema OR (better) standardize on file_path/repo_id/node_id consistently AND accept friendly aliases. Right now an LLM-driven client must do 3-5 round trips to learn each tool's args.

title: repo list status='promoted' even when last cold scan failed (stale state)
description: After repo add with cold scan failing (SQLITE_BUSY), 'veska repo list' showed status='promoted' for the new repo /tmp/junior-test-repo. The most recent promotion FAILED — there is no promoted SHA in the DB for this repo. Status should distinguish 'never-promoted' / 'last promotion failed' / 'promoted' clearly. Ask: surface a 'failed: <short reason>' state and a last_promoted_at column so users notice broken repos.

title: repo add --wait races: '0 files (0.0s)' + 'scan no longer in flight; check logs for errors' on fast-scanning repos
description: Adding a 1-file repo with --wait printed 'walking → 0 files (0.0s)' then 'scan no longer in flight; check ~/.veska/logs/daemon.log for errors'. The repo actually scanned successfully (or in my case, failed with SQLITE_BUSY). The CLI offers no signal of pass/fail — every fast scan looks like 'something went wrong, go read logs'. Asks: (1) --wait should report final cold-scan outcome (promoted vs failed + reason) instead of the alarming hint; (2) include file count from the actual scan, not the in-flight progress.

title: config reload races daemon, surfaces SQLITE_BUSY JSON-RPC error to user
description: After 'veska config reload', second reload returned: '✗ 0b3b70ab9f89: daemon: promote: promoter: sink before-delete for ...: database is locked (5) (SQLITE_BUSY) (code -32603)'. The leaked JSON-RPC code and 'sink before-delete' phrasing leak internals to the user, and the reload partial-fails silently in the exit code (continued onto next repo). Asks: (1) reload should serialize against pending vulnrefresh/promotion work so SQLITE_BUSY doesn't happen; (2) error formatting should strip internal code -32603 and sink phrasing for the CLI; (3) reload exit status should be non-zero when any repo failed (need to check).
close_reason: Fix in 02c7a3c: WriteHot + WriteEmbed merged into single Write pool. SQLite WAL admits one writer at the file level; two in-process pools just raced for it. With MaxOpenConns=1 on the single pool, concurrent writers queue on the Go conn instead of racing on the SQLite writer slot. New TestOpenPools_ConcurrentWrites_NoSQLITEBUSY pins the invariant (8 writers × 25 txns, no BUSY).

title: secret_leak rule flags beads issues.jsonl high-entropy memory keys as secrets
description: On solov2, 4 'high' severity findings of rule=secret_leak fired on .beads/issues.jsonl lines containing memory key entries like {"_type":"memory","key":"..."}. These are not credentials; they are bd-remember entries with high-entropy slugs. Recommendations: (1) make secret_leak honor a path-glob ignore list (out of box: .beads/, .bd/, etc.); (2) high-entropy heuristic should skip lines obviously inside structured non-secret schemas (JSONL with _type=memory). At minimum, ship a built-in ignore for the beads tracker file since beads is the official issue tracker for this project.

title: cold scan: status frozen at '(walking, N files)' for entire scan, even during 8m+ parse phase
description: On solov2 (~540 files), 'veska repo list' showed '(walking, 520 files)' continuously for ~9 minutes. Daemon log: cold scan start 01:27:57, promotion start 01:36:24 (8m27s later), promotion complete 01:37:19. Walk completes quickly; the long tail is parse+embed. Status message implies 'still walking,' so a junior user assumes the tool is hung. Two asks: (1) update status as it transitions (walking → parsing → embedding → promoting) so users see forward motion; (2) update the file count as parsing progresses (it sat at 520 even though 728 files were ultimately handled).

title: repo add post-add hint misattributes --wait to 'veska repo list'
description: After 'veska repo add <path>', the CLI prints: 'veska repo list shows status (pass --wait to block here)'. The --wait flag is on 'repo add', not 'repo list' — running 'veska repo list --wait' returns 'unknown flag: --wait'. As a first-time user I copy/pasted the hint and got an error. Suggested fix: rephrase the hint to make clear --wait is a flag on 'repo add' itself, e.g. 'veska repo list shows status, or re-run with --wait to block here'.
close_reason: Fixed in next commit: hint rephrased to 'veska repo list shows status; re-run with --wait to block until it finishes' — --wait is correctly attributed to the user's own re-invocation of repo add.

title: veska init does not create ~/.veska/config.toml — docs claim it does
description:
CONFIG-SURFACE.md §1 states: '~/.veska/config.toml | Daemon config. Created by veska init if absent.' But running 'veska init' on a clean system produces no config.toml — only cli.sock, mcp.sock, daemon.log, veska.db etc.
