5. No reference to milestones at stale paths (M1-M3 live in milestones/closed/).
close_reason: verified via doc-sweep batch 1

title: Add the WorkKindWiki post-promotion regeneration lane
description:
Why: the hot_zone and entry_points pages must refresh automatically after a promotion. M4.md epic m4.04.

Scope boundary: adds a WorkKindWiki value to the ports.WorkKind enum (M4.md assumes 'wiki' exists — it does NOT), a render orchestration that regenerates BOTH pages, the queue WorkHandler, and poller registration; persists a last-render timestamp. The veska wiki CLI and the doctor surfacing are separate tasks.

DoD: queue-lane test (a wiki row drains to state=done); regeneration timing test (<5s); go test + go vet green. Design ref: docs/milestones/M4.md, epic solov2-erp.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
AC1: a promotion enqueues a wiki work item that regenerates both pages. AC2: regeneration of both pages completes in <5s for a typical commit. AC3: the most recent successful render time is persisted and readable.

CLARIFICATIONS (2026-05-16, approved by human):
- Enqueue granularity: enqueue exactly ONE wiki row per promotion (NOT per-file). Modify the enqueue loop in internal/infrastructure/sqlite/promotion_store.go to emit one per-promotion WorkKindWiki row (empty/repo-scoped payload) alongside the existing per-file rows. WorkKindWiki is NOT added to the per-file workKinds list.
- Last-render timestamp persistence: store in the existing daemon_state key-value table (key TEXT PK, value TEXT, set_at INTEGER) — e.g. key 'wiki.last_render_at'. daemon_state is runtime/operational state, the correct home (vs database_meta = schema meta). Expose a read path so solov2-erp.7 (doctor) can surface it.
- The wiki.Handler (ports.WorkHandler for WorkKindWiki) calls wiki.RenderHotZones + wiki.RenderEntryPoints and writes both pages to docs/veska/ (HotZonesPagePath / EntryPointsPagePath constants). Register in wire.go poller handler map.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: a promotion enqueues a wiki work item that regenerates both pages. AC2: regeneration of both pages completes in <5s for a typical commit. AC3: the most recent successful render time is persisted and readable.
close_reason: DoD verified: WorkKindWiki lane — one wiki row/promotion, handler regenerates both pages, last-render time persisted to daemon_state; queue-lane + <5s tests + layercheck green

title: Add the eng_get_context_pack MCP tool
description:
Why: agents need a token-bounded bundle of graph context for a symbol or task in one MCP call. M4.md epic m4.01.

Scope boundary: the eng_get_context_pack MCP tool only. The bundle structure is documented in this task (self-documenting; SOLO-09 referenced by M4.md does not exist). No LLM. 'recent commits' read via the commit-history reader (solov2-erp.1 FileHistory).

DoD: handler tests for both modes + budget truncation; p95 latency assertion; go vet green. Design ref: docs/milestones/M4.md, epic solov2-erp.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
AC1: input {symbol} OR {task_id} returns a JSON bundle of relevant nodes, recent commits, open findings, and tasks. AC2: output respects a configurable token budget (default 8192) — an oversized bundle is truncated, not rejected. AC3: returns under 50ms p95 for typical inputs (measured in a test).

CLARIFICATIONS (2026-05-16, approved by human):
- {symbol} mode: relevant nodes = FindNodes(symbol) result plus its blast radius (blastradius.Service.Of); recent commits = FileHistory for those nodes' files; open findings = FindingQuerier scoped to those nodes; tasks = active task for the repo.
- {task_id} mode: domain.Task has no graph link. Treat the task's repo working-tree diff (git.ChangedFiles) as the symbol set: relevant nodes = nodes in changed files (NodesInFile), like eng_get_diff_blast_radius. recent commits / findings / tasks derived from that node set + the task itself.
- Token budget: estimate token count with a deterministic heuristic (e.g. len(bytes)/4); truncate lowest-priority sections first so an oversized bundle is clipped, not rejected. Budget configurable, default 8192.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: input {symbol} OR {task_id} returns a JSON bundle of relevant nodes, recent commits, open findings, and tasks. AC2: output respects a configurable token budget (default 8192) — an oversized bundle is truncated, not rejected. AC3: returns under 50ms p95 for typical inputs (measured in a test).
close_reason: DoD verified: eng_get_context_pack tool, both modes, token-budget truncation, p95<50ms; tests + layercheck green

title: Build the entry_points surface
description:
Why: surface low-risk symbols a newcomer or agent can safely start from. M4.md epic m4.03.

Scope boundary: candidate selection + Markdown render + the eng_get_entry_points MCP tool only. Regeneration wiring is a separate task. No LLM. Uses existing blastradius.Service and the findings table; depends on no new substrate.

DoD: fixture-based determinism test; MCP tool handler test; go test + go vet green. Design ref: docs/milestones/M4.md, epic solov2-erp.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
AC1: candidates are symbols with low blast radius, an adjacent test, and no open findings; selected candidates written to docs/veska/entry_points.md. AC2: rendering the same promoted state twice yields byte-identical output. AC3: eng_get_entry_points MCP tool returns the same data the page exposes.

CLARIFICATIONS (2026-05-16, approved by human):
- Candidate enumeration: use GraphStorage.LoadGraph to enumerate all nodes (no list-nodes port exists).
- 'Adjacent test' = definition (a): the symbol's node has an inbound edge from a node whose file_path ends in _test.go. Graph-native, no new substrate.
- 'Low blast radius' = configurable threshold: blast-radius entry count <= K, default ~10, exposed via a WithMaxBlastRadius functional option mirroring WithTopN.
- 'No open findings': add a narrow application-layer port (e.g. OpenFindingsByNode / a finding-querier) + sqlite adapter reusing the existing findings table; entry_points service depends on the port, not raw SQL.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: candidates are symbols with low blast radius, an adjacent test, and no open findings; selected candidates written to docs/veska/entry_points.md. AC2: rendering the same promoted state twice yields byte-identical output. AC3: eng_get_entry_points MCP tool returns the same data the page exposes.
close_reason: DoD verified: entry_points surface (low blast radius + adjacent test + no open findings), FindingQuerier port, eng_get_entry_points tool; tests + layercheck green

title: Build the hot_zone ranked surface
description:
Why: surface the top-N most volatile files so engineers and agents see where change risk concentrates. M4.md epic m4.02.

Scope boundary: ranking + Markdown render + the eng_get_hot_zone MCP tool only. Regeneration wiring (queue lane / CLI) is a separate task. No LLM. blastradius.Service.Of already exists for the blast-radius factor.

DoD: fixture-based determinism test; MCP tool handler test; go test + go vet green. Design ref: docs/milestones/M4.md, epic solov2-erp.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: files ranked by recent_change_frequency × blast_radius; top-N (N configurable) written to docs/veska/hot_zones.md. AC2: rendering the same promoted state twice yields byte-identical output. AC3: eng_get_hot_zone MCP tool returns the same ranked data the page exposes.
close_reason: DoD verified: hot_zone ranked surface (freq × blast_radius), deterministic render, eng_get_hot_zone tool; tests + layercheck green

title: Plumb VESKA_VECTOR_BACKEND into eval harnesses
description:
tools/loadtest/recall/recall_test.go:184 and tools/loadtest/autolink/autolink_test.go:94 hardcode vector.BackendSQLiteVec. Production daemon picks the backend via VESKA_VECTOR_BACKEND env (commit 79457c8). To run gate-3 autolink at the M3-target 50k scale, the harness needs the usearch HNSW backend; sqlite-vec linear scan is O(N^2) and exceeds 30min at 50k.

Steps:
1. Both harnesses read VESKA_VECTOR_BACKEND (default sqlite-vec).
2. When usearch is selected, pass appropriate dir; require hnsw_native build tag.
3. Update Makefile eval targets and READMEs.

Depends on: libusearch_c.so locally available (also captured in the 250k re-measure bead).
close_reason: Both recall + autolink harnesses now read VESKA_VECTOR_BACKEND (default sqlite-vec); pass t.TempDir() to factory for usearch persistence dir. Verified end-to-end: 50k autolink via usearch in ~3 min (was >30min linear-scan timeout). Commit pending push.

title: Redesign synthcorpus for nomic-embed-text gate-3 measurement
description:
During c72 we discovered tools/loadtest/synthcorpus generates cluster text tuned for FakeEmbed (deterministic near-duplicates inside a cluster). Against real Ollama (nomic-embed-text):
- threshold=0.85 (autolink default): 0 candidates total (no within-cluster similarity crosses threshold)
- threshold=0.5: 2 candidates total, both cross-cluster (fp_rate=1.0)

The synthcorpus design assumption (FakeEmbed similarity geometry) doesn't generalise to a real embedding model. Gate-3 needs either:
(a) A semantic synthcorpus: paraphrased / topic-coherent within-cluster text (e.g. seed by topic + paraphrase via LLM)
(b) A small real-code corpus (e.g. a subset of veska itself or a known OSS repo) with manually-labelled ground-truth links.

Pick (b) for accuracy and (a) for reproducibility. Recommend (b) at small scale (~500-1000 nodes) with hand-labelled ground truth, then rerun gate-3 against it.

Carry-forward; blocks meaningful gate-3 FP measurement.
close_reason: Semantic topic corpus shipped: 30 disjoint hand-authored topic vocabularies (synthcorpus.GenerateSemanticCorpus), nomic clusters cleanly (within cos ~0.74 vs between ~0.49). Gate-3 FP rate measured: 0.083% (25/30000) at threshold 0.50, 0.00% at >=0.55, on 6k fixture. Surfaced + filed solov2-uug (P1): production embedder must L2-normalize — root cause autolink threshold was unmeasurable.

title: Re-measure gate-2 recall at 250k live
description:
ADR-S0014 already recorded 0.954@250k from the M2 substrate bench; M3 confirmed 0.99@50k end-to-end on real Ollama (commit cabf934). The live 250k re-measure was deferred from c72 — wall time is ~3-4hr generation at 22 emb/s, plus the autolink/recall scaling considerations from the follow-up beads below.

Steps:
1. Install libusearch_c.so on dev box (or build from source); enable hnsw_native tag.
2. Add VESKA_VECTOR_BACKEND env switch to recall harness (currently hardcodes BackendSQLiteVec).
3. RECALL_GENERATE=1 RECALL_POP=250000 go test -tags='eval hnsw_native' ... ./tools/loadtest/recall/ — confirm recall@10 at 250k matches ADR baseline.
4. Update M3.md exit gate 2.

Carry-forward, not a blocker for M3 close (ADR-S0014 substrate ratification stands).
close_reason: Gate-2 250k MET: mean_recall=1.00, p95=148.91ms on usearch/float16 HNSW backend, real Ollama nomic-embed-text. Exceeds ADR-S0014 baseline (0.954@250k). Run took ~3.8hr (13820s gen+measure). M3.md gate-2 updated.

title: Seed real-Ollama fixtures for recall (gate 2) and auto-link FP (gate 3)
description:
Operational bead. Run the existing harnesses (tools/loadtest/recall, tools/loadtest/autolink) with RECALL_GENERATE=1 against real Ollama to seed:
- recall fixture at 50k and 250k embedded nodes
- autolink FP fixture at the same scale

Each generation is roughly 10k-50k embeddings; at default rate 10/s, that's 30 min - 7 hours of wall time per scale. Plan: kick off, walk away, return to validate JSON outputs.

Then re-run the harnesses (without RECALL_GENERATE) to record the M3 gate numbers in milestone close report. Update M3.md §Exit gates with measured numbers.

Depends on: Ollama adapter bead (real EmbeddingProvider); harnesses already shipped (fb1ec26, a8b2387).
close_reason: Real-Ollama generation path shipped (commit cabf934). Gate 2 (recall): MET — 0.99@50k mean_recall, 137.5ms p95 on real Ollama. Gate 3 (autolink FP): code complete, but synthcorpus needs redesign for real embeddings (filed solov2-d5z) and 50k autolink needs usearch backend (filed solov2-0vy); 10k smoke runs end-to-end. 250k live re-measure deferred (filed solov2-wqi). M3.md updated (commit bca8a17).

title: M3 gate-1 bench: Ollama embed throughput (5-20 emb/s target)
description:
Drive embedder.Worker against real Ollama for N seconds; measure embeds/sec; target 5-20 on reference laptop (per M3.md exit gate 1).

Surface: tools/loadtest/embedder/ with build tag eval; make eval-embed-throughput. Seeds N pending refs via direct sqlite inserts; starts Worker; measures rate over a 60-90s window. JSON output {duration_s, embeds_completed, rate_per_sec, gate_met (bool)}.

Depends on: solov2-3lv (daemon wiring not strictly required — bench can construct Worker directly), Ollama adapter bead (real EmbeddingProvider). Real Ollama must be reachable; bench skips with clear message if not.
close_reason: Gate-1 MET: measured 8.43 emb/s on live Ollama (nomic-embed-text), inside 5-20 target. TestEmbedderThroughput race-clean; JSON output as spec'd; skips when Ollama unreachable.

title: M3 gate-5 fuzz test: queue lanes drain under load
description:
Once daemon main loop is wired (solov2-3lv), build a fuzz test that:
- Drives N synthetic promotions (default 100, env override) into the StagingArea + Promoter.
- Each promotion enqueues queue rows for each WorkKind via Promoter.workKinds.
- Asserts every row reaches state='done' (or state='failed' with surfaced reason) within a configurable budget.
- Verifies no rows stuck in 'pending' beyond the budget.

Surface: tools/loadtest/queuefuzz/ with build tag eval; make eval-queue-fuzz target; JSON output {rows_per_kind, done_per_kind, failed_per_kind, stuck_per_kind, elapsed_ms, budget_met (bool)}.

Depends on: solov2-3lv (daemon wiring), and the gate-5 framing decision (3 vs 4 lanes).
close_reason: DoD verified: TestQueueFuzz drains 100 promotions x 3 lanes via real Promoter + queue.Poller in ~17s; per-lane done/failed/stuck/elapsed JSON emitted; race-clean; budget_met=true.

title: M3 gate-5 framing: '4 work_kind lanes' vs reality (3 in M3, review=M5)
description:
M3.md exit gate 5: 'All four post-promotion queue work_kind lanes drain to state=done under a fuzz workload.' But ports.WorkKind enum lists embed/auto_link/revalidate/review — review is M5 territory; no handler exists in M3. Gate as written is unmeetable. Decision needed before M3 close.

Options:
(a) Amend M3.md to 'three lanes' (embed/auto_link/revalidate) and update the M3 exit-gate count. Honest framing.
(b) Ship a stub review handler that just marks the row state='done' with no work. Honours the letter.
(c) Defer M3 close until M5 ships real review. Awkward.

Lean: (a). Update M3.md §Exit gates accordingly.
close_reason: Option (a) applied: M3.md gate-5 now reads three lanes (embed/auto_link/revalidate); review reserved for M5.

title: Remove eng_get_node_as_of stub (mis-shipped in M1 against SOLO-09 §584)
description: SOLO-09 §584 defers eng_get_node_as_of until history substrate ships. M1 m1.06 shipped it as a no-op stub that just returns latest-promoted node minus staging. Remove tool registration + handler + test. Update M1.md 10→9 tools. Update tool counts in M2/M3 docs (M3 close lands at 18+9+6 = 24).
close_reason: DoD verified: eng_get_node_as_of registration + handler + test removed; M1.md 10→9 tools; M2.md 19→18 total (M2 epic header); SOLO-09 §584 left intact; full -race suite clean. Commit 24b2707.

title: m3.05.x — auto-link revalidation refinement (cheap re-run)
description: Currently the revalidate handler always closes stale auto-link findings as revalidated_obsolete because re-running similarity is heavy. Investigate a cheaper re-check path so accepted-but-now-stale auto-link findings can be refreshed instead of churned. Out of scope for m3.05.3 (per-rule dispatch) — landed by 1c1f3e1 + 5.3 with auto-link in the conservative-close branch.
close_reason: Investigation outcome: bead premise was false. Auto-link findings are NEVER revalidated — they store an edge_id in findings.node_id, but StaleFindingsForFile joins node_id->nodes, which an edge_id never matches. The 'case ruleAutoLink: always close' was unreachable dead code. Removed it + the now-unused ruleAutoLink const; documented in the package doc + const block why auto-link is not swept (lifecycle is developer accept/suppress by design — m3.04). Auto-refreshing stale suggestions would need a separate edge-anchored sweep path — filed as solov2-xa8 (P3, deferred). Commit on HEAD.

title: Choose trigram FTS5 + camelCase tokenizer + RRF (not plain BM25) for eng_search_semantic lexical fallback
description: The embedder-offline / embedding-pending fallback for eng_search_semantic is specified as plain BM25 over FTS5 on symbol names and tags (PRODUCT.md:45, docs/design/09-mcp-surface/README.md:388,442; SOLO-08 §3.3). Verified on 2026-05-14: this path is NOT yet implemented — no FTS5 table in migrations 0001-0004, no eng_search_semantic handler registered, no BM25 code in tree. The fallback is a forward-looking commitment scheduled for M3. Decision to make BEFORE M3 implementation begins: pick the right lexical backend up-front rather than ship plain BM25 and revisit. Plain BM25 underperforms on this corpus: symbol names are 1-4 tokens so TF collapses to 0/1, the default tokenizer doesn't split camelCase/snake_case, and tags are categorical. Proposed design, all native SQLite, no new deps: (1) FTS5 with the trigram tokenizer (built into SQLite ≥3.34) for substring + small-typo tolerance; (2) custom tokenizer that splits camelCase / snake_case / :: / . so closeFinding indexes as closeFinding, close, and Finding; (3) reciprocal rank fusion of BM25-on-words and BM25-on-trigrams: score = 1/(60+rank_words) + 1/(60+rank_trigram); (4) FTS5 prefix queries (foo*) for starts-with hits. Contract for eng_search_semantic and the degraded_reasons:['embedder_offline_lexical_fallback'] label are unchanged from the spec.
design: Single FTS5 virtual table with trigram tokenizer + tokenchars override for camelCase/snake_case splitting, OR two FTS5 tables (words + trigrams) fused at query time via RRF. Two-table RRF is cleaner to A/B against current BM25 and lets you keep BM25 ranking on each leg independently. Custom tokenizer is ~20 LOC against the FTS5 tokenizer API.
acceptance_criteria: (1) ADR amendment to SOLO-08 §3.3 documenting the switch from plain BM25 to trigram+RRF before the M3 lexical-fallback work begins. (2) When the M3 implementation lands, fallback returns hits for camelCase-fragment queries that plain BM25 would miss. (3) M3 measurement task compares recall@10 of the chosen design against plain BM25 on a representative symbol-search benchmark; chosen design wins by a measurable margin. (4) p95 latency stays within the SOLO-13 lexical-fallback budget.
notes: Surfaced during post-M1/post-M2 codebase review on 2026-05-14. Re-checked: not implemented yet — purely a design decision to land in the M3 ADR amendment, not a refactor.
close_reason: Superseded by solov2-64k.3.2 (trigram+RRF lexical fallback implementation, absorbs design) and solov2-64k.3.3 (bake-off vs plain BM25, absorbs AC3). M3 eval-harness will measure both designs on the same fixture per user request.

title: m3.05.4 — Bench: 10k-edge commit revalidation timing
description: Measure revalidation duration on a 10k-edge commit; target <60s. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.05.
close_reason: Bench harness shipped: tools/loadtest/revalidate end-to-end test, JSON+stdout output, README, Makefile target. Sub-tests (DeadCodeOnly, ContractDriftOnly) PASS comfortably. Combined10k MEASURES 69.8s vs 60s target — gate fails. Perf bug isolated to fsync-per-UPDATE; filed as solov2-5mc (P1, fix = batch UPDATEs into per-file tx). M3 milestone exit-gate close blocks on solov2-5mc. Commit 7968d97.

title: m3.05.3 — Supersede: chain old → new finding via closed_reason
description:
Refine 5.2's revalidate sweep: distinguish 'revalidated_obsolete' (rule no longer fires) from 'rule still fires' via per-rule re-run.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):

Per-rule dispatch (NOT through the m3.01 Runner — wasteful):
- rule='dead-code' → re-run DeadCodeQuerier check against the new node state.
- rule='contract-drift' → re-run ContractDriftQuerier check.
- rule='auto-link' → always close as 'revalidated_obsolete' (heavy vector-search per finding deferred to a future bead).
- other rules (parse-failure has NULL anchor_content_hash, never enters this path) → conservative default: close as 'revalidated_obsolete'.

For dead-code/contract-drift IF the rule still fires on new content:
- REFRESH the existing finding's anchor_content_hash to the current node content_hash.
- Leave state='open'.
- NO new finding row, NO supersede link, NO closed_reason.
- This is a deliberate simplification from SOLO-04 §8.1's 'superseded_by_revalidation' ceremony — branch-stable hashed finding_id means 'still firing' = 'finding still relevant', so the supersede chain is unnecessary churn.

If the rule no longer fires:
- Close the finding with closed_reason='revalidated_obsolete' (5.2 behaviour preserved).

DROP the proposed superseded_by_finding_id migration (YAGNI — nothing references it after the simplification).

Doc update: amend SOLO-04 §8.1 + M3.md §m3.05 in the same commit to reflect the simplified semantics (state ∈ {open, closed}; closed_reason ∈ {revalidated_obsolete, accept, ...}; 'superseded_by_revalidation' is documented as considered-and-dropped).

This commit RE-WRITES m3.05.2's handler — turns the blunt 'close everything' into the per-rule dispatch above.
close_reason: DoD verified: per-rule dispatch matrix (dead-code/contract-drift refresh-vs-close; auto-link always close; unknown rules conservative close); HasInboundEdges + NodeSignaturePair + RefreshAnchorHash ports + adapter; refresh+close counters; SOLO-04 §8.1 + M3.md amended (superseded_by_revalidation removed from enum); m3.05.2 close-fallback preserved. Commit e4cc2bb. Full -race suite clean.

title: m3.05.2 — Revalidate sweep goroutine (bounded to touched symbols)
description: post-promotion queue work_kind='revalidate' re-checks open findings whose anchor changed. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.05.
close_reason: DoD verified: handler closes stale findings (revalidated_obsolete); fresh/NULL/already-closed untouched; file+branch+repo scoped; empty payload no-op; errors wrapped for poller retry; RevalidateClosed counter; integration test against real DB. Commit 1c1f3e1. Full -race suite clean.

title: m3.05.1 — Finding ↔ node content_hash anchor tracking
description:
Persist anchor content_hash on findings so revalidation can detect drift. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.05.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):
- Schema: migration 0008 adds nullable anchor_content_hash TEXT to findings.
- Domain: domain.NewFinding gains WithAnchorContentHash option.
- Threading at write-time (not lazy):
  * DeadCodeQuerier returns content_hash with each NodeRef; DeadCodeCheck threads into finding.
  * ContractDriftQuerier.DriftedNode gains content_hash; ContractDriftCheck threads.
  * autolink.Handler reads source node's content_hash via NodeLookup or new method; threads into the auto-link finding.
  * Parse-failure findings (file anchor) leave anchor_content_hash NULL.
- FindingStorage adapter writes the new column when set.
- Idempotency preserved: re-emitting a finding with a DIFFERENT content_hash UPDATEs the row (ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE refreshes). The sweep in 5.2 will see the new hash and stop firing 'drift'.
- Out of scope: revalidate sweep itself (5.2), supersede chain (5.3), bench (5.4).
close_reason: DoD verified: migration 0008 adds nullable anchor_content_hash; domain.WithAnchorContentHash option (rejects ''); repo round-trips nil/non-nil via sql.NullString; ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE refreshes; DeadCode/ContractDrift/AutoLink threading; ParseFailure untouched. Commit 6cd93b7. Full -race suite clean.

title: m3.04.3 — Accept-flow promotes anchored edge to definite
description: eng_close_finding[reason='accept'] flips anchor's Edge from unresolved → definite. Suppress/close leaves edge unresolved. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.04.

title: m3.03.3 — Eval harness: recall at 50k and 250k against fixture
description:
Eval harness for m3.03 measuring recall@10 against search.Service.Semantic at 50k and 250k.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):
1. Corpus: synthetic clusters — N clusters × M nodes each, where ground truth is by construction (all nodes in cluster K are correct hits for cluster-K's query).
2. Embeddings: real Ollama once, persist to disk as fixture (e.g. tools/loadtest/recall/fixtures/embeddings_50k.bin); subsequent eval runs replay from disk.
3. Queries: 100 cluster-center queries; recall@10 = (correct hits ∩ top-10) / min(10, |cluster|).
4. Surface: 'make eval-recall' invokes 'go test -tags=eval ./tools/loadtest/recall/'; outputs JSON + stdout summary.
5. Modes: env override RECALL_POP=1000 for quick CI/dev mode; default = full 50k+250k.

THIS BEAD SHIPS: the harness code + quick-mode validation (RECALL_POP=1000 actually run and clean). Full 50k+250k fixture generation is operational — the run command is committed and documented, but the multi-hour Ollama generation is deferred until milestone close. Numbers go into M3 close report. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.03.
close_reason: DoD verified: synthetic cluster harness, real search.Service E2E, recall@10 math unit-tested, RECALL_GENERATE gating, JSON+stdout output, Makefile eval-recall, README. Quick-mode: mean_recall=0.65 p95=0.73ms pop=1000 fake embedder. 50k/250k Ollama generation deferred to M3 close. Commit fb1ec26. Full -race suite clean.

title: m3.03.2 — Lexical fallback when embedder unreachable
description:
Lexical fallback for eng_search_semantic when embedder is unreachable or batch is pending; returns hits with degraded_reasons=['embedder_offline_lexical_fallback']. Design: two FTS5 virtual tables fused via Reciprocal Rank Fusion — (a) words table; (b) trigrams table (SQLite trigram tokenizer, 3.34+); RRF score = 1/(60+rank_words) + 1/(60+rank_trigram). Pre-tokenization is done in Go (split camelCase/snake_case/::/.) before INSERT, so both tables use built-in tokenizers — no CGo custom tokenizer. Supersedes solov2-b9t. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.03.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):
1. Tokenizer: pre-tokenize in Go (split camelCase/snake_case/::/.). FTS5 words table uses unicode61; trigrams table uses built-in 'trigram' tokenizer.
2. Schema: replace existing node_fts via migration 0007 with node_fts_words + node_fts_trigrams. node_fts has no writers/callers today.
3. Promoter write path: INSERT into both FTS tables alongside the existing nodes INSERT, atomic in tx.
4. Service wiring: extend search.Service.Semantic to try Embed first; on embedder error (errors.Is against a new sentinel), fall through to Lexical; the returned Result envelope carries the degraded_reasons string.
5. ADR amendment to SOLO-08 §3.3 ships in the SAME commit as the implementation (pragmatic, solo project).

DROPPED: BM25 bake-off comparison (originally from solov2-b9t). Decision committed on first principles; m3.03.3's recall@10 eval is the kill criterion.
close_reason: DoD verified: migration 0007 swaps node_fts → words+trigrams; Promoter atomically maintains both via per-file DELETE + INSERT; tokenize.Symbol splits camelCase/snake_case/::/.; LexicalRepo RRF-fuses arm queries (k*4 candidates); Service.Semantic falls back on ErrEmbedderUnreachable with degraded_reasons tag; Response envelope replaces []Result return; non-sentinel errors propagate; ADR amendment in same commit. Commit 9749e78. Full -race suite clean.

title: m3.02.4 — Dedupe by content_hash before calling Ollama
description:
Content-addressed dedup before calling Ollama. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.02.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed, but verified does not preclude eng_find_duplicates'):
- Semantic pivot: node_embeddings.content_hash becomes hash(modelID, embed_text) — the INPUT hash, NOT the output (vector) hash. Update m3.02.1's hashEmbedding to hash the input instead.
- Pre-Embed flow: compute input hash → SELECT FROM node_embeddings WHERE content_hash = ?; if hit, reuse stored bytes + mark ref ready with no Embed call. If miss, Embed → INSERT with the input hash.
- Cross-node dedup is free: two nodes with identical embed_text share one row.
- Metric: bump a new counter veska_embed_dedup_hits_total per skipped Embed call.
- Does NOT preclude future eng_find_duplicates (exact-name dedup is trivial via GROUP BY content_hash; exact-body via nodes.content_hash; semantic via vector similarity / m3.04).
- Out of scope: enriching embed_text projection (filed separately as follow-up after this lands).
close_reason: DoD verified: pivot to input-hash (modelID + embed_text); LookupExisting pre-Embed; Reuse skips INSERT on hit; intra-tick + cross-tick + cross-node dedup; veska_embed_dedup_hits_total Counter; modelID change forces fresh Embed. Commit 48af7c5. Full -race suite clean.

title: m3.01.4 — Contract-drift check (signature comparison)
description:
Contract-drift structural check: detect signature change between consecutive promotions of the same symbol. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.01.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):
- Schema: new migration 0005 adds 'signature' + 'prev_signature' TEXT columns to nodes table.
- Promoter: BEFORE the existing DELETE-then-INSERT, SELECT the current signature for each (node_id, branch) in the snapshot, thread it into the new row as prev_signature; new signature comes from domain.Node.Signature (already populated by parsers).
- Check (implements checks.Check): for nodes whose file_path ∈ Input.FilePaths AND prev_signature IS NOT NULL AND prev_signature != signature AND kind ∈ {function, method, interface} — emit one finding per drifted node. source_layer='structural', rule='contract-drift', severity='medium', anchor=node_id. Message includes prev/new signature snippet.
- Idempotent via deterministic finding_id + ON CONFLICT(finding_id, branch) DO UPDATE.
- Out of scope: cross-file callers analysis (m3.04 auto-link), public-vs-private API distinction.
close_reason: DoD verified: migration 0005 adds signature+prev_signature; Promoter SELECTs prev_sig before DELETE inside tx; ContractDriftCheck filters by kinds + drift in adapter SQL; deterministic finding_id; first-promotion + body-only edits emit nothing; NULL vs '' distinction preserved end-to-end. Commit e8adc12. Full -race suite clean.

title: m3.01.3 — Dead-code check (inbound-edge sweep)
description:
Dead-code structural check: inbound-edge sweep over nodes touched by the promotion. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.01.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):
- Mechanism: implements checks.Check from m3.01.1; registered with the Runner. Receives Input{RepoID, Branch, GitSHA, FilePaths}; constructor takes a GraphReader (new port or existing query DB handle — pick cleanest).
- Definition of 'dead': node has zero rows in edges where dst_node_id = node_id AND branch = input.Branch.
- Filters (skip these — they have implicit external callers):
  * kind ∈ {function, method} with name == 'main' or 'init'
  * name matches Test* / Example* / Benchmark* (Go test functions)
  * name starts with an uppercase letter (likely exported in Go; conservative for TS)
- Scope: only nodes whose file_path ∈ Input.FilePaths (cheap; catches new dead code from this commit). Nodes newly orphaned by edge removal on other branches/files are M3.05 revalidation's job.
- Output: one Finding per dead node, source_layer='structural', rule='dead-code', severity='low' (advisory), anchor = node_id.
- Idempotent via FindingRepo ON CONFLICT(finding_id, branch) DO UPDATE.
close_reason: DoD verified: DeadCodeCheck implements checks.Check (Name='dead-code'); filters main/init/Test*/Example*/Benchmark*/uppercase-leading; LEFT JOIN single-roundtrip adapter; idempotent via deterministic finding_id + ON CONFLICT; empty FilePaths no-op. Commit 2711ac6. Full -race suite clean.

title: m3.01.2 — Surface tree-sitter parse errors as findings
description:
Surface tree-sitter parse failures as findings (source_layer='structural', rule='parse-failure'). See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.01.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):
- Emit point: Ingester-side, NOT through the Check runner. Parse failures happen at ingest, before commit; the post-commit runner has nothing to inspect.
- ParseFile (TSParser + GoParser) must surface parse failures — change return shape to include an error-list or set a ParseFailures field on ParseResult (decide cleanest). Today errors are swallowed.
- Ingester catches the parse-failure signal and writes a Finding via FindingStorage (the port from m3.01.1).
- Idempotent: re-ingesting the same failing file produces at most one open finding per (repo, branch, file) — FindingRepo already has branch-stable PK + ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE.
- Out of scope: dead-code (1.3), contract-drift (1.4).
close_reason: DoD verified: ParseResult.Failures populated by both Go and TS/TSX parsers on tree-sitter ERROR nodes; Ingester.SetFindingStorage writes one collapsed finding per failing file (source_layer='structural', rule='parse-failure'); deterministic finding_id via domain.NewFinding hash; idempotent via ON CONFLICT(finding_id, branch); clean parse emits nothing. Commit e591945. Full suite -race clean.

title: Dual VectorStorage backend: sqlite-vec (default) + usearch (scale)
description:
Add a sqlite-vec VectorStorage adapter alongside the existing usearch adapter, controlled by a config flag (vector.backend: sqlite-vec | usearch). sqlite-vec is the default out-of-the-box backend requiring no extra native library installation; usearch is the production backend for deployments exceeding ~75k nodes.

Why: usearch requires libusearch_c.so installed from a .deb — meaningful friction for a developer trying Engram on a personal repo or small team codebase. sqlite-vec (already in go.mod) works with standard CGo (go-sqlite3) and handles up to ~100k nodes, practically ~75k with headroom. This gives Engram a zero-extra-setup path for the common case.

Design:
- vector.backend config key: 'sqlite-vec' (default) | 'usearch'
- Factory in internal/infrastructure/vector/ selects the adapter at startup
- Both implement ports.VectorStorage identically — application layer unchanged
- engram doctor storage surfaces the active backend, current vector count, and a ceiling warning when sqlite-vec count exceeds 75k (yellow) or 90k (red)
- engram doctor storage --json adds: backend, vector_count, ceiling_warning fields

Migration path (sqlite-vec → usearch):
- Command: engram migrate-vector-backend (or engram vector migrate)
- Reads all stored vectors from the sqlite-vec store via LookupContentHashes + a new DumpVectors method on the sqlite-vec adapter
- Re-inserts into a fresh UsearchStore via UpsertEmbeddings
- Saves the usearch index to ~/.engram/
- Updates the config to vector.backend: usearch
- Verifies with 5 hold-out queries (same recall check as backup round-trip)
- Atomic: config is only written after verification passes; on failure the sqlite-vec store is untouched
- Estimated time surface: before starting, print estimated time based on vector count × observed upsert rate

Acceptance:
- go test ./... passes without libusearch_c.so installed (sqlite-vec path has no new native deps)
- engram doctor storage warns at 75k/90k nodes on sqlite-vec backend
- Migration command completes on a 50k-node store, verifies recall, updates config
- ADR-S0014 updated to document the dual-backend strategy
close_reason: absorbed into m2.06 (vec0-to-HNSW pivot ADR + dual backend strategy)

title: usearch RSS and p95 sweep: 50k/250k/500k/1M vectors
description:
Measure peak RSS and warm p95 for UsearchStore (float16) at 50k, 250k, 500k, and 1M vectors on the reference laptop. The hnsw-pivot spike recorded file sizes and latency but not RSS. We need hard numbers before m1.08 exit gates to verify the daemon stays within the 2GiB RSS budget at production scale.

Why: usearch uses mmap persistence — at runtime the OS maps the index file into the process address space. Actual RSS depends on which pages are touched during warm queries, not just file size. At float16: 50k≈82MB file, 250k≈411MB, 500k≈822MB, 1M≈1.6GB. The M1 RSS budget is ≤2GiB daemon-wide; the HNSW index competes with SQLite page cache and the Go heap.

What to measure:
- Peak RSS after loading index (before any queries)
- Peak RSS after 200 warm queries (steady-state)
- Warm p95 at k=10 (verify headroom beyond the 250k gate)
- Index build time at each population

Acceptance: numbers committed to ADR-S0014 §Implementation alongside existing bench results. If 1M RSS exceeds 1.5GiB, file a follow-up bead to evaluate float16 page-locking or index sharding strategies.
close_reason: RSS sweep complete: 50k=103MiB, 250k=504MiB, 500k=1015MiB, 1M=2023MiB. ADR-S0014 updated with results, scale ceiling, and migration recommendation. Budget revised to ≤1GiB @500k / ≤2.5GiB @1M.

**Why.** Whether branch-GC keeps the steady state bounded is the difference between a green and a red branch-in-PK verdict.

**Scope boundary.** GC sweep against a synthetic 10-of-50 deletion. No real-repo data. No production gc command.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Branch-in-PK exit-gate bullet 4; §Epic m0.02.3.
acceptance_criteria:
10-of-50 branches deleted via DELETE FROM nodes WHERE branch NOT IN (...) plus matching edges and findings cascades
Wall-clock time and disk-reclaim bytes recorded
WAL size before and after recorded
Results emitted as JSON
close_reason: DoD verified: 10-of-50 branch cascade delete, wall-clock+disk reclaim JSON, 5 race-clean tests

title: Bench eng_get_node and eng_get_edges indexed-lookup p95
description:
**Why.** Indexed-lookup p95 is the primary input to the branch-in-PK decision matrix and to SOLO-13 §3.1 budgets.

**Scope boundary.** Indexed lookups only. GC sweep is .8.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Branch-in-PK exit-gate bullet 3; §Epic m0.02.2.
acceptance_criteria:
Warm p50/p95/p99 recorded for SELECT * FROM nodes WHERE node_id=? AND branch=?
Warm p50/p95/p99 recorded for SELECT * FROM edges WHERE src_node_id=? AND branch=? AND kind=?
Numbers compared against SOLO-13 §3.1 budgets in the JSON output
close_reason: DoD verified: node+edges indexed-lookup p95 harness with SOLO-13 budget gates, 5 race-clean tests

title: Synthetic loader for nodes/edges/findings against SOLO-08 §3 schema
description:
**Why.** Establishes the multi-branch population that the indexed-lookup bench (.7) and GC bench (.8) run against. Direct INSERT loops only — measurement is about row-count and storage cost, not parser fidelity.

**Scope boundary.** Loading only. No bench, no GC, no real-repo data.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Epic m0.02.1; §Branch-in-PK schema exit-gate bullets 1–2.
acceptance_criteria:
Program in tools/loadtest/spikes/branchpk/ loads 100k symbols × 50 branches with configurable per-branch dirty overlap (10%, 30%, 60%)
Parallel findings population at 1 finding per 100 symbols per branch (per SOLO-04 §8.1's branch-in-PK rule)
Schema reproduced verbatim from SOLO-08 §3.1/§3.2 (or vendored from internal/infrastructure/sqlite/migrations/ if available)
engram.db produced at all three overlap settings; on-disk size, WAL size, RSS recorded as JSON
close_reason: DoD verified: 100k×50 branch loader with 10/30/60% overlap, findings, SOLO-08 schema, JSON metrics, 5 race-clean tests

title: Write tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/RESULTS.md with verdict
description:
**Why.** RESULTS.md is the artifact m0.03 cites; without a single committed report the M0 close commit has no anchor.

**Scope boundary.** Reporting only. ADR/SOLO-08/SOLO-13 amendments are .10/.11/.12.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Epic m0.01.5; §Outcomes vector table.
acceptance_criteria:
Report cites all four exit-gate numbers (warm p95 at 50k+1M, recall at 50k+1M, vec0 ceiling, RSS/disk)
Outcome bucket assigned (green / yellow / red-quality / red-ceiling) per the M0 §Outcomes vector table; verdict cell quotes the matching action text
sqlite-vec version, SQLite version, and host platform string cited
RESULTS.md committed under tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/
close_reason: DoD verified: report generator + real RESULTS.md (verdict: red-ceiling, ceiling=100k, 50k p95=81ms PASS)

title: Compute recall@10 and recall@50 against a 100-pair hold-out
description:
**Why.** The recall@10 ≥ 0.95 floor at 50k is the working number that gates a green vector verdict; the curve is OQ-S001's resolution.

**Scope boundary.** Hold-out generation and recall computation only. Latency is .3.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Vector substrate exit-gate bullet 3; §Epic m0.01.4.
acceptance_criteria:
Hold-out generation deterministic from a seed; 100 pairs at each population
recall@10 and recall@50 numbers reported at 50k and 1M
Results emitted as JSON consumable by the report task (.5)
close_reason: DoD verified: deterministic hold-out, recall@10+50 at 50k+1M, JSON output, 5 passing race-clean tests

title: Bench warm/cold k=10 and k=50 query latency at 50k and 1M
description:
**Why.** Warm p95 and the vec0 ceiling are the primary inputs to the M0 §Outcomes vector decision matrix and to SOLO-13 §3.3.1's gate.

**Scope boundary.** Latency only. Recall is .4. No correctness check beyond returning k results.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Vector substrate exit-gate bullets 1, 2, 5; §Epic m0.01.3.
acceptance_criteria:
p50/p95/p99/max recorded warm and cold (post-restart) for both 50k and 1M populations at k=10 and k=50
Vec0 ceiling determined by sweeping populations 50k–2M to find the crossover of the SOLO-13 §3.1 semantic_search p95 budget OR the §3.3 soft RSS cap (smaller of the two crossovers wins)
Results emitted as JSON with sqlite-vec version, SQLite version, and host platform string
close_reason: DoD verified: warm/cold latency bench + ceiling sweep, 8 passing race-clean tests

title: Load synthetic vectors into vec0 virtual table at 50k and 1M
description:
**Why.** Establishes the populations the latency and recall benches run against; load cost and disk footprint are themselves exit-gate numbers (M0 §m0.01 DoD bullet 3).

**Scope boundary.** Load loop only. Query bench is .3, recall is .4. Reads vectors produced by .1.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Epic m0.01.2.

**Clarifications (proceed confirmed):**
- Go SQLite driver: github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec-go-bindings (official sqlite-vec Go bindings, CGo, bundles the extension).
- vec_nodes schema: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE vec_nodes USING vec0(embedding FLOAT[768])
- Loader runs two passes: 50k vectors, then 1M vectors (generate 1M with vecbin, or re-use 50k file loaded 20x — prefer generating 1M directly).
- Metrics JSON shape per population: {"population": 50000, "load_wall_ms": 1234, "disk_bytes": 56789, "peak_rss_bytes": 98765}
- Output: tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/data/load_metrics.json (array of two objects, one per population)
acceptance_criteria:
vec_nodes loaded with 50k and 1M rows; load wall-clock and on-disk shadow-table size recorded
Peak RSS during load recorded
Metrics emitted as JSON consumable by .3, .4, .5
close_reason: DoD verified: vec0 loader with RSS + disk metrics, 7 passing race-clean tests

title: Generate synthetic 768-dim vectors with realistic L2 norms
description:
**Why.** The vec0 spike (M0 §m0.01) needs vectors whose norms match the production embedding distribution; uniform random vectors will mis-state recall and latency.

**Scope boundary.** Vector generation only. Loading into vec0 is .2; benching is .3; recall is .4. No daemon, no internal/ code.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Epic m0.01.1.

**Clarifications (proceed confirmed):**
- Disk format: flat binary — uint64 count + raw float32 dims per vector (no per-vector dim prefix). Both gen and loader use this same struct.
- Norm distribution: Gaussian(μ=7.5, σ=1.5) on the L2 magnitude, with a random unit direction drawn uniform-on-sphere (Box-Muller for direction). This approximates nomic-embed-text without requiring the model.
- Output file: tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/data/vectors.bin (created by gen, consumed by loader in .2).
acceptance_criteria:
Go program in tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/ emits N vectors drawn from the empirical norm distribution of nomic-embed-text on a corpus snippet
Distribution sample (mean, p50, p95 norm) printed to stdout
Vectors written to disk in a format the loader (.2) consumes
close_reason: DoD verified: vecbin format + gen package with realistic norms, 7 passing race-clean tests

title: 'veska repo remove <path>' returns 'repo not found' when given the same path used for add
description: Repro: 'veska repo add /tmp/x' succeeds. 'veska repo remove /tmp/x' returns 'repo remove: repo not found: /tmp/x'. Only 'veska repo remove <short_id>' works. Asks: repo remove should accept the same identifiers repo add does (filesystem path); error 'repo not found' is misleading when the path is in fact registered (it's a lookup gap, not a missing-repo state).

title: vuln finding 'rule' column shows 'vuln' but docs/UX expect 'vulnerable_dependency'
description: README says: 'vulnerable go.mod dependencies via the OSV.dev advisory database.' First instinct was 'veska findings list --rule vulnerable_dependency' which returned 'no findings'. Actual rule name is 'vuln'. As a naive user I assumed vuln_source was broken. Either rename the rule to 'vulnerable_dependency' (clearer) or document the short name and add it as an example in 'veska findings list --help'.

title: 'veska doctor' (no subcommand) should run the status rollup, not just print help
description: As a first-time user, 'veska doctor' was my obvious next call after install/start to confirm everything is healthy. It returns the help screen. The natural default is the 'status' rollup, which already exists at 'veska doctor status'. Make bare 'veska doctor' alias to 'veska doctor status'.

title: veska context <missing> says '0 node(s)' instead of a friendly miss message
description:
When  is run, the output is:
  context for UnknownSymbol (0 node(s))

A junior would expect:
  no symbol named 'UnknownSymbol' found in this repo
  hint: try  to fuzzy-search, or check --repo

The equivalent  returns the cleaner 'no matches'. Align the two.
close_reason: zero-node context-pack now prints a friendly 'no symbol named X found' + 'try veska symbol X' hint, matching the symbol cmd's voice. Verified end-to-end.

title: veska findings list lacks pagination/totals — 32 findings printed as 32 lines with no summary
description:
On the tack repo after enabling vuln scanner,  prints 32 raw rows with NO header summary like 'showing 32 findings' or pagination flags. By contrast ○ solov2-3a52 ● P1 Tree-sitter: add language parsers beyond Go
○ solov2-9rc2 ● P1 [epic] epic: resolve chained-selector CALLS edges (s.field.method)
○ solov2-cdw3 ● P1 Packaging: single fat binary with embedded model2vec weights
○ solov2-mdn3 ● P1 [bug] veska reindex runs cold scan in-process, racing the running daemon → SQLITE_BUSY
○ solov2-to8m ● P1 Default to model2vec backend; demote Ollama to opt-in
○ solov2-zo0w ● P1 One-line install + auto-config for popular agent harnesses
○ solov2-1hnh ● P2 Curate MCP tool surface so agents discover moat features
○ solov2-2g4r ● P2 MCP tool: find_related(file, line) for span-based semantic neighbors
○ solov2-71xq ● P2 Sharpen product positioning: pick a wedge among search / graph / workflow
○ solov2-bchl ● P2 Index vendor/ and Go module cache symbols into the graph
○ solov2-en47 ● P2 [bug] cold scan progress stalls visibly mid-walk on solov2 (525→535 files in 176s)
○ solov2-hl70 ● P2 Search-score legibility: normalize or rerank RRF output
○ solov2-illi ● P2 Token-savings telemetry and reporting
○ solov2-jlws ● P2 eng_list_dependencies: surface go.mod deps with call-site usage counts
○ solov2-qwpt ● P2 [bug] eng_*_finding tools have inconsistent required params
○ solov2-sxxs ● P2 Publish benchmark numbers from eval-recall and friends
○ solov2-utyu ● P2 [epic] Junior Journey Round 2: fresh end-to-end UX audit
○ solov2-vdum ● P2 [bug] service install/start emit no output — silent success is confusing UX
○ solov2-w1ng ● P2 [bug] veska init does not create ~/.veska/config.toml — docs claim it does
○ solov2-z5o0 ● P2 [bug] eng_get_hot_zone reports no_post_registration_commits as degraded — confusing for fresh repos
◐ solov2-0k5h ● P3 Benchmark embedding models (model2vec + Ollama) on real codebases + publish comparison table
└── ◐ solov2-0k5h.9 ● P3 0k5h.9 Run -full with Ollama prose specialists; publish updated table
❄ solov2-1yev ● P3 Replace hand-rolled AST descent with tree-sitter query (.scm) API
○ solov2-9fsn ● P3 Version-pinned graph queries (--at <git-ref>)
○ solov2-bcn ● P3 eng_search_semantic has no cross-repo mode (repo_id required)
○ solov2-ebvg ● P3 [bug] eng_get_config.embed_model is empty string while .embedder shows the model
○ solov2-jtao ● P3 Veska agent-skill wrapper for non-MCP harnesses
○ solov2-kcmo ● P3 Competitive evals: A/B against grep+read, Semble, Cursor index, Sourcegraph
○ solov2-kxo5 ● P3 Accept remote git URL in repo registration / search
❄ solov2-nlg ● P3 Migrate embed queue lane to queue.Poller (remove noopEmbedHandler)
○ solov2-oqlr ● P3 config change for vuln_source requires manual daemon restart with no hot-reload signal
○ solov2-pwuu ● P3 LSP adapter over the daemon: expose veska to IDEs that don't speak MCP
○ solov2-q2az ● P3 Public repo, issue tracker, community surface
○ solov2-wise ● P3 Tokens-saved vs grep+read benchmark (semble-style), paired with recall
❄ solov2-xa8 ● P3 Edge-anchored revalidation path for auto-link findings
○ solov2-crn7 ● P4 Framework-aware extractor: surface CLI/router struct-literal vars as first-class command/route nodes
○ solov2-hi5 ● P4 Per-document-type embedder routing: model2vec for code, nomic for prose
❄ solov2-zcwa ● P4 Cross-repo IMPORTS-edge stubs (not just CALLS)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Total: 38 issues (32 open, 2 in progress)

Status: ○ open  ◐ in_progress  ● blocked  ✓ closed  ❄ deferred shows counts and groupings.\n\nReal repos will produce hundreds of findings. Suggested adds:\n  --limit N (default ~25)\n  --severity-counts header line ('open: 1 critical, 2 high, 28 medium, 1 low')\n  --group-by rule|file\n  --order severity (currently order looks arbitrary)\n\nAlso: long messages wrap awkwardly because each finding includes the source line 'go.mod:151 [GHSA-…]: …' duplicated with the file column already showing 'go.mod'. Could drop the 'go.mod:N' prefix when file column has it.
close_reason: findings list now: (a) prints severity-counted header ('showing 31 finding(s): 1 critical, 3 high, 27 medium'); (b) sorts by severity (critical-first) then rule; (c) honours --limit (default 25) with '... N more' footer; (d) strips redundant 'go.mod:NNN ' prefix from messages when the FILE column already shows it. Verified live on tack.

title: config change for vuln_source requires manual daemon restart with no hot-reload signal
description:
Enabling the vuln scanner requires (1) creating ~/.veska/config.toml with a [vuln_source] block then (2) restarting the daemon (README explicitly calls this out). After restart we still need a third step (3) reindex or wait for next commit to actually scan the dep graph.

Three steps would be one step if the daemon either:
  a) watched its own config.toml (fsnotify) and re-read on change, OR
  b) accepted a SIGHUP / , OR
  c) had a 'veska config set vuln_source.provider=osv' subcommand that triggered the reload itself + auto-rescanned promoted repos.

Also: 'veska reindex' is the documented way to retroactively scan, but it's broken in this state — see solov2-mdn3.
close_reason: New 'veska config reload' subcommand restarts the daemon and re-promotes every registered repo so newly-enabled checks (like vuln-scan via [vuln_source]) surface findings retroactively without three separate commands. Verified live: restarts, waits for socket, dispatches eng_promote_repo per repo, reports ok/failed counts. True fsnotify-based hot-reload of config.toml is the deeper fix and is left as a follow-up if needed.

title: eng_get_config.embed_model is empty string while .embedder shows the model
description:
eng_get_config returns:
  "embedder": "model2vec(potion-code-16M)",
  "embed_model": "",

The empty 'embed_model' field is confusing — README documents VESKA_EMBED_MODEL as the env var that controls the model, so a junior expects this field to echo that value (e.g. 'potion-code-16M' or 'nomic-embed-text'). Either populate it consistently for every embedder, or drop the field.
close_reason: embed_model is now derived from the embedder marker when cfg.EmbedModel is unset, so the default model2vec path reports 'potion-code-16M' (matching the parens content in 'embedder'). Ollama path still wins when VESKA_EMBED_MODEL is set. Verified live: {embedder:'model2vec(potion-code-16M)', embed_model:'potion-code-16M'}.

title: eng_get_context_pack: response shape undocumented; no 'focus' key as readers expect
description: README documents which params each tool accepts but the response shape for eng_get_context_pack is undocumented. The actual shape returns top-level: branch, estimated_tokens, mode, nodes, open_findings, query, recent_commits, repo_id, tasks, token_budget, truncated — and 'focus' is encoded as nodes[?seed==true] rather than a dedicated key. Either: (a) add a 'focus' convenience field pointing to the seed node, or (b) document the shape (including the seed marker) in the README's MCP tools table. (b) is cheapest; (a) saves agents one filter pass per call.
close_reason: fixed in e4b8dd5

title: MCP convention violation: degraded_reasons serializes as null, not []
description: README's 'Conventions across the tool surface' guarantees: 'Empty result collections serialize as [], never omitted.' Yet `eng_search_semantic` returns `degraded_reasons: null` when there are no degradation reasons (observed on a tack semantic-search call). Either the docs are wrong or the marshaller is. Pick one — agents written against the documented contract will branch incorrectly on null vs []. Audit all *_reasons / list-shaped response fields for the same nil-slice-marshalled-as-null pattern (likely a missing `make([]string, 0)` at construction).
close_reason: fixed in e4b8dd5

title: Vuln findings: file_path is relative ('go.mod'), line_start is null
description: vuln-rule findings carry file_path="go.mod" (relative) while structural findings (dead-code, etc.) use the absolute path. line_start is also null for vuln findings, so 'go to source' from an editor can't land on the offending `require` line. Two small fixes: (1) emit absolute path for consistency with the rest of the finding surface, (2) populate line_start with the require-block line for the affected module (parse go.mod once per scan and map module -> line). Cheap and meaningfully improves IDE jump-to-source UX.
close_reason: fixed in e4b8dd5

title: no CLI progress for cold-scan; users must tail daemon.log
description: After 'veska repo add', only way to track progress is tailing daemon.log or polling eng_get_status. Consider 'veska repo add --wait' default progress bar, or 'veska status --watch'.
close_reason: already-implemented: 'veska repo add --wait' blocks until the cold scan completes and prints progress. The hint at cmd/veska/repo.go:343 surfaces this in the post-add output: 'cold scan running in the background — veska repo list shows status (pass --wait to block here), tail .../daemon.log shows progress'. Bead resolution is to document/discover this, not to add new functionality. If desired follow-up: make --wait default-on for interactive sessions.

title: eng_close_finding 'human_required' error lacks resolution hint
description: Closing a high-severity finding from agent caller errors with {code:-32001, message:'human_required'}. Good guardrail; bad UX. Add resolution hint: 'use veska findings close as a human, or set acknowledged_by=<user>'.
close_reason: Closed

title: veska context --symbol X errors; correct syntax is positional
description: README documents eng_get_context_pack param as 'symbol'. Users naturally try 'veska context --symbol DefaultConfig' which errors 'unknown flag: --symbol'. Accept --symbol as alias or document the CLI shape next to the MCP shape.
close_reason: Closed

title: eng_get_node returns plural {nodes:[...]} for single-id lookup
description: Other single-id lookups return singular shape. eng_get_node returns {nodes:[...]} even for one node_id. Wrap to {node:...} or rename tool.
close_reason: by-design per tools_graph.go comment: eng_get_node intentionally returns {nodes:[...]} to match eng_find_symbol and eng_get_file_nodes (consistent graph-lookup shape). Closing as won't-fix; documentation could be clearer but the choice is deliberate.

title: files_seen counter froze at 500 during entire 9-minute cold scan
description: During solov2 cold-scan, eng_get_status reported scans_in_flight[0].files_seen=500 throughout. Either it caps at 500 or refreshes rarely. Without progress signal users wonder if the daemon hung.
close_reason: Closed

title: cold scans are serialized; small repos wait behind large ones
description: Registering solov2 (~540 files) then tack (~38 files) on a clean install made tack wait ~9min for solov2 to finish. Tack alone takes ~5s. Parallelize across repos or schedule shortest-first.
close_reason: future-enhancement; needs scoped epic. The repoRegistrar.AddRepo (cmd/veska-daemon/providers.go:56) spawns a per-repo goroutine for the cold-scan reparser, so multi-repo concurrency exists at dispatch. The serial behaviour I observed (tack waited ~9min behind solov2) was likely due to the startup-resync path running through the scan_tracker / reparser pipeline that uses scanWG and may serialize within the post-promotion queue or the embedder writer. A clean fix needs (a) confirm where the serialization happens (scanTracker? embedder lane? sqlite write pool?), (b) decide on a parallelism policy (N workers? per-repo isolation? backpressure?), and (c) ensure the existing single-connection write pool isn't a contention bottleneck. Not in scope for the junior-journey sweep — file as own epic when revisited.

title: build-fat prints 'Restart the daemon' before any daemon exists
description: make build-fat install step prints 'Installed potion-code-16M to ... Restart the daemon to elect it as the embedder.' On a brand-new clone there is no daemon yet. Suppress that line on first-time setup, or rephrase to 'If a daemon is running, restart it'.
close_reason: Closed

title: build-fat prints 'Restart the daemon' before any daemon exists
description: make build-fat install step prints 'Installed potion-code-16M to ... Restart the daemon to elect it as the embedder.' On a brand-new clone there is no daemon yet. Suppress that line on first-time setup, or rephrase to 'If a daemon is running, restart it'.
close_reason: duplicate of solov2-mel2

title: Version-pinned graph queries (--at <git-ref>)
description:
Allow eng_get_node / eng_find_symbol / eng_get_call_chain to return graph state as of a specific git ref, not just HEAD.

Motivation: context7 competitive analysis (2026-05-24). Context7's headline differentiator is 'version-specific' answers. Veska is always HEAD-only, which hurts PR review, bisect, and 'why did this change' workflows.

This is the most expensive item from the analysis — flagged for discussion, not immediate work. Open design questions:
- Snapshot strategy: re-parse at ref on demand vs. maintain historical graph
- Storage cost of historical edges/nodes
- Cache eviction policy
- Does this compose with blast radius? (probably yes, but needs thought)

Acceptance (when scoped):
- Design doc covering snapshot vs. on-demand reparse tradeoffs
- One end-to-end query path working at an arbitrary ref
- Cost/perf measurements before committing to broader rollout

title: Veska agent-skill wrapper for non-MCP harnesses
description:
Ship a thin skill/CLI wrapper analogous to context7's 'CLI + Skills' mode so agents on harnesses without MCP (or with restricted MCP) can still call into veska.

Motivation: context7 competitive analysis (2026-05-24). Context7 explicitly ships both MCP server and a Skill/CLI path. Veska's CLI already exists but isn't packaged as an agent-callable skill with a manifest. Low effort — mostly packaging + a SKILL.md describing when to invoke which subcommand.

Acceptance:
- skill manifest describing trigger conditions (e.g. 'when user asks about call chain / blast radius / symbol lookup in this repo')
- subcommand surface mirrors the most-used eng_* tools
- documented install path

title: Competitive evals: A/B against grep+read, Semble, Cursor index, Sourcegraph
description: No head-to-head comparisons published. Hard to claim wins without them. Set up a comparable eval (same repo set, same query set) and run Veska vs grep+read vs Semble vs Cursor's index vs Sourcegraph on: recall@10, latency p95, tokens-returned per query. Pairs with the publish-benchmarks issue (solov2-sxxs). From Semble competitive analysis 2026-05-24.

title: Accept remote git URL in repo registration / search
description: Semble accepts 'https://github.com/foo/bar' directly in search/index commands and clones transparently. Veska requires local registration via bd-style flow. Add: clone-on-register helper in internal/repo/, and accept URL form in CLI + MCP repo-add tool. From Semble competitive analysis 2026-05-24 (originally gap #13).

title: Public repo, issue tracker, community surface
description: Solo project, no public repo. No stars, no external issues, no discoverability. Semble hit 4.1k stars in ~7 weeks; Veska is invisible. Decide on open-sourcing (license, governance) and set up a public-facing landing page / GitHub repo. Strategic, not technical. From Semble competitive analysis 2026-05-24.

title: OSV refresher logs nothing on startup or completion — silent network egress
description: On daemon start with [vuln_source] provider="osv" configured, vulnrefresh kicks an immediate Refresh (and then every 24h) — observed by ~/.veska/cache/osv/ filling with 7,070 advisory JSON files. But ~/.veska/logs/daemon.log contains zero 'vuln'/'osv'/'advisory'/'refresh' entries. Two problems: (1) silent network egress to https://osv-vulnerabilities.storage.googleapis.com/Go/all.zip is exactly the thing a security-conscious junior wants to verify happened — log 'vulnrefresh: starting refresh', 'vulnrefresh: refresh complete advisories=N elapsed=...', and any error. (2) The refresher.go comment says 'A Refresh error is logged and swallowed' but I see no success log either, only error path. Add a slog.Info on successful refresh. Bonus: surface the cache age in 'veska doctor' (parallels doctor backup).
close_reason: README now clearly marks vuln-scan as opt-in and links to CONFIG-SURFACE.md; vulnrefresh now logs both refresh starting and complete (with elapsed_ms) so operators can verify the OSV.dev network egress.

title: eng_get_hot_zone returns empty zones even on a fully-promoted, 50-file repo
description: After a cold scan + reindex of /home/jrose/src/all-reglet/tack (50 files, status=promoted, eng_get_entry_points returns 20+ entries), calling eng_get_hot_zone with {limit:3} returns zones:[] and no degraded_reasons. The generated wiki page agrees: 'No hot zones yet — files appear here once commits land while the repo is registered with veska.' That copy is the actual behaviour — hot-zones is only populated by post-promotion churn, not by cold-scan history walk — but a junior reading the README expects 'mechanical wiki computed from the graph' to work immediately. Either (a) bootstrap from git log on registration (we already shell out to git for HEAD, blame, diff), or (b) surface a degraded_reasons:['hot_zones_need_commits'] entry so the empty result is self-explaining instead of looking broken.
close_reason: Already addressed by solov2-636y (commit 9d4d440 — fix(mcp,cli): consistency + cwd-resolution UX cluster). eng_get_hot_zone now appends degraded_reasons=['no_post_registration_commits'] when ranking returns empty zones, exactly per the bead's option (b). My junior-eng report observed a stale veska-mcp binary built before that commit landed. Pinned by TestHotZone_EmptyZonesSurfacesDegradedReason / TestHotZone_NonEmptyZonesNoDegradedReason. Option (a) — bootstrap from a wider git-log window — intentionally deferred: extending the window dilutes the 'hot' / recency signal that drives the score, and the existing reason makes the empty result self-explaining.

title: 'veska doctor status' says 'status: broken' when daemon is simply not running
description: Right after a fresh 'veska init' before the user has run 'veska service start', 'veska doctor status' returns: 'status: broken (embedder=healthy, egress=broken, config=healthy, ingestion=healthy, queue=degraded)'. A junior following Quick Start steps 2->5 (init, doctor) before step 3 (service start) sees 'broken' as the very first signal Veska gives them. The hint line does say 'daemon socket not present — start it with veska service start', but the top-line word should not be 'broken' for a 'daemon not started yet' state. Suggest: a distinct status 'not_running' / 'stopped' for the egress=broken-AND-no-socket case, vs. true 'broken' (socket exists but unreachable, crash-loop marker, etc.).
close_reason: doctor status no longer says 'broken' on a fresh init pre-service-start: now distinguishes a stopped daemon (no marker file, no crash) from a real broken state. Lead status reflects the actual rollup, egress shows 'stopped', exit 0 in the benign case (exit 2 reserved for real faults like a broken marker).

title: Cold scan stages go.mod/README/etc. as empty parse results — bloats staging and confuses checks
description: walkAndSave in coldscan.go (internal/application/coldscan.go:216-274) passes EVERY non-binary file in the repo to Ingester.Save. The parsers (treesitter/ts.go:28, treesitter/go.go:41) return an empty ParseResult for unsupported extensions, but Ingester.Save still calls staging.StageIfCurrentGenerationWithParseData regardless of result.Nodes being empty. Consequences: (a) staging accumulates rows for every README, go.mod, .yml, .json, image, etc. in the repo, all with nothing to promote; (b) the resulting PromotionFile.Path list is the union of ALL repo files, which is what made me think the vuln-scan touchesGoMod check was 'kind of' getting the right input — but only because we were spraying everything at it. Either (1) skip staging when ParseResult is empty AND the file isn't a known manifest (go.mod / package.json / etc.) we'll need later, or (2) keep the wide net but document that batch.Files is 'every repo file' not 'every parsed file', and rename it. Related to solov2-3tqb.
close_reason: Cold-scan walker now filters at the file level via isInterestingForStaging: only feeds the ingester source files the wired parsers handle (.go, .ts, .tsx) or known manifests downstream checks gate on (currently go.mod for vuln-scan). README/.yml/.json/etc. are skipped, so staging is no longer the wide 'every non-binary file' set. Manifest list is centralized in coldscan.go and easy to extend when a new scanner arrives (package.json, Cargo.toml, etc.). Regression test TestColdScanReparser_SkipsNonSourceNonManifest added.

title: eng_get_status: large pending_embeds but degraded_reasons=[] — contradictory
description: eng_get_status returned {pending_embeds: 4699, repo_count: 5, status: 'ok', degraded_reasons: []} simultaneously. A 4.7k embed backlog is exactly the condition that should land in degraded_reasons (and that the search tool already surfaces as [degraded: embeddings_pending] inline). Either: (a) add a 'embeddings_pending' reason when the queue is non-trivial (>some threshold), or (b) ensure the status surfaces from the same code path as the search degradation so the two never disagree. Currently a junior sees 'status: ok' from eng_get_status, then 'degraded' from search — undermines trust.
close_reason: statusProvider.Status now flips rollup status to 'degraded' and appends 'embeddings_pending' to degraded_reasons when pendingEmbeds > 0, matching the signal eng_search_semantic already emits for the same backlog. The two surfaces no longer disagree (junior would see status: ok + [degraded: embeddings_pending] simultaneously). Added regression test TestStatusProvider_HealthyWhenNoPending and updated TestStatusProvider_ReportsDBState which had pinned the buggy behaviour.

title: eng_promote_repo requires root_path while every other tool accepts repo_id/short_id
description: Every read tool plus eng_remove_repo accepts {repo_id} (full or 12-char short_id). eng_promote_repo uniquely requires {root_path}, forcing the caller to look up the path from eng_list_repos before promoting. This is the lone exception in the repo-lifecycle family. Accept repo_id/short_id as a peer of root_path; resolve to root via the registry.
close_reason: Closed

title: duplicate TODO findings logged with mismatched actor_kind (agent:unknown vs service:veska)
description: eng_list_findings on reglet shows three structural/todo findings created within ~500ms of each other; one has actor_id='agent:unknown', actor_kind='agent', the next has actor_id='service:veska', actor_kind='system'. These are emitted by the same cold-scan promotion path. The 'who created this' attribution should be deterministic for system-generated checks — agent:unknown shouldn't surface for findings the daemon emitted itself.
close_reason: Closed

title: eng_list_findings and eng_find_todos disagree on file_path representation (relative vs absolute)
description: For the same TODO finding, eng_list_findings returns file_path='internal/infrastructure/container/container.go' (relative to repo root) while eng_find_todos returns file_path='/home/jrose/src/reglet/internal/infrastructure/container/container.go' (absolute). README states a single canonical node shape with file_path. Pick one — absolute is consistent with eng_find_symbol/eng_get_node — and update all finding-shaped responses to match.
close_reason: Closed

title: eng_get_file_nodes demands 'branch' while peer query tools default it
description: eng_get_file_nodes returns: 'file_path (or path), repo_id, and branch are required'. Every other query tool (find_symbol, get_node, get_blast_radius, find_todos) defaults branch to the repo's active branch when omitted. Either eng_get_file_nodes should follow the same default, or the README's 'branch defaults to active' claim needs an explicit exception.
close_reason: Closed

title: semantic search score scale doesn't match documented 1/(1+L2dist) formula
description: README says similarity = 1/(1+L2dist) for L2-normalised vectors. For unit vectors L2 distance is bounded by 2, so scores should land in roughly [0.33, 1.0]. In practice eng_search_semantic returns top scores of ~0.018 for reasonable queries (e.g. 'register cobra subcommand' against a cobra-rooted repo). Either the formula is wrong, vectors aren't being normalised at query time, or the cosine path is producing dot products of nearly-orthogonal vectors. Junior users will read 0.018 as 'no good match'.
close_reason: Closed

title: repo list status inconsistent between CLI and MCP
description: 'veska repo list' marks /tmp/big-Au8c as STATUS='(missing)' because the directory is gone, but the MCP tool eng_list_repos returns the same repo with status='promoted'. Both surfaces should agree — either both detect the missing root and both surface 'missing', or the CLI suppresses the check. Today an agent reading from MCP has no way to know the repo's root has vanished.
close_reason: Closed

title: LSP adapter over the daemon: expose veska to IDEs that don't speak MCP
description:
Today veska serves an editor only via MCP (veska-mcp stdio shim → daemon's mcp.sock). That covers MCP-aware editors (Cursor, Zed, Claude Desktop, Continue, Copilot-via-bridge), but leaves the bulk of the IDE market — JetBrains, VS Code without a bridge, Neovim with built-in LSP, Helix, etc. — out. The veska graph already has the structural data those IDEs want (nodes, CONTAINS/CALLS/IMPORTS edges, ranges); what's missing is an LSP server in front of it.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SCOPE OF THIS BEAD
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Sketch the LSP surface — which methods map cleanly, which need new data,
which are out of scope — and the wiring shape (new binary vs. mode of an
existing one; how it shares the daemon's SQLite). Implementation is a
follow-up after the sketch is reviewed.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
LSP METHODS — FIRST-PASS MAPPING
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Clean fits (graph already has the data):
  textDocument/definition       — node.symbol_path + LineRange → Location
  textDocument/references       — EdgeReader.InboundEdges (CALLS kind) on
                                  the target node_id; project edge sources
                                  back to (file_path, line_start)
  textDocument/documentSymbol   — NodeLookup.NodesInFile → SymbolInformation[]
                                  (kind mapping function/method/struct/...
                                  → SymbolKind already 1:1)
  workspace/symbol              — eng_find_symbol → SymbolInformation[]
  textDocument/hover            — node.signature + node.raw_content[:N]
                                  formatted as MarkupContent
  textDocument/implementation   — for interfaces: scan structs whose method
                                  set covers the interface (NEW data — not
                                  in the graph today; could be a derived
                                  view or precomputed at promotion)
  textDocument/callHierarchy/*  — eng_get_call_chain already does BFS over
                                  CALLS; map prepareCallHierarchy → seed
                                  node, incoming/outgoing → InboundEdges /
                                  OutgoingEdges
  textDocument/typeDefinition   — needs a TYPE_OF edge (NEW data) or fallback
                                  to find_symbol on the signature's return
                                  type

Needs new extraction or a tighter parser pass:
  textDocument/completion       — agent-shaped semantic search is not what
                                  IDEs expect here; would need scope-aware
                                  identifier completion which veska does NOT
                                  index today. EXCLUDE from v1.
  textDocument/signatureHelp    — needs param-position parsing; tree-sitter
                                  has the data, no extraction pass writes it.
                                  Defer.
  textDocument/rename           — needs definite-cross-file usage edges
                                  (today CALLS is the only cross-file edge;
                                  refactor would mis-rename ambiguous
                                  identifiers). Defer.

Probably out of scope (compiler-grade analysis):
  textDocument/diagnostics      — overlaps with promotion-time findings,
                                  but live syntax/type errors belong to
                                  the language's own LSP (gopls, tsserver).
                                  Possible LATER: surface promotion
                                  Findings as Information diagnostics
                                  ('vulnerable dep', 'leaked secret',
                                  'dead code') — distinct from compile
                                  errors and complementary, not competing.
  textDocument/codeAction       — refactors out of scope; quick-fix for
                                  promotion Findings could land later.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
WIRING — TWO PLAUSIBLE SHAPES
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
A. New binary 'veska-lsp' that talks JSON-RPC over stdio (LSP transport)
   and opens the daemon's mcp.sock for tool calls. Mirrors veska-mcp's
   model: thin shim, daemon does the work, no second SQLite connection
   to coordinate. EASIEST. Likely 500-1000 LoC.

B. Mode flag on veska-daemon ('--lsp-stdio') that runs an LSP server
   inline in the daemon process. Avoids the extra round-trip but couples
   the daemon's lifecycle to a single editor session, breaking the
   one-daemon/many-editors story. NOT RECOMMENDED.

Lean: A. Same hexagonal shape as today (CLI / MCP shim / daemon).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
PROTOCOL NOTES — WHERE THIS DEPARTS FROM AN LSP-NATIVE SERVER
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
- No didChange streaming. veska only sees files at commit / staging
  boundaries; it can't answer 'definition' on lines the editor just typed.
  Document this loudly: 'veska-lsp answers against your last save +
  staging snapshot; for live-edit answers, run alongside gopls/tsserver
  not instead of'.
- Single-language gopls-style assumption doesn't apply: veska indexes Go
  + TS today (parser_pool), so the LSP can answer for both in one
  process. Editors usually expect one LSP per language; some support
  multiple. Could ship as 'veska-lsp' that registers for go + ts.
- selectionRange / foldingRange could be derived from the graph's
  LineRange data essentially for free — low-effort add.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
COEXISTENCE WITH MCP
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Both servers are read-only against the same SQLite (the daemon's
pools.ReadDB). No write coordination needed. An editor could even run
both: MCP for the agent panel ('explain this' / semantic search) and
LSP for go-to-def / find-references in the gutter. They're complementary
surfaces over one graph.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ACCEPTANCE FOR THE SKETCH
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
- Design doc under docs/design/ enumerating the v1 method set with the
  graph data each one reads.
- Decision on wiring shape (A vs B).
- Identification of any new edges/columns required (TYPE_OF? interface
  implementation index?).
- A short note on liveness limits (last-save freshness) to set editor-
  user expectations.

Out of scope for this bead: writing the LSP server itself, picking
client wiring for any specific IDE.

title: veska-mcp shim should forward cwd to eng_get_current_repo
description:
Today eng_get_current_repo requires an explicit cwd param. The veska-mcp stdio shim runs in the editor's working directory but doesn't inject cwd into params for tools that accept it. An MCP client (editor) that doesn't know to pass cwd hits 'cwd is required' even when the user is plainly inside a registered repo.

Fix: in the shim transport, when forwarding to eng_get_current_repo (and any other tool that accepts cwd as optional), inject os.Getwd() into params if not present. Don't override an explicit cwd from the client.
acceptance_criteria: 1) printf '{...eng_get_current_repo...params:{}}' | veska-mcp from inside a registered repo returns the repo. 2) Explicit cwd in params still wins. 3) Test in cmd/veska-mcp.
close_reason: Closed

title: Symbol/context CLI wrappers: 'veska symbol', 'veska context'
