close_reason: Fat/thin build mechanism shipped + verified: make build-fat embeds model2vec via //go:embed (build tag embed_model), thin build unchanged. Verified live — thin daemon 34M elects static-v2, fat 96M elects model2vec(potion-code-16M) with no install/network; veska-mcp stays thin (4.7M). On-disk install wins over embedded; both share model_id (no reindex churn). Weights gitignored (never committed). Remaining: goreleaser/release default flavor + memory-profile decision are ops/packaging follow-ups.

title: veska install model2vec: download + sha-verify potion-code-16M model files
description:
One-command model fetch so users don't curl files by hand. The download path (ensureModel + sha verification) is already built in internal/infrastructure/embedding/model2vec/download.go; this bead is the CLI surface + the concrete ModelSpec (HF base URL + per-file sha256 hashes for tokenizer.json + model.safetensors).

'veska install model2vec' → downloads to <VeskaHome>/static-model/potion-code-16M/, verifies sha, prints next-step ('restart daemon to use it'). Pairs with the auto-elect UX (solov2-1az): once the files are present, boot election picks them up automatically.

Needs: pin the exact HF repo + revision + file shas (so the download is reproducible and verifiable). Confirm potion-code-16M exists / pick the right minishlab model if the name differs.
notes: Done: veska install model2vec CLI + pinned ModelSpec (HF rev 86848193..., tokenizer.json + model.safetensors shas). model2vec.Install wraps ensureModel; ModelDir helper shared with TryLoad. Tested end-to-end (download+sha+idempotent) and unit (Install httptest + spec-pin validation).
close_reason: veska install model2vec shipped: pinned ModelSpec + CLI; download path reused; tested.

title: Real Model2Vec inference (potion-code-16M): HF tokenizer + safetensors + download
description: Pure-Go Model2Vec framework lives in internal/infrastructure/embedding/model2vec/ (safetensors reader, BertNormalizer+BertPreTokenizer+WordPiece tokenizer, inference with mean-pool + L2-normalize + zero-pad to 768, sha-verified download, TryLoad with ErrModelNotPresent gate). 23 tests against synthetic fixtures pass. Production wire-up was WITHDRAWN in commit after dfb393e because the composite-as-write-time-fallback design has a correctness hole during transitions (ollama-up-then-down sequences silently land model2vec vectors into an ollama-shaped index, causing ranking inversions). Daemon is back on composite(ollama, static-v2). Next step before re-wiring model2vec: (1) verify against a real potion-code-16M file — tokenizer.json field names + embeddings tensor name (probably 'embeddings.weight' not 'embeddings') likely need adjustment; (2) decide on the cross-provider mixing fix — either embedder-lock marker + reindex command, or per-row model_id filtering at search time.
close_reason: Stale-description close: model2vec(potion-code-16M) is live as the daemon's default elected embedder. Real model files (64M safetensors + 1M tokenizer.json) load via TryLoad and produce semantic embeddings (verified live: query 'persist document' returns the function whose comment is 'persists a document to disk'). The two open concerns named in the bead are addressed: (1) real-file verification — works in practice; (2) cross-provider mixing — fixed in solov2-fz8 (auto-reindex on switch wipes the old-model store before the vector cache rehydrates). Any future model2vec correctness issue can be filed fresh against a specific failure mode.

title: Rework eng_get_entry_points scoring: high inbound fan-in, not low blast radius (moat)
description: m8d's close-reason flagged this: current scoring rewards LOW inbound blast radius (i.e. leaf functions with one test caller). Real entry points have HIGH fan-in (lots of callers across the codebase), are exported (capitalized), live in non-test files. Rework: rank by inbound-edge count desc, then by 'is exported', then by 'is not in _test.go'. Keep adjacent-test as a tiebreaker bonus rather than hard gate. Live-verify: cobra's eng_get_entry_points should put Command.Execute, Command.AddCommand etc near the top, not bench helpers.
close_reason: Reworked scoring: rank by inbound_count DESC → exported DESC → has_adjacent_test DESC → name ASC. Adjacent-test is now a tiebreaker not a hard gate. Dropped blast-radius cap and the per-candidate blast.Of call. New JSON shape: {symbol_name, file_path, kind, inbound_count, exported, has_adjacent_test}.

title: TypeScript receiver-selector calls parity with Go (moat)
description: solov2-q9p extended Go parser to resolve 's.foo()' inside a method on *Server. TypeScript needs the same for 'this.foo()' inside a class method. The TS parser in internal/infrastructure/treesitter/ts.go currently passes empty strings to collectCallNames so the selector path is dead. Bind 'this' as the receiver name + className as receiver type during class body traversal. AC: (1) intra-class this.foo() emits CALLS edge; (2) constructor calls 'this.x()' resolved; (3) tests against a small TS class fixture.
close_reason: Class-method bodies now visited as callers; collectCallNames extended to handle TS member_expression with recvName='this' + recvType=className. Mirrors Go's selector_expression path (solov2-q9p). Includes constructor case via standard method_definition path.

title: Include source snippets in eng_get_context_pack node entries (moat)
description: Today context_pack returns {NodeID, Name, Path, Kind, Distance, has_open_finding}. To use it, the agent has to Read each file. Add an optional 'include_snippets' (default true for new callers) that fills in a snippet field with the symbol's raw_content trimmed to a budget. We already store raw_content; just need to slice + budget it. AC: (1) snippet field per node; (2) per-node budget so a single huge symbol doesn't eat the pack; (3) token estimation honored against TokenBudget (already there). This makes the flagship 'context pack' tool actually one-shot.
close_reason: NodeInfo.Snippet now carries the symbol's raw_content, trimmed to PerNodeSnippetBytes (1500). Propagated via blastradius.Entry.Snippet from ports.NodeMeta. Existing token-budget clip still enforces whole-pack ceiling.

title: Per-file chunk index (in addition to per-symbol nodes) for non-declaration code
description: Today we index parsed symbols (functions/methods/types). Free-floating code — package-level vars, init() guts, top-of-file documentation, anything outside a declaration — is invisible to semantic search. Semble's chunk-based approach catches all of this. Add a parallel chunk index keyed by (repo, file, start_line, end_line) so search can return non-declaration matches too. Probably extends node_embeddings table with a chunks variant or adds a chunks table. Needs design before implementation.
close_reason: Added domain.KindChunk + language-agnostic chunkFile helper in internal/infrastructure/treesitter/. Each parser calls chunkFile after symbol extraction; emits KindChunk nodes (80-line windows) for uncovered line ranges only — symbols stay first-class, chunks fill the gaps. Chunks flow through the existing storage/embedder/FTS/search pipeline unchanged.

title: Reranking signals: definition boost, identifier stems, file coherence, noise penalty
description: Semble's quality edge over a naive vector + BM25 fusion is a small set of rerankers applied to the fused candidate list: (a) DEFINITION boost — a chunk that defines the queried symbol (func/method/class) outranks chunks that reference it; (b) IDENTIFIER STEMS — query 'parse config' should boost ParseConfig/configParser/etc; (c) FILE COHERENCE — multiple matches in same file boost the file; (d) NOISE PENALTY — *_test.go, legacy/, examples/, .d.ts ranked down. We already have building blocks (Kind on nodes, basename, path inspection). Depends on solov2-NEW-hybrid (filed) so we have a fused candidate list to rerank.
close_reason: Implemented post-fusion rerank pipeline: definition boost, identifier stems, file coherence, noise penalty. Subsumes solov2-x35 (applyNameMatchBoost).

title: veska search <query> [path-or-url] CLI: clone+index+query in one shot
description: Semble: 'semble search "X" https://github.com/foo/bar' clones, indexes, returns. We require 'veska repo add /path' first plus a running daemon. Add a CLI shortcut that handles the whole flow for one-off evaluation use-cases. AC: (1) git URL accepted as positional, clones to a tmp dir on demand; (2) reuses index when re-run on same URL/path; (3) emits same shape as eng_search_semantic; (4) standalone mode (no daemon required).
close_reason: Added 'veska search <query> [path|url]' subcommand. AC1: git URLs cloned to ~/.veska-search-cache/<sha>/repo on demand (--depth=1). AC2: deterministic cache dir + repo registry persistence reuses index on re-run. AC3: --json emits {results,degraded_reasons} envelope (matches eng_search_semantic). AC4: standalone — opens veska.db + ollama in-process, runs cold-scan + drains embedder queue synchronously when repo is unindexed, no daemon required.

title: Entry-points scoring promotes test funcs as the primary entries (filter unmasks it)
description: With solov2-bos's test-symbol filter in place, eng_get_entry_points returns 0 entries for cobra and 315 entries with include_tests=true — i.e. 100% of what wiki.EntryPointsService.Select picks are Test*/Benchmark*/Example* funcs. The selection rule ('symbol with adjacent test + small blast radius') happens to match test funcs themselves because they have trivially small blast radius and a 'sibling' relationship to the SUT they exercise. The real public-API symbols (Execute, AddCommand, Run, etc.) don't surface. Investigate wiki/entrypoints.go scoring: probably needs an explicit 'symbol is not a test' guard inside Select itself, plus a blast-radius LOWER bound so 'symbol nothing calls' doesn't masquerade as 'symbol that is the entrypoint to a feature'.
close_reason: Service-level fix at candidate selection: Test*/Benchmark*/Example*/Fuzz* names and *_test.go paths now excluded before scoring. Added SelectWith(opts) + SelectOptions{IncludeTests}; eng_get_entry_points pipes include_tests through. Service-level + MCP-level filter are belt-and-suspenders (defence in depth). Live-verified on cobra: default=0 entries, include_tests=true=315 entries (Test/Benchmark/Example funcs). The fact that default=0 reflects the deeper scoring quirk noted in the original bead — entry-point scoring rewards LOW inbound blast radius which is the opposite of 'top-of-stack entry'; that rework is out of scope for the minimal fix the user requested.

title: No cross-repo edges (cobra imports pflag → no IMPORTS edge across repos)
description: cobra/command.go has 'flag "github.com/spf13/pflag"' as an import. pflag is registered as a separate repo. No IMPORTS edge spans repos in our graph today (edges are keyed by (repo_id, branch)). The multi-repo workspace value-prop is severely limited without this. Design notes: (a) need a module-path → repo_id registry (we have go.mod for each repo so resolution is doable for Go), (b) need to decide whether to extend the edges table schema (drop the per-repo scoping on edge src/dst) or model cross-repo links as a separate table, (c) blast_radius and call_chain queries need to follow these edges. Significant change — flag for design/spike before implementing.
notes: WRITE SIDE NOW IMPLEMENTED via solov2-xc51.3: promotion emits cross_repo_edge_stubs for package-qualified calls into external modules (module_path=import path, symbol_path=callee, language=go). The pre-existing query-time resolver (internal/infrastructure/sqlite/resolver) binds them to the owning registered repo, and call_chain already expands them. REMAINING for full closure: (a) IMPORTS-edge stubs (not just CALLS) if desired; (b) subpackage imports of multi-package modules don't resolve — resolver matches repos.module_path EXACTLY, so import github.com/x/y/sub won't match module github.com/x/y; (c) blast_radius cross-repo expansion parity with call_chain. Verify end-to-end with two registered repos (e.g. an app + pflag).
close_reason: Two phases landed: xc51.3 emitted cross_repo_edge_stubs (write side), now wired WithResolveFunc on RegisterGraphTools and added matching WithBlastResolveFunc on RegisterBlastTools so call_chain AND blast_radius surface cross_repo_edges. Verified live: app->pflag cross-repo CALLS edge resolved by both tools. Remaining gaps split into hkr9 (subpackage resolution) and a P4 IMPORTS-stubs bead, each independently sized.

title: eng_get_context_pack returns 2 nodes (self + package) for cobra Command.Execute
description: The flagship 'give me context for this symbol' MCP tool degrades to nothing on a real library: eng_get_context_pack for Command.Execute on cobra returns nodes=[self, package]. Downstream of solov2-NEW9 (selector-call parsing gap): with no real call edges, the related[] traversal has nowhere to walk. Re-verify after #9 lands; if still anemic, the context pack may also need to consider SIMILAR_TO edges as a fallback signal.
close_reason: Two fixes combine: (a) solov2-q9p gave us real CALLS edges on cobra; (b) context-pack now walks blast radius with Direction=DirBoth so the pack covers what-the-symbol-calls AND who-calls-it, not just inbound callers. Live-verified on cobra: eng_get_context_pack(Command.Execute) returned 2 nodes before, now returns 133 (within the existing MaxDepth=3, MaxNodes=200 caps).

title: eng_get_hot_zone returns empty for large repos because hot-zone scoring needs edges
description: Cobra (~32k LOC, 65 files) returns 0 hot zones from eng_get_hot_zone. Likely root cause: hot-zone scoring uses inbound-edge counts, but the parser emits very few CALLS edges (mostly empty thanks to selector-call gap — see solov2-NEW9). Once selector-call extraction lands, re-verify hot zones populate; if they still don't, debug the scoring threshold (probably tuned for the dogfood repo).
close_reason: Root cause was a path-key mismatch: git ChangeCounts returns repo-root-relative paths but the nodes table stores absolute file_paths. nodesInFile returned [] for every entry → blast_radius=0 → all 20 hot zones tied at score=0, with non-source files (.github/dependabot.yml etc) crowding out real code. HotZoneService.Rank now filepath.Joins repoRoot before lookup. Live-verified on cobra: command.go (br=133), command_test.go (br=137) now top the list with non-zero scores. Note: the parser improvements from solov2-q9p (selector-call edges) are what make the scores meaningful — without those, blast radius would be tiny everywhere even after this fix.

title: eng_get_entry_points floods results with Test*/Benchmark*/Example* funcs
description: For cobra (65 files), eng_get_entry_points returns hundreds of TestX / BenchmarkX / ExampleX functions as 'entry points'. Test funcs are not entry points to the public API; they're noise. Fix: by default exclude nodes whose file_path ends in _test.go (Go convention) and whose name starts with Test/Benchmark/Example/Fuzz. Add an 'include_tests' boolean param (default false) so callers can opt-in. Rank remaining entries by blast_radius desc.
close_reason: MCP layer now post-filters Test*/Benchmark*/Example*/Fuzz* names and *_test.go paths by default; include_tests=true param opts back in (no parameter rename so existing callers keep working). Live-verified on cobra: 315 unfiltered entries (all tests/benchmarks/examples) → 0 entries after filter. The fact that the filter zeroes the list exposes a deeper scoring bug — filed as new bead [solov2-NEW] for follow-up.

title: eng_search_semantic has no cross-repo mode (repo_id required)
description: The multi-repo value-prop is 'ask questions across your whole workspace', but eng_search_semantic rejects calls without a repo_id. Add either: (a) optional repo_id (when omitted, search all registered repos), or (b) a separate eng_search_workspace tool that fans out per-repo and merges results. Needs care around vector backend per repo (sqlite-vec vs usearch) and score normalization across repos.
notes:
DESIGN PASS (deferred — full discussion preserved verbatim so a future implementer or future-self has the whole thought process, not just the conclusion).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
PROBLEM
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
eng_search_semantic requires repo_id. The workspace value-prop is "ask
questions across your whole workspace," but today you can't. The
mechanical part (fan-out per repo, run k-NN) is easy. The hard question
is merging results from independent indices into one ranked list when
the underlying scores aren't directly comparable.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
KEY DECISIONS (with all options + my lean at each branch)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
D1. API shape
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
A. Make repo_id optional on eng_search_semantic  [MY LEAN]
   Pros: one tool, two modes. Most LLM-friendly — the agent doesn't
   have to know which tool to call. Backwards compatible.
   Cons: slightly more handler complexity (branching on presence).

B. New tool eng_search_workspace
   Pros: explicit separation; per-repo and cross-repo have different
   perf characteristics, two tools makes that visible.
   Cons: adds surface area; the agent has to choose.

C. Both
   Pros: most flexible.
   Cons: two tools doing the same thing — a discoverability liability.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
D2. Result merge strategy (THE REAL DESIGN QUESTION)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
A. Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF, k=60)  [MY LEAN]
   Behavior: each repo produces its top-K. Each result's contribution
   is 1/(60+rank_in_its_list). Sum across lists per node, sort desc.
   Trade-off: proven technique (TREC, Elasticsearch). Score-
   distribution-agnostic. Loses absolute score info, but that info
   wasn't comparable anyway.

B. Per-repo min-max or z-score normalization
   Behavior: rescale each repo's scores into a common range, then
   global sort by normalized score.
   Trade-off: preserves relative magnitudes within each repo. Brittle
   when a repo has very few results (z-score blows up) or when
   distributions are skewed.

C. Raw scores, global sort
   Behavior: concatenate all results, sort by score.
   Trade-off: simplest. BROKEN IN PRACTICE — score scales differ
   across repos due to vocabulary, density, embedder warm-up. The
   "best" result is often whoever has the most extreme score
   distribution, not the best match.

D. Per-repo grouping; return top-K per repo, no merge
   Behavior: caller merges.
   Trade-off: simple for us. Forces every caller (every LLM) to
   implement merging — wrong direction.

RRF is exactly designed for this problem (combining ranked lists from
heterogeneous systems) and the existing scores are already only useful
as ranks, not as magnitudes (similarity = 1/(1+L2dist), a monotonic but
warped transform).

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
D3. Per-repo k (how many results to ask each repo for)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
User asks for k=10 across N repos.

A. Over-fetch: perRepoK = max(10, min(50, ceil(2*k / N)))  [MY LEAN]
   Get enough from each to make RRF's tail useful, capped at the
   user's k after merge.

B. Per-repo k = user's k
   Simple, but wasteful when N is small and noisy when N is large.

C. Adaptive per-repo k by repo size
   Optimal but complex.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
D4. Repo filter (workspace scoping)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
include_repos: ["app", "lib"] (allow-list of short_ids or full ids)
   MY LEAN: ship this.
exclude_repos: [...]
   Less useful, defer.
branch_overrides: {repo_id: branch}
   Each repo uses its active_branch by default; this is an optional
   override map. Defer.

Default branch behaviour: each repo uses its own active_branch.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
D5. Partial failures
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
One repo's search errors (vector store glitch, branch missing, etc.).
Today's eng_search_semantic returns an error. Cross-repo can do better:

MY LEAN: continue with the other repos, add
  degraded_reasons: "partial_repo_failure"
  failed_repos: ["xyz"]
to the response. Same pattern as existing low_quality_static_embedder.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
D6. Cross-repo dedupe
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
The same node_id could appear in two repos (rare — would require
deterministic ids colliding across repos). Content collisions are more
plausible (vendored code).

MY LEAN: dedupe by (repo_id, node_id) tuple only — keep distinct
repos' hits separate. No content-hash dedupe; if two repos have the
same code, the user genuinely wants to see both.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
D7. Composition with hi5 (multi-model)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
If hi5 is in: each repo has per-bucket indices. Cross-repo + multi-
model = up to N_repos × N_buckets ranked lists into RRF. RRF handles
arbitrarily many input lists with no extra design — same formula, more
terms in the sum. No additional work needed beyond making the fan-out
loop over the (repo × bucket) Cartesian product.

If hi5 is not in (today): one list per repo, same RRF logic.

CONCLUSION: bcn works with or without hi5. It does not need to wait.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
D8. Backend cost
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
- sqlite-vec (in-memory linear scan per repo): cost scales
  O(N_repos × repo_size) — fine for typical workspace size (≤ a few
  dozen repos).
- usearch HNSW (above the M2 threshold): one index per repo, each
  search is O(log n). Even better for cross-repo.

Either way, per-query cost is N_repos × (single-repo cost). No new
bottleneck.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
OPEN QUESTIONS (asked for user's call before writing the bead;
captured here so they're picked up when un-deferring)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

1. Tool name when repo_id is omitted: does that count as "workspace
   search" in the docs? Stays eng_search_semantic; behaviour in the
   optional-repo case becomes a documented mode. Or distinct tool name
   for discoverability? MY LEAN: keep one tool, document both modes.

2. Empty workspace (repo_count=0): return [] with
   degraded_reasons: "empty_workspace"? MY LEAN: yes.

3. Mixed-language workspace (Go repo + TS repo, hi5 active): the
   search-time bucket fan-out is per-repo, so each repo's bucket set
   is naturally the union of installed models. No special handling
   needed.

4. Score in the response when using RRF: the existing response has a
   score field per result. RRF produces a fused score that's not
   directly comparable to today's 1/(1+L2dist) scale. MY LEAN: add a
   score_basis: "rrf" | "raw" field for forward-compat.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
MINIMUM-VIABLE ARCHITECTURE (assuming all leans accepted)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

1. eng_search_semantic params: repo_id becomes optional. Add
   include_repos []string. Add expand_cross_repo bool (already exists
   in call_chain — symmetric flag for stub expansion).

2. Handler detects mode:
   - repo_id present → existing single-repo path, unchanged.
   - repo_id empty → workspace mode:
     - Resolve target set: include_repos if given, else all registered
       repos.
     - For each repo (and each installed bucket if hi5 lands): k-NN
       search with perRepoK = max(10, min(50, ceil(2k/N))).
     - Collect ranked lists, RRF-merge with k=60.
     - Truncate to user's k.
     - Hydrate node metadata (file_path, kind, etc.) per result — this
       is already per-repo via NodeLookup.

3. Response: add score_basis: "raw" | "rrf" field; add
   degraded_reasons entries partial_repo_failure / empty_workspace as
   needed; add searched_repos: []short_id so the caller sees what was
   actually queried.

4. search.Service gets a Workspace(ctx, query, k, opts) (Response,
   error) method that takes []repoID; the per-repo loop and RRF live
   there, not in the MCP handler. Keeps the layering clean (handler
   stays thin).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
QUESTIONS LEFT UNANSWERED WHEN bcn WAS POSTPONED
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1. D1 (API shape): optional repo_id on existing tool, vs. new
   eng_search_workspace tool? (My lean: A — optional repo_id.)
2. D4 (filter): ship include_repos only, or also exclude_repos /
   branch overrides? (My lean: include_repos only.)
3. #4 (score_basis): add the field now (forward-compat) or just
   document? (My lean: add it.)
4. Sequencing vs hi5: bcn is fine to land independently. (My lean:
   agree.)

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
DECISION CRITERIA FOR UN-DEFERRING
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
- The four open questions above are decided.
- A user-facing need to search across repos surfaces (e.g. agents
  routinely working across a multi-repo workspace).
- No technical dependency on hi5 — can be picked up at any time.
UN-DEFERRED 2026-05-23. Triggers: (a) the user just walked a real 2-cobra-repo workflow, which is exactly the workspace-search use case; (b) the design pass already in this bead chose RRF (k=60) and an optional repo_id on the existing tool — no open architectural questions left; (c) no technical blocker (each repo's vector backend handles its own k-NN; the merge is rank-based and score-distribution-agnostic). Ready for implementation; pick up after current parser work settles.

title: eng_search_semantic ignores 'limit' param (returns 10 when limit=5)
description: MCP call: eng_search_semantic with limit=5 returned 10 results. The limit param is documented but silently ignored — the service caps at some internal default. Trace through application/search.Service.Semantic and ensure the requested limit is applied (clamped to a reasonable hard max). Add a regression test.
close_reason: eng_search_semantic / eng_search_similar now accept 'limit' as an alias for 'k'. Root cause was a naming mismatch: every other MCP tool uses 'limit', but search tools historically named the param 'k', so callers sending {"limit":5} silently got k=10 (default). When both are present, k wins to keep the historical contract. Test added.

title: MCP tools/list returns method-not-found — AI clients can't introspect
description: Standard MCP clients call 'tools/list' to discover the tool catalog. veska-mcp responds with -32601 method-not-found. veska's JSON-RPC server uses flat method-name dispatch (method == tool name) rather than the MCP envelope (tools/list + tools/call). For an IDE plugging into the daemon via veska-mcp, this means the AI tool sees no advertised capabilities. Either implement tools/list returning the registered tool set with JSONSchema, or document that veska speaks a flat dialect and ship a generator for clients.
close_reason: Registry.Dispatch now recognises 'tools/list' and 'tools/call' (the MCP standard envelope) alongside flat eng_<verb>_<object> dispatch. tools/list returns name + description + inputSchema/outputSchema for every registered tool, sorted by name. tools/call expects params={"name":"<tool>","arguments":{...}} and dispatches with the unwrapped arguments. Backwards-compat preserved: existing flat callers (veska-mcp shim, CLI's callMCP) keep working unchanged. Live-verified: tools/list returns 31 tools; tools/call wrapping eng_list_repos returns the same 4-repo decorated view as the flat call.

title: eng_list_repos / doctor status give no human signal when a repo is broken
description: When a repo's startup-resync fails, eng_list_repos surfaces it only as LastPromotedSHA="" (empty string). 'doctor status' ignores ingestion state entirely and reports 'healthy'. Add: (a) a 'last_promoted_sha is empty AND repo is older than X' check to doctor, (b) a per-repo status field in eng_list_repos ('promoted' | 'never_promoted' | 'last_resync_failed:<msg>').
close_reason: Two surfaces now signal ingestion state: (a) eng_list_repos returns RepoView with a 'status' field ('promoted'|'unindexed') so callers don't have to interpret empty LastPromotedSHA; (b) 'doctor status' adds an ingestion probe that walks the repos table and marks degraded when any repo has empty last_promoted_sha, with the unindexed repo_ids in the detail message. Verified on a degraded-state env: 'status: broken (egress=broken, ingestion=degraded) — 1 unindexed repo(s): [f7905efb9014]'.

title: No 'veska repo list' CLI subcommand — Sam can add/remove repos but can't list them
description: 'veska repo' has only add+remove. The MCP tool eng_list_repos works, but the CLI is the first surface a new user touches. After 'veska repo add' Sam has no way to confirm what is registered. Add 'veska repo list' that prints repo_id + root_path + active_branch + last_promoted_sha (or '(never promoted)' when empty).
close_reason: Added 'veska repo list' Cobra subcommand. Prefers eng_list_repos via the daemon (so it stays consistent with what the daemon sees), direct-DB fallback when daemon is down. Output: REPO_ID / BRANCH / STATUS / ROOT table with truncated 12-char ids; status='(unindexed)' when last_promoted_sha is empty (also surfaces the solov2-b9y signal). Verified against Sam's 4-repo workspace.

title: No MCP / CLI path to CREATE a task — task tools are read+activate only
description:
Sam's exploration. The MCP tool surface has eng_set_active_task, eng_get_active_task, and eng_get_task_history but no eng_create_task or eng_add_task. set_active_task requires the task to exist in the tasks table; the only way to insert is direct SQL.

Result: a user exploring the tools cannot get tasks working. eng_set_active_task fails with 'task not found' for any user-supplied task_id; there's no documented or surface path to create one first.

Fix options:
  1. Add eng_set_active_task auto-create behaviour: if task_id doesn't exist, insert it with the call's title (which is currently parsed but never used because validation fires first). Document the change.
  2. Add a separate eng_create_task tool that inserts a fresh tasks row.
  3. Remove the task surface entirely until an integration (Jira/Linear/GitHub) actually creates them.

Most likely the right answer is (1) — let set_active_task be 'upsert and activate' rather than 'lookup and activate'. The existing TitleParams field on set_active_task strongly suggests this was the original intent.

Acceptance: A user with no prior task setup can run eng_set_active_task(task_id="abc", title="investigate") and have a row land in tasks plus become active, observable via eng_get_active_task.
acceptance_criteria: eng_set_active_task with title creates the task row when absent and sets it active. eng_get_active_task returns the just-set task.
close_reason: Parked: wire.go's RegisterTaskTools call commented out with doc-comment + symbol kept reachable for future re-enable. wire_test.go and tests/mcp updated. Three tools (eng_set_active_task, get_active_task, get_task_history) no longer in the surface.

title: Semantic search quality on small corpora: scores cluster tight, name+docstring match ranks low
description:
Discovered during Sam's exploratory journey. On a 10-node test repo with nodes.snippet populated:

  query: 'save a note to memory'
  rank #7 (of 10): NoteStore.Save — the function whose docstring literally is
                   'Save appends a note' and method name is 'Save'

All 10 scores are within 0.00003 of each other (0.00211–0.00214). Effectively no ranking signal between the right answer and unrelated items. Reproduced with snippets present (sxa fixed), so this isn't a 'no body in embed text' regression.

Likely causes (need investigation):
  - nomic-embed-text produces vectors whose cosine similarity is dominated by package/path tokens, drowning out the distinguishing function-name signal at small scale.
  - 1/(1+L2dist) scoring compresses small differences (most pairs are near-orthogonal but the inverse hides that).
  - Embed-text projection might be weighting the wrong fields. Cf. domain.EmbedText.

Possible improvements (in order of complexity):
  1. Switch the scoring function to expose raw cosine similarity (or 1-L2dist/2) so callers see the spread.
  2. Boost name-match in the ranking: hybrid BM25 lexical + semantic score.
  3. Re-project embed-text to weight the function name more (repeat it, or prepend a 'symbol:' marker).
  4. Larger model (mxbai-embed-large, etc.) — better separation but heavier.

Acceptance: on a 10-node test repo, a query containing both the function's name AND a verb from its docstring ranks that function in top 3 with a clear score gap over irrelevant nodes.
acceptance_criteria: On the Sam-style notes-API repo, eng_search_semantic(query='save a note to memory') ranks NoteStore.Save in top 3 with score >= 1.25× the median hit's score.
close_reason: Added applyNameMatchBoost in search.Service.Semantic: tokenise query (≥3 chars), 0.25×max_vector_score bonus per matching symbol_path token, stable-sort. Live-verified: 'save a note to memory' on Sam's notes-API repo now ranks NoteStore.Save #1 (0.00318) vs previous #7 (0.00211). Unit tests + boost.go.

title: Add scan_state per-repo field to eng_get_status
acceptance_criteria: eng_get_status returns scans_in_flight=[…] while a cold scan is running and scans_in_flight=[] once complete. Harness test asserts the latter.
close_reason: eng_get_status now returns scans_in_flight: [{repo_id, phase, started_at}, …]. Reparser Start/End on a shared ScanTracker; statusProvider.Snapshot serialises into the result. Empty list when idle, populated while a cold scan is running. Harness test added.

title: veska service install --dry-run prints only 'would install service' with no detail
description:
Newbie journey, gap #6. 'veska service install --dry-run' outputs a single line:

  dry-run: would install service

This tells the user nothing useful: which supervisor (systemd? launchd? plist?), what files would be written where, what user, what env. A newbie cannot decide whether the change is safe without first running it for real — defeating the purpose of --dry-run.

Fix: --dry-run should print the concrete changes that would happen, e.g.:

  dry-run: would install veska daemon as a systemd user service
    write:   ~/.config/systemd/user/veska-daemon.service
    enable:  systemctl --user enable veska-daemon.service
    start:   systemctl --user start veska-daemon.service
    binary:  /home/jrose/go/bin/veska-daemon

…or whatever the actual install path does on the host's OS.
acceptance_criteria: 'veska service install --dry-run' prints the supervisor type, every file path it would touch, and every command it would invoke. No 'just trust me' summaries.
close_reason: Dry-run now delegates to the manager's existing detailed output (NewDryRun constructor + pick() in serviceCmd subcommands). Verified live: install/start/uninstall dry-runs print the concrete unit-file path and every systemctl invocation.

title: README has no example MCP invocation; veska-mcp --help is silent on protocol
description:
Newbie journey, gaps #10 + #11. Once the daemon is up, a user with no MCP-aware editor (or who wants to verify things from a shell) has no documented way to call any tool. The README's MCP-tools table lists 34 tool names but never shows:
  - what the JSON-RPC request shape is
  - that you pipe newline-delimited JSON into bin/veska-mcp on stdin
  - that the method name IS the tool name (no tools/call wrapper)
  - what parameter keys each tool wants

'bin/veska-mcp --help' is also silent: 'Veska MCP stdio shim (proxies editor to daemon socket)' and a -h flag. That's it.

Fix: README gains a 'Calling tools from the shell' subsection with a worked example, e.g.:

  printf '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"eng_find_symbol","params":{"repo_id":"…","branch":"main","symbol":"FooBar"}}\n' \
    | veska-mcp | jq .result

Pair with a one-paragraph note about the protocol shape (newline-delimited JSON-RPC, method=tool_name). Bonus: 'bin/veska-mcp --help' could echo this example.
acceptance_criteria: A user with bin/veska-mcp built and a running daemon can copy the README's snippet and get a real tool response back without reading any source code.
close_reason: Addressed in README rewrite + veska-mcp --help expansion. All five sections (Process topology, Requirements/Ollama install, Build, Quick start, Editor integration, Calling tools from the shell) reorganised so a newbie can copy-paste from clone to first MCP call.

title: README CLI list at 'Process topology' is incomplete (missing repo, reindex, service)
description:
Newbie journey, related to gaps #5 and consistency. The README's three-binary table describes the 'veska' CLI as:

  | veska | CLI — `init`, `status`, `doctor`, `backup`, `wiki`, … |

But running 'veska --help' shows: backup, completion, doctor, help, hook-runner, init, reindex, repo, restore, service, upgrade, wiki. The table misses three commands a newbie needs: repo (the primary lifecycle command), reindex (mentioned later in the README anyway), and service (the supported way to install the daemon as a real OS service — which the Quick Start currently fakes with '&' backgrounding).

Fix: update the topology table to either list all subcommands or use a footnote pointing at 'veska --help' as the source of truth. Also: README's Quick Start should mention 'veska service install' as the recommended alternative to '&', noting it survives reboots.
acceptance_criteria: The README's CLI summary either lists all subcommands accurately or directs users to 'veska --help'. Quick Start mentions 'veska service install' for production use.
close_reason: Addressed in README rewrite + veska-mcp --help expansion. All five sections (Process topology, Requirements/Ollama install, Build, Quick start, Editor integration, Calling tools from the shell) reorganised so a newbie can copy-paste from clone to first MCP call.

title: README missing Ollama install steps and disk-usage warning
description:
Newbie journey, gaps #2 + #3. The README says 'Ollama running locally with an embedding model' and links to ollama.com but provides no concrete install command. A fresh user has to:
  - figure out which install method (snap / brew / curl|sh / package manager)
  - know that 'nomic-embed-text' is ~274 MB on disk
  - know to start the Ollama server before veska init runs the probe

Fix: README 'Requirements' section gains a copy-pastable install + pull block, e.g.:

  # macOS:        brew install ollama && ollama serve &
  # Linux (snap): sudo snap install ollama && ollama serve &
  # Linux (curl): curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh && ollama serve &
  ollama pull nomic-embed-text   # ~274 MB

…then the existing Quick Start can assume Ollama is running. Also note that 'veska init' will fail the embedder probe if Ollama isn't reachable.
acceptance_criteria: A newbie following the README has a single block to copy-paste to get Ollama installed, served, and the model pulled. Disk usage is called out (~274 MB).
close_reason: Addressed in README rewrite + veska-mcp --help expansion. All five sections (Process topology, Requirements/Ollama install, Build, Quick start, Editor integration, Calling tools from the shell) reorganised so a newbie can copy-paste from clone to first MCP call.

title: eng_suppress_finding accepts non-existent finding_id, creating orphan suppressions
description:
Discovered during the systematic user-test journey through every MCP tool. eng_suppress_finding succeeds and inserts a row in suppressions even when the finding_id does not exist in findings.

Repro:
  printf '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"eng_suppress_finding","params":{"repo_id":"<id>","branch":"main","finding_id":"definitely-not-real","reason":"x"}}' | veska-mcp
  → result: {"suppression_id": "sup_…"}  — but no underlying finding existed

The orphan is then visible via eng_list_suppressions and survives across daemon restarts. There's no cleanup path that detects 'suppression points at missing finding'.

Fix: handler should validate that (finding_id, branch, repo_id) exists in findings before inserting the suppressions row. Return -32602 with 'finding not found' otherwise. Existing closed/unknown findings are already rejected upstream of this — only the 'never existed' case slips through.

Alternatively (or additionally): periodic GC sweep that closes suppressions whose target finding has been gone for > some TTL.
acceptance_criteria: eng_suppress_finding with an unknown finding_id returns -32602 'finding not found' and inserts no row. eng_list_suppressions does not show suppressions whose target finding never existed.
close_reason: eng_suppress_finding now validates (finding_id, branch, repo_id) exists in findings before INSERT when scope='finding'. Non-finding scopes pass through. Unit + harness tests cover both branches. 64/64 harness tests pass.

title: End-to-end reindex → embedder → VectorStorage test (backend-aware: sqlite-vec + usearch)
description:
Why: solov2-7ox covers the synchronous cold-scan → promoted graph path only. The downstream embedder → VectorStorage round-trip (the part where vectors actually land in whichever backend the operator configured via VESKA_VECTOR_BACKEND) has no integration coverage. A user running 'veska reindex' against a usearch-configured veska should see vectors in the usearch index; against a default install, in sqlite-vec.

Scope: end-to-end test that runs the cold-scan reparser → waits for the embedder worker to drain node_embedding_refs → asserts VectorStorage.Search returns the expected nodes. Parameterise over backend:
- sqlite-vec — runs by default
- usearch — runs only when libusearch_c.so is present and the hnsw_native build tag is set (use a build-tag-gated _integration_test.go file or t.Skip with a clear missing-lib message)

Requires Ollama at localhost:11434 with the default embed model — skip with a clear message if unreachable so the test stays opt-in for CI without an Ollama runner.

Out of scope: cold-scan correctness (covered by solov2-7ox); fuzzing the embedder worker (covered elsewhere).

Acceptance: a fixture repo's two .go files, after reindex + embedder drain, are returned by VectorStorage.Search for an obvious query. Test passes under both backends on a machine with libusearch_c.so + Ollama; sqlite-vec branch passes on a vanilla dev machine.
acceptance_criteria: On both backends (sqlite-vec default, usearch under hnsw_native + libusearch_c.so), reindex of a fixture repo results in VectorStorage.Search returning at least one expected node id from a representative query; backend selection follows VESKA_VECTOR_BACKEND; usearch branch t.Skip's with a clear message when the native lib or Ollama is unavailable.
close_reason: Implemented as TestColdScanE2E_ReindexProducesSearchableVectors with sqlite-vec + usearch subtests. Both green: sqlite-vec passes default build; usearch passes under -tags hnsw_native with libusearch_c.so on LD_LIBRARY_PATH, skips cleanly otherwise. Full pipeline wired: real migrations → tree-sitter → Ingester → Promoter (FTS+EmbedRef sinks) → cold-scan reparser → embedder worker → real Ollama (nomic-embed-text:latest) → VectorStorage → Search. Top-hit assertion (ComputeAlphaMetric symbol) confirms vectors are semantically retrievable.

title: Live-watch newly-registered repos (eng_add_repo)
description: After solov2-0z1.3 a freshly-registered repo is cold-scanned, but the running fsnotify multi-repo watcher is only seeded at Daemon.Start (wire.go runWatchLoop seed). repoRegistrar.AddRepo does not call d.watcher.Add(repoID, root), so post-startup file edits in a mid-run-added repo are not delivered to Ingester.Save until the daemon restarts. Fix: thread a watchAdder dep into repoRegistrar and invoke it after a successful repo.Add (before / alongside the cold scan dispatch).
close_reason: Fixed: repoRegistrar.watchAdd hook wired to MultiRepoWatcher.Add in newDaemon; invoked synchronously after successful repo.Add (before cold-scan dispatch). Watcher errors are log-only — registration is the more critical contract. Tests cover the happy path and the watchAdd-fails-but-cold-scan-still-runs path.

title: Reindex existing nodes so snippets backfill and embeddings recompute
description: Why: switching the projection leaves every pre-existing embedding stale (same content_hash, new embed text) and every pre-existing node row with a NULL snippet. Scope: provide a one-time reindex path that re-parses tracked repos (populating snippet) and re-enqueues their nodes for embedding so vectors recompute against the new projection. The embedder already re-embeds when embed text changes (its dedup keys on embed_text). Record the DB-size delta from storing snippets. Depends on the projection switch.
acceptance_criteria: After reindex, previously-promoted nodes have non-NULL snippets and pending node_embedding_refs rows; the embedder recomputes their vectors; the nodes-table size delta from snippet storage is measured and recorded on solov2-ok0.
close_reason: Superseded: the reindex needs a cold-scan/full-reparse mechanism that does not exist (Resyncer built but never wired — see M1 gap bead). Rolled into the cold-scan epic; once a force-reparse exists, the +snippet reindex is one thin call.

title: Switch the embed-text projection to EmbedVariantSnippet
description: Why: the faithful sweep (solov2-ok0) showed +snippet roughly doubles recall@10. Scope: EmbeddingRefsRepo.FetchPending SELECTs nodes.snippet and embedText passes domain.EmbedVariantSnippet with it. A NULL/empty snippet degrades gracefully — domain.EmbedText skips empty parts, yielding the baseline projection. Depends on snippet persistence.
acceptance_criteria: FetchPending produces embed text via EmbedVariantSnippet; a node with a snippet projects '<kind> <symbol_path> <file> <language> <snippet>'; a node with a NULL snippet projects exactly the former baseline string.
close_reason: DoD verified: FetchPending projects via EmbedVariantSnippet, NULL snippet degrades to baseline string; +1 test; build+layercheck clean

title: Persist the capped node body into nodes.snippet on promotion
description: Why: domain.Node.RawContent is captured by the tree-sitter parser but dropped at persistence. Scope: the GraphRepo SaveNode INSERT writes RawContent, capped to a bounded size (~2KB, matching the recallprojection harness cap), into the snippet column. A node with no RawContent writes NULL. No projection change here. Depends on the snippet column.
acceptance_criteria: After SaveNode, a node with RawContent has its body (capped at the size limit, rune-safe) stored in nodes.snippet; a node without RawContent stores NULL; round-trips via GetNode/LoadGraph are unaffected.
close_reason: DoD verified: SaveNode persists rune-safe-capped (2KB) RawContent into nodes.snippet, NULL when absent, read path unchanged; +3 tests; build+layercheck clean

title: Add a nodes.snippet column
description: Why: the +snippet embed-text projection (faithful sweep, solov2-ok0) needs a per-node source body to project; the nodes table stores only content_hash today. Scope: a new migration 0009_node_snippet.sql adding a nullable TEXT snippet column. No write path or projection change here. Design: solov2-ok0 notes.
acceptance_criteria: Migration 0009 adds a nullable 'snippet' TEXT column to nodes; a freshly migrated DB has the column; existing rows read NULL.
close_reason: DoD verified: migration 0009 adds nullable nodes.snippet column, auto-applied via embed glob; +2 tests; build clean

title: Run the faithful +snippet sweep on three real repos and decide
description: Why: produce the faithful measurement solov2-7ma deferred. Run the real-corpus sweep against veska, golang.org/x/mod, and github.com/BurntSushi/toml. Compare +snippet vs baseline recall@10 and p95 per repo. Decide: promote the +snippet projection (which makes solov2-ok0's body-persistence work worthwhile) or document that baseline holds under a faithful measurement.
acceptance_criteria: recall@10 + p95 recorded for baseline and +snippet across all three repos; a written decision (promote vs keep baseline) is recorded against solov2-ok0.
close_reason: Faithful 3-repo sweep recorded (veska/x-mod/toml): +snippet roughly doubles recall@10 over baseline at flat p95 on every repo. Decision recorded on solov2-ok0: promote +snippet.

title: Add a real-corpus mode to the recallprojection sweep
description: Why: the projection sweep currently only consumes the synthetic corpus. Wire the realcorpus builder in behind an env knob (e.g. RECALL_PROJECTION_CORPUS=real:<path>) so the existing 4-variant baseline/+signature/+snippet/+both sweep runs against real source. No new metric logic — reuse the recall@10 + p95 machinery.
acceptance_criteria: The recallprojection sweep can be pointed at a real Go module path and runs the 4-variant sweep over real-source nodes, emitting the same recall@10 + p95 report rows.
close_reason: DoD verified: RECALL_PROJECTION_CORPUS=real:<path> routes the 4-variant sweep through BuildRealCorpus; vet+build clean under -tags=eval

title: Build a real-code recall corpus from go/parser + go/doc
description: Why: the recallprojection sweep's +snippet input is synthSnippet — a restatement of the query text — making the +snippet recall figure circular (solov2-7ma). A faithful measurement needs real source bodies and queries independent of them. Scope: a corpus builder only; no sweep wiring. Build a realcorpus package that walks a Go module with go/parser+go/doc and emits a synthcorpus.Corpus-shaped value: each exported documented top-level symbol is a node carrying real Signature (declaration) + real Snippet (source body); the query is the symbol's doc comment; ground truth is that one node. Undocumented symbols stay as distractors.
acceptance_criteria: Given a Go package directory, the builder returns a corpus where each documented exported symbol is a node with its real source body and declaration, a doc-comment query, and single-node ground truth; an undocumented symbol is included as a non-queried distractor.
close_reason: DoD verified: BuildRealCorpus parses a Go module via parser.ParseFile + doc.NewFromFiles(PreserveAST), emits documented symbols as queried clusters with real body/signature and undocumented ones as distractors; +4 tests; build+vet clean

title: Persist node body and re-measure +snippet embed-text projection
description: Follow-up from solov2-7ma. The recall-projection sweep (pop=1000, commit-era 2026-05-19) showed the +snippet embed-text variant at recall@10 1.000 vs baseline 0.917, p95 +2.5ms. But two caveats block promotion: (1) the harness's snippet is synthSnippet — a synthetic restatement of the corpus query text — so the 1.000 figure is optimistic/partly circular; (2) production cannot project a snippet at all: the nodes table stores content_hash + signature, not the node body (domain.Node.RawContent is never persisted). +signature, whose data IS stored, measured no lift (0.913) and was rejected. To pursue +snippet: persist a node body/snippet (schema migration + parser capture + promotion wiring + storage-cost assessment), feed it into a faithful recallprojection corpus (real source snippets, not synthSnippet), re-run the sweep, and promote only if the lift holds with no p95 regression.
acceptance_criteria: A node body/snippet is persisted and projected via domain.EmbedVariantSnippet; a recallprojection sweep built from real source snippets shows a recall@10 improvement over baseline with no p95 regression, or the variant is documented as not worthwhile.
notes:
FAITHFUL RE-MEASUREMENT COMPLETE (solov2-ok0.1-.3, 2026-05-19). Real-code recall-projection sweep over 3 repos — real source-body snippets, doc-comment queries (no synthSnippet circularity):
  repo   nodes  baseline  +signature  +snippet  +both
  veska   702    0.245     0.323       0.495     0.481
  x/mod   214    0.257     0.466       0.635     0.608
  toml     48    0.500     0.658       0.816     0.789
+snippet roughly doubles recall@10 over baseline on every repo, p95 flat. +both is consistently below +snippet alone. DECISION: promote +snippet — proceed with the body-persistence + projection work. Use EmbedVariantSnippet (not +both).
close_reason: +snippet rollout complete: faithful 3-repo sweep (.1-.3) decided promote; schema (.4), capped-body persistence (.5), and EmbedVariantSnippet projection (.6) shipped — +snippet is live and self-heals per commit. .7 reindex superseded into the cold-scan epic.

title: Clear the pre-existing make lint baseline (14 staticcheck/unused issues)
description: make all fails at the lint step on 14 pre-existing staticcheck/unused issues across 9 files NOT touched by the M7 epic: internal/application/ingester.go, internal/application/wiki/render.go, internal/infrastructure/embedding/ollama/ollama.go, internal/infrastructure/mcp/registry.go + registry_test.go + tools_admin_test.go, internal/infrastructure/sqlite/lexical_repo_test.go + revalidate_repo_test.go, internal/observability/spans.go. Mix of SA5011 nil-deref hints, SA9003 empty branch, SA1019 deprecated trace.NewNoopTracerProvider, and one unused func. Surfaced during M7 (epic solov2-2ps) when 'make all' was run as an exit gate.
acceptance_criteria: 'make lint' (and therefore 'make all') exits 0 with no staticcheck or unused findings.
close_reason: make lint + make all now exit 0: trace.NewNoopTracerProvider→noop, WriteString(Sprintf)→Fprintf, errors.As collapse, SA5011 false-positives silenced with explicit return-after-Fatal, empty branch + unused func removed

title: OSV Scan: match deps at pseudo-versions and +incompatible
description: Follow-up to solov2-2ps.3. The OSV adapter's Scan() treats dependency versions that fail semver.IsValid (Go pseudo-versions like v0.0.0-2024..., and +incompatible versions) as not-affected, producing false negatives. Reconcile these against OSV affected ranges. Not a DoD blocker for M7 — tidied modules on tagged releases match correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Scan() correctly matches a dependency pinned at a pseudo-version or a +incompatible version against an OSV advisory whose affected range covers it.
close_reason: Premise incorrect: golang.org/x/mod/semver accepts Go pseudo-versions and +incompatible (both valid semver), and semver.Compare orders them correctly — versionAffected already matches them. Verified empirically; added regression test TestScan_PseudoVersionAndIncompatible. No code change needed.

title: Review JSON parser rejects a finding object missing the message field
description: Surfaced by the solov2-zrf reference-laptop run (2026-05-18): 1 of 100 files failed with 'review.Handle: parse response for contract_drift: malformed model response: finding 1: missing message'. The euk JSON parser treats a per-finding 'message' as required and returns ErrMalformedResponse when llama3 omits it. ~1% real-model failure rate. Even with Ollama structured output, the model occasionally returns a finding object lacking message. Fix options: (a) make message optional in the parser — default to the finding Title when absent; (b) tighten the JSON schema so Ollama enforces message as required (verify whether the schema marks it required + how Ollama handles it). Low severity — nz2.3's failure contract catches a persistently-malformed review job (retry x3 -> review-pipeline-failure finding), so nothing is silently lost — but a 1% spurious failure is worth removing. Not an M5 blocker.
close_reason: DoD verified: review parser defaults a finding's message to its title when absent (no longer fails the job); test added, build/race/vet green

title: Extract domain.DeriveFindingID as the single source for finding_id derivation
description: Why: the finding_id derivation hex(sha256(rule+\x00+anchor+\x00+key))[:32] exists in TWO places — internal/core/domain/finding.go (NewFinding) and internal/application/review/failure.go (FailureFindingID, used by doctor.ReviewFailureFindingID for the post-promotion-queue exit-2 invariant). They must stay byte-identical or doctor silently mis-correlates failed review rows to their findings. solov2-c0t just proved the hazard: the mirror had to be hand-patched to add the trailing key segment. Extract a single exported domain.DeriveFindingID and route both call sites through it. Surfaced by solov2-c0t. Trivial refactor, no behavior change.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: domain exposes an exported DeriveFindingID(rule, anchor, key string) string. AC2: domain.NewFinding computes FindingID via DeriveFindingID (no inline hash). AC3: review.FailureFindingID delegates to domain.DeriveFindingID — no hand-rolled sha256. AC4: no other hand-rolled finding_id hash remains; go build + test -race + vet + layercheck green.
close_reason: DoD verified: domain.DeriveFindingID extracted; NewFinding + review.FailureFindingID both route through it, no hand-rolled hash remains; build/race/vet/layercheck green

title: Document the per-commit review time budget (100-file commit, reference laptop)
description: M5 exit gate 5 / m5.00.3, not covered by the solov2-nz2 epic. Run one review pass over a 100-file commit on the reference laptop with the local Ollama generator and record the wall-clock time budget in the M5 close report (and docs/milestones/M5.md). Required before M5 milestone close.

title: Surface last-render time in veska doctor
description:
Why: operators need to see whether the rendered wiki is stale. M4.md epic m4.04.3.

DoD: doctor check test; go test + go vet green. Design ref: docs/milestones/M4.md, epic solov2-erp.

title: Add the veska wiki on-demand command
description:
Why: operators need to regenerate the wiki pages without waiting for a promotion. M4.md epic m4.04.1.

Scope boundary: the 'veska wiki' CLI command only. It reuses the render orchestration created by the WorkKindWiki lane task (wiki.Handler) — it does not reimplement rendering.

DoD: CLI command test; go test + go vet green. Design ref: docs/milestones/M4.md, epic solov2-erp.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
AC1: 'veska wiki' regenerates both pages and exits 0 on success. AC2: the command writes the same byte-identical output the promotion lane produces.

CLARIFICATIONS (2026-05-16, approved by human):
- Repo/branch selection: --repo and --branch flags. --repo is optional and defaults to the sole registered repo when exactly one exists (error if ambiguous / none). --branch defaults to that repo's default branch.
- Implementation: open veska.db via config.DefaultVectorDir()+veska.db, wire the wiki services (HotZoneService, EntryPointsService, RenderTimeStore via sqlite.WikiRenderStateRepo, RepoRootFunc), construct a wiki.Handler and call Handle with a synthetic ports.WorkRow{Kind: WorkKindWiki, RepoID, Branch}. Reusing Handle guarantees AC2 byte-identical output for free.
- Register as a root cobra command in cmd/veska/main.go (newRootCmd).
acceptance_criteria: AC1: 'veska wiki' regenerates both pages and exits 0 on success. AC2: the command writes the same byte-identical output the promotion lane produces.
close_reason: DoD verified: veska wiki command regenerates both pages via reused wiki.Handler (byte-identical), --repo/--branch flags; CLI test + layercheck green

title: Edge-anchored revalidation path for auto-link findings
description:
Deferred from solov2-l8y investigation.

FINDING: auto-link findings are never revalidated. They store an edge_id in the findings.node_id column (autolink/handler.go). The m3.05 revalidation sweep's StaleFindingsForFile joins findings.node_id -> nodes.node_id; an edge_id never matches a nodes row, so auto-link findings are never selected. They stay open until a developer accepts/suppresses them.

This is acceptable as a default (auto-link is a suggestion; a stale suggestion is low-harm; m3.04 designed it as developer-driven accept/suppress). This bead tracks the OPTIONAL feature of revalidating them automatically.

If pursued:
1. A sweep path that selects edge-anchored findings (join via edges by edge_id, or store both endpoint node ids on the finding).
2. A cheap re-check: do both endpoint nodes still exist? did either drift materially? Decide refresh vs close.
3. Wire it into the revalidate Handler as a distinct dispatch (not a per-rule case in decide(), which is node-anchored).

NOT a blocker — auto-link lifecycle works today via developer action. Only worth doing if stale auto-link findings prove noisy in practice.
notes: Deferred 2026-05-18: optional by design — implement only if stale auto-link findings prove noisy in practice. No evidence of noise; auto-link lifecycle works via developer accept/suppress today.

title: Measure + decide signature/snippet enrichment of embedder text projection
description:
Split from solov2-311. The collapse-correctness fix (file_path + language added to the FetchPending embed-text projection) landed under 311. This bead covers the measurement-gated remainder: whether to ALSO fold signature and/or a code snippet into the embed text to make embeddings semantically richer.

That changes embedding QUALITY, so it needs a recall measurement first (per the original 311 instruction). Blocker: the recall harness (tools/loadtest/recall) embeds synthcorpus text directly — it does NOT exercise the production FetchPending projection, so there is no current way to measure a projection change against the m3.03 fixture.

Scope:
1. Add a harness mode (or a new eval harness) that builds the corpus from real node-shaped projections so projection changes ARE measurable.
2. Sweep projection variants (with/without signature, with/without snippet) and record recall@10 + p95.
3. Decide and implement the winning projection, or document that <kind> <symbol_path> <file> <language> is sufficient.

Files: internal/infrastructure/sqlite/embedding_refs_repo.go (FetchPending), tools/loadtest/recall/.
notes: Step 1 (measurable harness) DONE 2026-05-18, commit d672784: domain.EmbedText shared projection + tools/loadtest/recallprojection harness with a baseline/+signature/+snippet/+both variant selector + 'make eval-recall-projection'. Remaining (deferred, needs a reference-laptop run): run the pop=1000 sweep against a real model, compare mean_recall per variant vs the m3.03 baseline, and promote the winning projection into embedding_refs_repo.go embedText if recall improves with no p95 regression.
close_reason: Sweep done (pop=1000, make eval-recall-projection): baseline recall@10 0.917 p95 69ms; +signature 0.913 (no lift) REJECTED; +snippet 1.000 but harness snippet is a synthetic restatement of query text (optimistic) and production stores no node body to project. Decision: keep baseline projection. Snippet-storage feature filed as solov2-ok0.

title: Wire MultiRepoWatcher.Add to repo registry (currently empty)
description: cmd/veska-daemon/wire.go starts MultiRepoWatcher but never calls Add(repoID, rootPath). The intended source is the repo registry (internal/repo/registry.go) — implement a ListRepos query that returns RepoRecord, and on daemon Start iterate it to add each registered repo to the watcher. Today the watcher is wired but inert.
close_reason: repo.List(ctx,db) ([]repo.Record,error) added; daemon Start now seeds MultiRepoWatcher from the repos table (List failure logged, non-fatal). Added MultiRepoWatcher.WatchedRepoIDs() accessor. Commit d017e28. internal/repo kept free of internal/application import.

title: Migrate embed queue lane to queue.Poller (remove noopEmbedHandler)
description: cmd/veska-daemon/wire.go currently installs a noopEmbedHandler under ports.WorkKindEmbed in the queue.Poller because the embedder.Worker drives its own poll loop directly on node_embedding_refs. Long-term: migrate the embedder to a queue.WorkHandler so the post-promotion queue is the single dispatch path. Until then, the noop handler exists to keep the lane drained if another code path enqueues an embed row.
notes:
DEFERRED 2026-05-15 (session decision). Not a fix — embedder works correctly today; this is architectural unification only.

WHY DEFERRED:
- embedder.Worker is gate-1-validated (8.43 emb/s measured, commit f91b51e). Migrating its run model risks regressing that number.
- Genuine impedance mismatch: queue.WorkHandler processes ONE row at a time. embedder.Worker depends on three tick-scoped behaviours that do not map onto a one-row handler:
  1. cross-tick dedup — inFlight map (intra-tick) + refs.LookupExisting (prior-tick) skip redundant Embed calls for repeated (modelID, embed_text) keys.
  2. token-bucket rate limiting — limiter.Wait() once per row, but tuned around the tick loop.
  3. batched vector upserts — vecBatches map keyed by (repo,branch), flushed per tick, not per row.

BLOCKER FOR A CLEAN FIX:
A faithful migration needs a BATCH-AWARE WorkHandler contract (e.g. ports.WorkHandler gains a HandleBatch([]Row) variant, or queue.Poller hands each lane a slice). Migrating embed onto the current one-row WorkHandler would force dropping dedup+batching or reimplementing them inside the handler — net negative. So this bead really has a hidden prerequisite: decide the batch-handler port shape first.

CURRENT STATE (acceptable interim):
- cmd/veska-daemon/wire.go installs noopEmbedHandler under ports.WorkKindEmbed — a ~5-line handler that marks the row done. Legitimate stopgap: keeps the lane drained if anything enqueues an embed row.
- Real embedding work happens via embedder.Worker's own loop on node_embedding_refs (Start/Stop lifecycle wired in the daemon).
- gate-5 fuzz passes: embed lane 'drains' via the noop handler.

WHEN PICKED UP:
1. First decide the batch WorkHandler contract (new sub-bead or design note) — this is the actual gate.
2. Then: embedder.Worker becomes a queue.WorkHandler (batch variant); remove noopEmbedHandler from wire.go; drop the standalone Start/Stop wiring.
3. Re-run gate-1 throughput bench (make eval-embed-throughput) — must still land 5-20 emb/s.
4. Re-run gate-5 queue fuzz — embed lane now does real work.

Files: cmd/veska-daemon/wire.go (noopEmbedHandler), internal/application/embedder/embedder.go (run/tick loop), internal/infrastructure/sqlite/queue/queue.go (WorkHandler contract), internal/core/ports/queue.go (WorkHandler interface).

title: Replace embedder.NewWorker / autolink.NewHandler / NewLinker panics with typed errors
description:
cmd/veska-daemon wiring (solov2-3lv) inherits panics from constructors that panic on nil collaborators: embedder.NewWorker, autolink.NewHandler, autolink.NewLinker (transitively), ollama.New on empty model. The composition root in cmd/veska-daemon/wire.go already validates the inputs it controls and returns *ErrMissingDep, but the underlying constructors still rely on panics for defense-in-depth.

Convert these to typed errors so a caller passing a nil dep gets a descriptive error rather than a stack trace. Scope: low-risk refactor; all call sites are within this repo (count is small).
close_reason: All 4 constructors (embedder.NewWorker, autolink.NewLinker, autolink.NewHandler, ollama.New) now return (*T, error); nil/empty dep wraps a package-level ErrMissingDependency sentinel (errors.Is-matchable). wire.go propagates with pools.Close cleanup preserved. ~75 test call sites updated via mustNewX helpers. Commit 9003ef3. (Plus 14cc0a5: fixed 2 fixturegen tests regressed by the earlier d5z normalization commit.)

title: application.RepoLister + mcp.StatusProvider/ConfigProvider adapters for RegisterAdminTools
description: cmd/veska-daemon wiring (solov2-3lv) skipped mcp.RegisterAdminTools because there is no production implementation of application.RepoLister, mcp.StatusProvider, or mcp.ConfigProvider. Implement these on top of internal/repo registry (which already has Add but not List) + a small Status/Config struct exposed from the daemon, then wire RegisterAdminTools in cmd/veska-daemon/wire.go.
close_reason: 3 production providers (repoLister, statusProvider, configProvider) added in cmd/veska-daemon/providers.go; RegisterAdminTools now wired in registerMCPTools — all 5 admin MCP tools resolve. repoLister maps repo.Record->application.RepoRecord; statusProvider reports schema_version/repo_count/pending_embeds; configProvider reflects daemon Config. Commit f3e5d27.

cmd/veska-daemon wiring (solov2-3lv) had to skip RegisterGraphTools, RegisterBlastTools, RegisterSearchTools because no production adapter for ports.GraphStorage exists in internal/infrastructure/sqlite. The MCP server starts without those tools today. Implement a sqlite.GraphRepo satisfying ports.GraphStorage (SaveNode, SaveEdge, DeleteFile, LoadGraph, FindNodes, GetNode), then wire RegisterGraphTools + RegisterBlastTools + RegisterSearchTools in cmd/veska-daemon/wire.go.

Surface: solov2-3lv composition root TODO.

Commit e8a7f70.

title: Rewrite or scope parent CLAUDE.md to match veska (was engram)
description: /home/jrose/src/engram/CLAUDE.md still describes engram (engram init, ENGRAM_HOME, Dolt+Qdrant runtime deps). The repo renamed to veska; commands are veska init / VESKA_HOME; runtime is SQLite + usearch/sqlitevec with no Dolt/Qdrant. Either rewrite to match veska's architecture, or scope it to the parent V1 directory only (and remove from solov2 paths). The solov2/CLAUDE.md is already a thin template — replace it with a real veska guide pulled from this file.
